Lokayan 26 is designed to showcase India’s rich maritime heritage while fostering goodwill, mutual understanding, and public engagement across the Indian Ocean region, reinforcing India’s role as a responsible maritime partner.

questions.question: In which state did SJVN commission the 70 MW Dhubri Solar Power Project to strengthen renewable energy generation?
questions.explanation: The 70 MW Dhubri Solar Power Project was commissioned in Assam by SJVN through its subsidiary, SJVN Green Energy Limited. Developed at a cost of ₹367.44 crore over 330 acres, the project marks a significant step in expanding the state’s renewable energy capacity. It is expected to generate 141.13 million units of electricity in its first year and about 3,230 million units over a 25-year period. The project will supply affordable solar power at ₹3.92 per unit to the Assam Power Distribution Company, supporting clean and sustainable energy goals.

questions.question: Which state approved the use of paper ballots instead of EVMs for upcoming Panchayat and civic body elections to strengthen public trust in the electoral process?
questions.explanation: Karnataka decided to reintroduce paper ballots for both rural and urban local body elections, including Zilla and Taluk Panchayats as well as Greater Bengaluru civic polls. The decision, taken by the state cabinet led by Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, aims to enhance transparency and rebuild public confidence in the electoral system. This change will apply to all forthcoming local body elections in the state and will replace the use of electronic voting machines. To facilitate the shift, amendments to the Karnataka Conduct of Election Rules are to be introduced within a short timeframe.

questions.question: Which institution partnered with BEML and the Foundation for Science, Innovation and Development to sign a tripartite agreement for next-generation engineering research?
questions.explanation: IISc Bengaluru entered into a tripartite agreement with BEML and the Foundation for Science, Innovation and Development to promote advanced collaborative research in critical sectors such as aerospace, defence, and advanced manufacturing. The partnership is designed to accelerate blue-sky, applied, and translational research, with a strong emphasis on indigenisation and national self-reliance. By enabling shared access to research infrastructure and expertise, the collaboration aims to develop high-impact technologies that support India’s strategic and industrial capabilities while strengthening the country’s innovation ecosystem.

questions.question: In which city did PLASTINDIA 2026, one of the world’s largest plastics exhibitions, take place from February 5–10?
questions.explanation: PLASTINDIA 2026 commenced in New Delhi as a major global event in the plastics sector, supported by multiple Indian ministries. The exhibition brought together over 2,000 exhibitors from around the world, showcasing cutting-edge innovations in plastics machinery, advanced materials, and circular economy solutions. With the theme “Bharat Next,” the event emphasised India’s future-ready industrial vision. Notably, it was India’s first Zero Waste plastics expo, featuring dedicated spaces for youth, startups, and a museum highlighting the positive role of plastics across key economic and social sectors.

questions.question: Which bank became the first in India to issue a fully digital Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI) licence using an end-to-end online process with e-signatures?
questions.explanation: Kotak Mahindra Bank achieved a significant milestone by becoming the first institution in India to issue a fully digital FPI licence. The entire process, from application to approval, was completed online using electronic signatures, without any physical paperwork. This was enabled by SEBI’s unified digital workflow, which allows seamless coordination among custodians and market intermediaries. The initiative simplifies onboarding for foreign investors, reduces time and compliance costs, and enhances transparency. By adopting global best practices in digital finance, this step strengthens India’s capital market infrastructure and supports the country’s broader push for ease of doing business and Digital India.

questions.question: Who is the Indian mountaineer from Arunachal Pradesh who successfully summited Mount Aconcagua, the highest peak in the Western Hemisphere?
questions.explanation: Kabak Yano, a 27-year-old mountaineer from Arunachal Pradesh, achieved a major milestone by successfully summiting Mount Aconcagua in Argentina. Standing at 22,831 feet, Aconcagua is the highest peak in South America and the Western Hemisphere, known for its extreme altitude, harsh winds, and freezing conditions. The ascent forms part of her ambitious Seven Summits expedition, which aims to climb the highest peak on each continent. Her accomplishment highlights exceptional endurance, mental strength, and technical skill, while also bringing national attention to Arunachal Pradesh’s growing presence in global adventure sports.

questions.question: Which institution partnered with Indian Highways Management Company Limited to strengthen cyber security and digital forensics in national highway operations?
questions.explanation: Indian Highways Management Company Limited entered into a strategic partnership with the National Forensic Sciences University to enhance cybersecurity and data protection across digital highway operations. The collaboration focuses on areas such as cyber forensics, digital evidence integrity, research, training, and consultancy. With highways increasingly dependent on technologies like Multi-Lane Free Flow tolling and advanced traffic management systems, safeguarding sensitive data has become critical. NFSU’s expertise in cyber investigation and forensic sciences will help strengthen resilience against cyber threats, ensure secure digital operations, and build institutional capacity for managing cyber incidents in national highway infrastructure.

questions.question: Which state government approved a ₹50 crore heritage grant to preserve and restore the legacy of the Darrang kings of the Koch dynasty?
questions.explanation: The government of Assam approved a ₹50 crore grant to preserve the heritage of the Darrang kings of the Koch dynasty, reflecting a strong commitment to safeguarding indigenous history and culture. The initiative focuses on restoring royal sites, monuments, and culturally significant landmarks associated with the Koch rulers. Announced during Mahabir Chilarai Divas, the project also aims to honour the legacy of Mahabir Chilarai, one of Assam’s greatest military leaders. Beyond conservation, the effort seeks to develop the region into a major cultural tourism hub, linking historical preservation with economic and community development.

questions.question: From which state was the Agni-III intermediate-range ballistic missile successfully test-fired to validate India’s strategic operational readiness?
questions.explanation: The Agni-III missile was successfully test-fired from the Integrated Test Range at Chandipur, located along the coast of Odisha. Conducted under the supervision of the Strategic Forces Command, the launch was part of a routine training exercise aimed at validating operational readiness rather than developing new capabilities. The test confirmed the performance of key subsystems such as propulsion, guidance, and control. Such launches play a vital role in maintaining India’s credible minimum deterrence posture by ensuring that deployed missile systems remain reliable, responsive, and fully prepared for real-world operational conditions.

questions.question: Which leader was elected by the ruling BJP legislature party to become the next Chief Minister of Manipur as President’s Rule neared its end?
questions.explanation: Yumnam Khemchand Singh was elected as the leader of the BJP legislature party in Manipur, paving the way for the formation of a new state government. His selection comes at a critical time as President’s Rule, imposed after prolonged ethnic violence and political instability, is set to end. A senior BJP leader and two-time MLA from Singjamei, Singh has served as Speaker of the Manipur Assembly and as a cabinet minister. Seen as an experienced administrator with strong organisational backing, his elevation signals a political reset and an effort to restore stability, governance, and public confidence in the violence-affected state.

questions.question: Which state is set to launch the Amaravati Quantum Valley project to emerge as a global hub for quantum research, innovation, and industry collaboration?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh is preparing to launch the ambitious Amaravati Quantum Valley project with the objective of positioning the state as a global centre for quantum research and innovation. The initiative focuses on key areas such as quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum-safe technologies, supported by collaborations with industry leaders like IBM and Tata Consultancy Services and prominent academic institutions. By creating specialised infrastructure, encouraging public–private partnerships, and nurturing skilled talent, the project aligns with India’s broader push toward emerging technologies and aims to strengthen national capabilities in strategic and future-oriented domains.

questions.question: In which city was Operation Shastra launched as a large-scale police crackdown to curb fear-mongering and intimidation through social media?
questions.explanation: Operation Shastra was launched in New Delhi as a city-wide initiative by the Delhi Police to address the misuse of social media for intimidation, fear creation, and criminal signalling. The operation targeted individuals who posted images with weapons, used abusive language, or projected criminal dominance online. With the deployment of over 500 police teams and more than 2,000 personnel, thousands of social media profiles were scrutinised, leading to multiple FIRs and arrests. The initiative reflects a preventive policing approach that integrates cyber monitoring with conventional law enforcement to stop potential crimes before they escalate into real-world violence.

questions.question: Which port in southern India became the first in the country to deploy an advanced anti-drone system to strengthen coastal and infrastructure security?
questions.explanation: VOC Port, officially known as V.O. Chidambaranar Port at Thoothukudi in Tamil Nadu, created history by becoming India’s first port to deploy an advanced anti-drone system. The system integrates radar and radio frequency technologies to provide 360-degree surveillance and real-time detection, tracking, and neutralisation of unauthorised drones. This initiative enhances the safety of port assets, personnel, and cargo while addressing emerging aerial threats. The deployment aligns with Maritime India Vision 2030 and Amrit Kaal Vision 2047, positioning the port as a benchmark for technology-driven maritime security in India.

questions.question: In which state will the 13th India–Kyrgyzstan Joint Military Exercise KHANJAR-XIII be conducted in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The 13th edition of the India–Kyrgyzstan Joint Military Exercise KHANJAR-XIII will be held at Misamari in the state of Assam from February 4 to 17, 2026. Misamari is a key military training location in Northeast India, offering diverse terrain suitable for advanced combat training. The exercise will involve elite Special Forces from both countries, focusing on counter-terrorism, urban warfare, and special operations under United Nations mandates. Hosting the exercise in Assam highlights India’s strategic emphasis on its eastern region and strengthens defence cooperation with Kyrgyzstan.

questions.question: Which state emerged as the largest wool producer in India in 2026, contributing nearly half of the country’s total wool output?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan ranked first among India’s wool-producing states in 2026, producing about 16,013.50 thousand kilograms of wool, which accounts for nearly 47.53 per cent of the nation’s total output. The state’s arid climate and vast grasslands provide ideal conditions for large-scale sheep rearing. Indigenous breeds such as Marwari and Chokla are well suited to these conditions and mainly yield coarse wool used in carpets, rugs, and blankets. Due to its scale of production and regional specialization, Rajasthan forms the backbone of India’s wool industry and supports rural livelihoods across the state.

questions.question: Who has been approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet for appointment as Director in the Department of Defence under the Central Staffing Scheme?
questions.explanation: M Anitha, an officer of the Indian Revenue Service (Income Tax) from the 2007 batch, has been approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet for appointment as Director in the Department of Defence. Her posting is under the Central Staffing Scheme, which enables experienced civil servants to serve in key positions across central ministries. She will join the Department of Defence on a lateral shift from the Department of Investment and Public Asset Management, where she was serving as Director. The appointment reflects the government’s approach of leveraging administrative expertise for strategic and policy-oriented roles.

questions.question: Which state’s women’s team won the gold medal at the National Federation Cup 2026 Para Throwball tournament?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand emerged as the gold medal winner in the women’s category of the National Federation Cup 2026 Para Throwball tournament. The team secured victory by defeating the host state Rajasthan with a score of 21–17 in the final held at Jhunjhunu. This achievement highlights Jharkhand’s growing presence and performance in para sports at the national level. The tournament, conducted from February 3 to 5, also acted as a selection trial for the upcoming 2nd Asian Para Throwball Championship in Malaysia, making the win particularly significant for future international representation.

questions.question: Who led the Liberal Democratic Party to a historic landslide victory in Japan’s 2026 general election?
questions.explanation: Sanae Takaichi led the Liberal Democratic Party to a historic landslide victory in Japan’s 2026 general election, securing a two-thirds supermajority in the lower house of parliament. This decisive mandate came just months after she assumed office as Japan’s first female prime minister. The scale of the win strengthened her authority to pursue major reforms in the economy, defence, and national security. The outcome also reflected weak and fragmented opposition, high public support, and confidence in her leadership, marking a significant shift in Japan’s political landscape with global strategic implications.

questions.question: Which country rolled out its first locally produced foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) vaccine in two decades to strengthen animal health and self-sufficiency?
questions.explanation: South Africa rolled out its first locally produced foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) vaccine in 20 years as part of a strategic effort to enhance self-sufficiency in animal healthcare. Developed by the Agricultural Research Council (ARC), the initiative aims to scale vaccine production to 20,000 doses per week. This move supports efforts to regain FMD-free status, which is critical for protecting cloven-hoofed livestock and maintaining access to global export markets. Beyond disease control, the science-driven approach helps stabilize rural economies by safeguarding livelihoods dependent on livestock farming.

questions.question: On which date will International Epilepsy Day 2026 be observed globally?
questions.explanation: February 9 is observed as International Epilepsy Day 2026 to raise global awareness about epilepsy, a neurological condition affecting over 50 million people worldwide. Led by the International Bureau for Epilepsy (IBE) and the International League Against Epilepsy (ILAE), the day focuses on breaking myths and social stigma while promoting inclusion, empathy, and access to healthcare. The observance highlights education, community participation, and policy advocacy, aligning with global health goals. In 2026, special emphasis is placed on turning awareness into action through initiatives like the Epilepsy Pledge, encouraging meaningful and measurable societal change.

questions.question: Which organisation is leading the development of an improved Kyasanur Forest Disease (KFD) vaccine and has initiated Phase I human clinical trials?
questions.explanation: ICMR is the organisation spearheading the development of an improved Kyasanur Forest Disease vaccine in India. As the country’s apex body for biomedical research, it has taken responsibility for advancing an indigenous solution to protect vulnerable populations in the Western Ghats region. The vaccine has successfully completed animal studies and toxicity testing and has now entered Phase I human clinical trials after regulatory approval. Through collaboration with scientific and manufacturing partners, ICMR’s effort highlights India’s growing capability in vaccine research, regional disease control, and strengthening public health security.

questions.question: In which city did Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw inaugurate Zetwerk's mega electronics manufacturing plant?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is the city where Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw inaugurated Zetwerk’s mega electronics plant. The facility is designed to support manufacturing for defence, automotive, and IT hardware sectors, strengthening India’s electronics ecosystem. This inauguration aligns with the government’s expanded manufacturing incentive scheme, which has been increased to ₹40,000 crore with an ambitious target of achieving $500 billion in electronics production by 2031 under the Silicon 2.0 initiative. The development also complements India’s long-term vision of building advanced semiconductor capabilities and supporting the journey toward a Viksit Bharat by 2047.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as Chair of the Global Value Chains Committee of the US–India Strategic Partnership Forum (USISPF)?
questions.explanation: Rohit Kumar Singh has been appointed as the Chair of the Global Value Chains Committee of the US–India Strategic Partnership Forum, marking an important step in strengthening India–US economic cooperation. A former Consumer Affairs Secretary and a senior IAS officer, he brings extensive experience in economic governance, regulatory reform, and technology-driven public administration. In his new role, he is expected to promote resilient and diversified global supply chains, enhance regulatory alignment, and support public-private partnerships. His appointment reflects growing trust in Indian policy expertise at a time of global trade realignment and supply chain restructuring.

questions.question: Which country is India finalising a €300M deal with for SCALP missiles following their use in Operation Sindoor (2025)?
questions.explanation: France is the country with which India is finalizing a €300M deal for SCALP missiles. The agreement comes after the successful and precise deployment of these missiles during Operation Sindoor in 2025, reinforcing confidence in their effectiveness. This deal reflects the strong and long-standing defence partnership between India and France, which extends beyond arms procurement to include joint exercises, technology transfer, and strategic cooperation. The collaboration aligns with India’s broader goals of modernising its armed forces while strengthening ties with reliable international partners in defence, technology, and manufacturing sectors.

questions.question: Which country partnered with India to launch a Hydrogen Fellowship and an IIT–Groningen collaboration for green energy research?
questions.explanation: The Netherlands partnered with India to launch a Hydrogen Fellowship and an academic collaboration between IITs and the University of Groningen. These initiatives are aimed at advancing research and innovation in green hydrogen technologies, with a strong emphasis on capacity building and integrated energy systems. The partnership supports India’s National Green Hydrogen Mission by facilitating knowledge exchange, research collaboration, and technological development. It also aligns with India’s long-term climate commitments, particularly the goal of achieving Net-Zero carbon emissions by 2070, while strengthening bilateral cooperation in clean and sustainable energy solutions.

questions.question: From which state was India’s nationwide pulses revolution launched under the Self-Reliance in Pulses Mission?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh is the state from which India launched its nationwide pulses revolution, marking a major shift in agricultural policy. The launch took place at Amlaha in the Sehore district under the leadership of Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan. The initiative aims to end India’s dependence on imported pulses and transform the country into a global exporter. With a strong focus on farmer participation, seed-to-market integration, cluster-based farming, and local processing through pulse mills, the mission positions Madhya Pradesh as a central hub in strengthening food security, nutrition, rural employment, and farmer incomes.

questions.question: Which country agreed with India to develop a joint work plan on security, law enforcement, and cybersecurity policy?
questions.explanation: Canada agreed with India to develop a joint work plan aimed at enhancing cooperation in security, law enforcement, and cybersecurity policy. The partnership includes the appointment of liaison officers to streamline coordination and promote timely information-sharing on matters such as drug trafficking and transnational organised crime. Both countries also plan to strengthen collaboration on immigration enforcement while adhering to domestic laws and international obligations. This coordinated approach is designed to address evolving cross-border security challenges and reinforce bilateral cooperation through structured and institutional mechanisms.

questions.question: What was India’s rank in the Network Readiness Index 2025 after improving four places globally?
questions.explanation: 45th is the position secured by India in the Network Readiness Index 2025 after climbing four places globally among 127 economies. The improvement reflects steady progress in digital infrastructure, technology adoption, and innovation-driven growth. India’s overall score also increased compared to the previous year, indicating stronger digital foundations. The country performed exceptionally well in areas such as telecom investment, AI scientific publications, ICT services exports, and e-commerce legislation. Additionally, India ranked 2nd among lower-middle-income economies, highlighting its ability to leverage digital transformation effectively despite income-level constraints.

questions.question: Which company received RBI in-principle approval to join Centralised Payment Systems (RTGS/NEFT)?
questions.explanation: Transcorp received in-principle approval from the Reserve Bank of India to join the Centralised Payment Systems, including RTGS and NEFT. This approval allows the company to obtain its own IFSC code and maintain a bank account directly with the RBI, significantly strengthening its operational framework. The move enhances its digital payment ecosystem, improves compliance standards, and boosts operational efficiency. By gaining direct access to centralized payment infrastructure, the company is better positioned for technology-driven expansion and independent growth. It is a public limited company and is listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange.

questions.question: Which state presented its first-ever Economic Survey 2025-26, outlining a roadmap to achieve a $1 trillion economy?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh presented its first-ever Economic Survey 2025-26, marking a significant milestone in the state’s fiscal and developmental planning. The survey outlines a comprehensive, investment-led growth strategy aimed at transforming the state into a $1 trillion economy. It highlights strong Gross State Domestic Product growth, rising investor confidence, and major infrastructure expansion, including expressways and airports. The document also emphasises industrial cluster development, agricultural productivity, digital governance reforms, and improved law and order. With sustained double-digit growth and large-scale investment proposals, the state positions itself as a rapidly emerging economic powerhouse in India.

questions.question: What is the revised collateral-free loan limit for Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) as increased by the RBI effective April 1?
questions.explanation: ₹20 Lakh is the revised collateral-free loan limit set by the Reserve Bank of India for Micro and Small Enterprises. The earlier limit of ₹10 lakh has been doubled to enhance access to formal credit for small businesses. This step is aimed at promoting entrepreneurship, strengthening last-mile credit delivery, and supporting enterprises that face constraints in providing collateral. The decision also reflects the strong credit performance of the MSE sector, which recorded a 31% year-on-year growth, highlighting its expanding role in the economy.

questions.question: On which date is World Pulses Day observed annually?
questions.explanation: February 10 is observed annually as World Pulses Day to highlight the nutritional and environmental significance of pulses such as beans, lentils, chickpeas, and peas. The day emphasises their role in ensuring food security, promoting sustainable agriculture, and supporting healthy diets worldwide. The United Nations General Assembly established this observance in 2018, building on the momentum created by the 2016 International Year of Pulses. Each year features a specific theme, and in 2026, the theme focuses on recognising pulses’ journey from modest crops to globally valued sources of nutrition and sustainability.

questions.question: Which state’s Legislative Assembly witnessed the launch of the National e-Vidhan Application (NeVA)?
questions.explanation: Bihar witnessed the launch of the National e-Vidhan Application (NeVA) in its Legislative Assembly by Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The initiative promotes paperless functioning and enables MLAs to access legislative documents such as bills, rules, questions, notices, and reports digitally through their devices. Developed on a NIC-cloud based workflow system, the platform enhances transparency, efficiency, and streamlined legislative processes. It also aims to integrate all state legislatures into a unified digital framework, creating a comprehensive legislative data repository across the country.

questions.question: Which state’s Pharmacy Council signed an MoU with the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission to enhance drug safety and quality standards?
questions.explanation: Goa signed the Memorandum of Understanding with the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission to strengthen drug safety and improve quality standards nationwide. The collaboration aims to enhance pharmacovigilance systems and improve the reporting of Adverse Drug Reactions (ADR). It also promotes the rational use of medicines and focuses on professional training and capacity-building programs for pharmacists. Through these coordinated initiatives, the effort seeks to elevate regulatory compliance, ensure safer pharmaceutical practices, and improve overall healthcare delivery across the country.

questions.question: Which company launched inFINity 3.0 to accelerate early-stage fintech startups?
questions.explanation: PayU introduced inFINity 3.0 as its most comprehensive fintech accelerator program aimed at supporting early-stage startups from the build phase to market readiness. The initiative provides rapid distribution access through PayU’s extensive network and includes a structured four-week acceleration sprint. Participating startups receive direct mentorship, compliance guidance, and assistance in developing effective Go-To-Market strategies. The program also features a Demo Day in collaboration with investor Atrium Ventures and offers AWS cloud support. Through these resources and partnerships, the accelerator seeks to strengthen innovation and scalability within the fintech ecosystem.

questions.question: Which country's air force launched the 'Buddy Squadron' joint aerial drill along with the US at Osan Air Base?
questions.explanation: South Korean joint aerial exercise titled 'Buddy Squadron' was launched by the South Korean and US air forces at Osan Air Base in Pyeongtaek. The 2026 edition of the drill features a significant expansion, including doubled aircraft participation and increased sorties. It also integrates training involving fourth- and fifth-generation fighter jets to improve tactical coordination. The exercise rotates between the two allies and plays a crucial role in strengthening interoperability, enhancing joint operational readiness, and reinforcing regional security cooperation between the allied forces.

questions.question: In which city will the manufacturing facility for advanced Type IV & V composite pressure vessels under the indigenous hydrogen storage project be established?
questions.explanation: Madurai is the city where the manufacturing facility for advanced Type IV and Type V composite pressure vessels will be established under the indigenous hydrogen storage project. The Technology Development Board under the Department of Science and Technology has signed an agreement with Gudlyf Mobility to support 700 bar hydrogen storage systems. The project includes funding for the development of advanced composite pressure vessels and setting up a manufacturing unit in Madurai. This initiative promotes Aatmanirbhar Bharat by reducing import dependence and supporting hydrogen mobility, UAV, CNG, and defence applications.

questions.question: Which country has been designated as the Country of the Year at BIOFACH 2026 in Nuremberg?
questions.explanation: India is the country designated as the Country of the Year at BIOFACH 2026 held in Nuremberg. This recognition highlights the nation’s leadership and growing prominence in the field of organic agriculture. APEDA is spearheading India’s participation by organising a large pavilion that showcases a wide range of organic products, including rice, spices, oilseeds, herbs, and value-added items. The involvement of over 20 States and Union Territories demonstrates the country’s rich agricultural diversity and its expanding footprint in the global organic export market, strengthening its position internationally.

questions.question: On which date is the International Day of Women and Girls in Science observed every year?
questions.explanation: February 11 is observed annually as the International Day of Women and Girls in Science by the United Nations. The day promotes equal participation of women and girls in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) fields. It aims to address the persistent gender gap in scientific research, leadership roles and emerging sectors such as artificial intelligence. By highlighting the importance of inclusive access to science education and careers, the observance supports the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. Encouraging women’s participation in STEM strengthens innovation, economic growth and sustainable global development.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the new dragonfly species “Lyriothemis keralensis” discovered?
questions.explanation: Kerala is the state where the new dragonfly species Lyriothemis keralensis was discovered. The species was identified by researchers near Kothamangalam and has been named to honour the state’s rich biodiversity. It primarily inhabits shaded plantation canals and pools and is mostly seen during the Southwest monsoon season. For nearly a decade, the species had been misidentified, highlighting the importance of detailed taxonomic research. The discovery also raises conservation concerns because most of its known populations are located outside protected areas, making habitat preservation and ecological monitoring crucial for its survival.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) of the National Capital Territory of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Ashok Kumar has been appointed as the Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) of the National Capital Territory of Delhi by the Election Commission of India. He is an IAS officer and has been directed to assume charge immediately. He replaces R Alice Vaz in this important administrative role. His selection followed the submission of a panel of three IAS officers by the Delhi government. The appointment is significant as it aims to strengthen the electoral administration in Delhi, especially in preparation for upcoming electoral processes and to ensure smooth and efficient conduct of elections.

questions.question: In which city did the Delhi Tourism and Transportation Development Corporation sign an MoU to partner with Bharat Taxi for tech-enabled taxi services?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the city where the Delhi Tourism and Transportation Development Corporation signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Sahkar Taxi Cooperative Limited to partner with Bharat Taxi. This initiative makes Delhi the first state to collaborate with Bharat Taxi for providing technology-enabled and transparent taxi services. The partnership aims to enhance tourism and urban mobility by offering curated sightseeing circuits, improving last-mile connectivity, and deploying trained driver-ambassadors. The move is expected to modernize transportation services, ensure better commuter experiences, and strengthen the capital’s tourism infrastructure.

questions.question: Which country clinched the SAFF U-19 Women’s Championship title after defeating Bangladesh 4–0 in the final?
questions.explanation: India is the country that clinched the SAFF U-19 Women’s Championship title with a dominant 4–0 victory over Bangladesh in the final held at Pokhara Rangasala Stadium in Nepal. The team delivered an impressive performance, with Captain Julan Nongmaithem opening the scoring, followed by goals from Elizabeth Lakra, Pearl Fernandes, and Anwita Raghuraman. The triumph also served as redemption for an earlier setback and provided valuable competitive exposure for the U-17 squad. The tournament acted as crucial preparation for the upcoming AFC U-17 Women’s Asian Cup, strengthening the team’s international prospects.

questions.question: Which country signed a Social Security Agreement with India to prevent double contributions for employees on assignments up to 36 months?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom is the country that signed the Social Security Agreement with India to prevent double social security contributions for employees posted on short-term assignments of up to 36 months. The agreement applies to the UK and Northern Ireland and is intended to facilitate workforce mobility by reducing financial and administrative burdens on professionals working in each other’s territories. It will come into effect along with the India–UK Comprehensive Economic Trade Agreement, marking a significant step in strengthening bilateral economic cooperation and deepening strategic ties between the two nations.

questions.question: Which country signed a military cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia to deepen defence ties amid rising Gulf rivalries in the Horn of Africa?
questions.explanation: Somalia is the country that signed a military cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia to strengthen defence ties in the Horn of Africa. The pact focuses on areas such as military training, technical assistance, and broader defence support aimed at enhancing the capabilities of the Somali armed forces and safeguarding its airspace. This agreement comes at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions in the region, including issues related to Somaliland, Red Sea security, and shifting alliances among Gulf nations. The move reflects Somalia’s efforts to bolster its national security framework amid evolving regional dynamics.

questions.question: What is India’s global rank in LEED-certified real estate space outside the United States?
questions.explanation: Second is India’s global rank in LEED-certified real estate space outside the United States. According to data from the US Green Building Council and Green Business Certification Inc., India stands just after Mainland China in terms of certified green building space. The country has recorded around 16 million square meters of LEED-certified space, reflecting significant progress in sustainable construction practices. Much of this growth has been driven by increased adoption of LEED Operations and Maintenance (O&M), highlighting a strong focus on improving energy efficiency in existing buildings across sectors such as offices, industry, retail, and education.

questions.question: Which state police launched Phase 2 of the 72-hour ‘Operation Prahar’ to intensify action against gangsters and wanted criminals?
questions.explanation: Phase 2 of the 72-hour ‘Operation Prahar’ was launched by the police force of Punjab to strengthen action against gangsters and wanted criminals across the state. The first phase resulted in more than 3,000 arrests and the seizure of 69 weapons, indicating a strong crackdown on organised crime. Senior headquarters officers were deputed to supervise operations statewide, ensuring strict monitoring and coordination. The Director General of Police, Gaurav Yadav, also encouraged citizens to provide information through the Anti-Gangster Helpline to support ongoing enforcement efforts.

questions.question: Which city has unveiled India’s first ‘Musical Road’ that plays the song “Jai Ho” as vehicles pass over it?
questions.explanation: Mumbai has introduced India’s first-ever musical road on its Coastal Road project, creating a unique blend of infrastructure and innovation. The special stretch, located on the northbound carriageway between Nariman Point and Worli, plays the tune of “Jai Ho” when vehicles travel at a steady speed of 70–80 km/h. Precisely cut grooves on the road surface generate vibrations that produce musical notes over a 500-metre stretch. Commissioned by the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC), the initiative also incorporates safety measures such as advanced warning signboards to ensure smooth traffic flow and a safe driving experience.

questions.question: What is India’s rank in global Research and Development (R&D) spending according to the World Intellectual Property Organisation report?
questions.explanation: Global Research and Development expenditure reached $2.87 trillion in 2024, reflecting a 3% year-on-year increase and nearly tripling since 2000. Asia accounts for 45% of the total global share, highlighting the region’s growing innovation capacity. China leads global R&D spending with $785.9 billion, slightly ahead of the United States at $781.8 billion. India holds the 7th position with an expenditure of $75.7 billion. Although India’s R&D spending has grown more than threefold since 2000, it remains about one-tenth of China’s total outlay, indicating scope for further expansion.

questions.question: On which date will the National Productivity Council celebrate its 68th Foundation Day in 2026?
questions.explanation: February 12 marks the 68th Foundation Day of the National Productivity Council (NPC) in 2026. The celebration also coincides with the beginning of National Productivity Week, observed from February 12 to 18 across India. The 2026 theme, “Clusters as Growth Engine: Maximising Productivity in MSMEs,” focuses on strengthening Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises through cluster-based development. Established in 1958, NPC functions under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry. It promotes productivity enhancement through consultancy, training, research, and international cooperation, including engagement with the Asian Productivity Organisation.

questions.question: Who has become India’s youngest male analog astronaut after completing an 8-day lunar habitat simulation in Dholavira, Kutch?
questions.explanation: Aditya Pandya has achieved the distinction of becoming India’s youngest male analog astronaut at the age of 17. He completed an eight-day lunar habitat simulation conducted in Dholavira, Kutch, where a four-member crew lived in a container-based habitat designed to replicate Moon-like conditions. The mission focused on simulating isolation and autonomous operations similar to real space missions. Advanced technologies such as digital twin systems, IoT sensors, biometric tracking, and real-time telemetry were employed to monitor habitat conditions and crew health, demonstrating innovation in space research and training methodologies.

questions.question: According to the SBI report, what percentage of India’s agricultural export items to the United States now enjoy zero tariff access?
questions.explanation: The SBI report highlights a significant boost to India’s agricultural exports to the United States, with 75% of export items now receiving zero-tariff access. This improved tariff framework enhances price competitiveness and expands market opportunities for Indian exporters. Agricultural products worth over $1 billion are assured zero reciprocal tariff benefits, strengthening India’s existing $1.3 billion trade surplus in agri-trade with the US. Key beneficiary sectors include rice, fisheries, tea, coffee, and spices. The move is expected to increase export volumes, improve price realisation, and support farmers and rural income growth through better global market integration.

questions.question: What is the current doctor-population ratio in India, as informed to the Rajya Sabha, surpassing the World Health Organisation norm?
questions.explanation: India’s doctor-population ratio has improved significantly, reaching 1:811, which is better than the World Health Organisation’s prescribed norm of 1:1000. This improvement is based on the Health Dynamics 2022–23 report that considers 13.88 lakh allopathic and 7.51 lakh AYUSH doctors, assuming 80% of them are actively available for service. The strengthened healthcare workforce indicates progress in medical infrastructure and human resource planning. Additionally, government support through initiatives like the National Health Mission has contributed to addressing gaps in healthcare personnel across states, enhancing overall public health service delivery.

questions.question: Which country has assumed the chair of the Kimberley Process for 2026?
questions.explanation: India has assumed the chair of the Kimberley Process for 2026, marking an important role in the global regulation of the diamond trade. The Kimberley Process is a tripartite initiative involving governments, the diamond industry, and civil society to prevent the circulation of conflict diamonds. It functions through the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme (KPCS), which is a voluntary and consensus-based arrangement rather than a legally binding treaty. Compliance is ensured through domestic laws of participating countries, reflecting a cooperative international framework aimed at maintaining transparency and ethical practices in the diamond industry.

questions.question: The Indian Air Force conducted its first international air exercise of 2026 with which country near the strategically vital Strait of Malacca?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force carried out its first international air exercise of 2026 with Thailand, specifically with the Royal Thai Air Force (RTAF). The joint drills were conducted near the strategically significant Strait of Malacca, a key maritime chokepoint handling nearly one-quarter of global seaborne trade and a substantial share of global energy shipments. The exercise featured IAF’s Su-30MKI fighters alongside Thailand’s Gripen aircraft and focused on advanced aerial combat, mid-air refuelling, surveillance integration, and maritime domain awareness. The engagement strengthens Indo-Thai defence cooperation and enhances India’s strategic presence in the Indo-Pacific region.

questions.question: Which state has the Balaghat district selected as the pilot district for the World’s Largest Grain Storage Plan in the cooperative sector?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has selected the Balaghat district selected as the pilot district for implementing the World’s Largest Grain Storage Plan in the cooperative sector. A 500 MT godown was inaugurated in February 2024 under this initiative. The plan is implemented through Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS) and cooperative societies by converging schemes such as the Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF), Agricultural Marketing Infrastructure (AMI), Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanisation (SMAM), and PMFME. Reforms include reducing the beneficiary margin from 20% to 10%, revising construction costs, and enhancing scientific storage capacity to reduce post-harvest losses and support farmers.

questions.question: What is the official duration of the mandated version of Vande Mataram at key functions as per the Ministry of Home Affairs protocol?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs issued a protocol regarding the proper rendition of Vande Mataram during official events. It directed that when performed along with Jana Gana Mana, all six stanzas of Vande Mataram must be sung first. The official version has a prescribed duration of 3 minutes and 10 seconds and is mandated at key government functions. During its rendition, the audience is required to stand in attention as a mark of respect, except in specific contexts such as film screenings. Educational institutions are also encouraged to begin the day with community singing.

questions.question: When is World Radio Day 2026 observed globally to celebrate radio as a public service medium?
questions.explanation: World Radio Day 2026 is observed on February 13 each year to recognise the enduring importance of radio as a powerful and accessible medium of communication. The date commemorates the launch of United Nations Radio in 1946, symbolising global cooperation and information sharing after World War II. The 2026 theme, “Radio and Artificial Intelligence: AI is a tool, not a voice,” highlights the supportive role of technology in broadcasting while preserving human creativity and credibility. The observance also acknowledges the radio’s role in education, disaster communication, cultural preservation, and community engagement worldwide.

questions.question: Which state launched the ‘Sthree Suraksha’ Scheme, providing ₹1,000 monthly pension to over 10 lakh women and transwomen beneficiaries?
questions.explanation: Kerala launched the ‘Sthree Suraksha’ Scheme to provide financial assistance of ₹1,000 per month to economically backward women and transwomen aged between 35 and 60 years. The initiative aims to promote gender justice and economic independence by supporting those not covered under other social welfare pension programmes. Over 10 lakh verified beneficiaries received the first instalment through direct benefit transfer. The scheme reflects Kerala’s welfare-oriented governance model, emphasising inclusive development, gender budgeting, and women’s empowerment while ensuring transparency through careful verification and timely fund disbursement.

questions.question: Which endangered mammal has been protected through a hunting and trade ban imposed by the Sangtam Naga tribal body in Nagaland?
questions.explanation: The Sangtam Naga tribal body in Nagaland has imposed a ban on the hunting and trade of pangolins, marking a significant step toward wildlife conservation in Northeast India. Pangolins are among the most trafficked mammals globally, targeted for their scales and meat. Despite legal protection under the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, illegal trade continues in several regions. The community-led decision strengthens local monitoring and supports broader conservation initiatives aimed at countering wildlife trafficking. Such grassroots efforts complement national laws and highlight the vital role of indigenous communities in protecting endangered species.

questions.question: Where will Exercise Vayushakti-26 be conducted to showcase the Indian Air Force’s combat readiness and operational capabilities?
questions.explanation: Exercise Vayushakti-26 is scheduled to be conducted at Pokhran in Rajasthan. The exercise aims to demonstrate the Indian Air Force’s operational reach, dominance over airspace, and its ability to carry out humanitarian assistance missions. It will feature a wide array of assets, including fighter aircraft such as Tejas and Rafale, transport aircraft like C-130J and C-295, helicopters including Apache and Chinook, and unmanned aerial vehicles. The event also highlights the use of indigenous defence systems and reflects India’s commitment to strengthening self-reliance under the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative.

questions.question: Which state has introduced an AI-based facial recognition system to record attendance of MLAs in the legislative assembly?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh has implemented an AI-powered facial recognition system to digitally record the attendance of Members of the Legislative Assembly. The new system replaces traditional physical registers and ensures that attendance is marked only when MLAs are seated in the House. It also tracks the duration of their presence, promoting greater accountability and transparency. The initiative aims to curb absenteeism and address concerns related to paid absences. By enforcing stricter monitoring and supporting the principle of “no work, no pay,” the reform strengthens legislative discipline and institutional integrity.

questions.question: What was the value of the RBI Digital Payments Index (RBI-DPI) for September 2025, reflecting growth in India’s digital payment ecosystem?
questions.explanation: The RBI Digital Payments Index for September 2025 stood at 516.76, indicating significant growth in digital payment adoption across India. This increase from the previous value of 493.22 in March 2025 reflects improvements in key parameters such as Payment Performance and Payment Enablers. The index measures the extent of digitisation in the country and includes factors like infrastructure, transaction volume, and consumer centricity. A higher index value signifies deeper penetration of digital payment systems, showcasing India’s steady progress toward a less-cash and more transparent economy supported by robust digital infrastructure.

questions.question: When is National Women’s Day in India observed to mark the birth anniversary of Sarojini Naidu?
questions.explanation: National Women’s Day in India is observed on February 13 to commemorate the birth anniversary of Sarojini Naidu, a prominent freedom fighter, poet, and advocate of women’s rights. Born in 1879, she played a key role in India’s independence movement and became the first Indian woman President of the Indian National Congress as well as the first woman Governor of the United Provinces. The observance highlights her contributions to literature, social reform, and political leadership while promoting awareness about gender equality, women’s empowerment, and the importance of active participation of women in public life.

questions.question: Which political party claimed a “decisive victory” in the 2026 general elections in Bangladesh, positioning Tarique Rahman as the likely Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party claimed a decisive victory in the 2026 general elections, marking a significant political shift in the country. This development positions Tarique Rahman as the likely next Prime Minister following his return from a long period of exile in London. The party campaigned on restoring democratic governance, institutional stability, and national reconciliation after years of political unrest. The election outcome represents a major comeback for the BNP after being in opposition for an extended period and follows the political upheaval that led to leadership changes in Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which Mumbai-based company signed an MoU with the Indian Army to supply advanced carbon fabrics and composite materials for defence applications?
questions.explanation: Agneevasthraa, a Mumbai-based company, signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Indian Army to provide advanced carbon fabrics and high-performance composite materials for defence use. These materials are essential for modern military applications due to their high strength-to-weight ratio, durability, and resistance to extreme conditions. They are used in protective gear, armoured systems, aerospace components, and structural defence applications. The collaboration supports indigenous defence manufacturing and aligns with the Make in India initiative, reducing reliance on imports while strengthening India’s technological self-reliance and overall defence preparedness.

questions.question: Which organisation partnered with the Department of Posts to distribute mutual fund products through post offices across India?
questions.explanation: The Department of Posts signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the National Stock Exchange (NSE) to enable the distribution of mutual fund products through India Post’s extensive network. Under this partnership, selected and certified postal employees will be trained and onboarded as mutual fund distributors using NSE’s platform. This initiative aims to expand access to mutual fund investments, especially in rural and semi-urban areas, by leveraging the widespread reach and trusted presence of post offices. The collaboration is a significant step toward promoting financial inclusion and increasing retail participation in capital markets across the country.

questions.question: Which state emerged as the largest wool-producing state in India in FY24 with a 47.5% share of national output?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan recorded the highest wool production in India during FY24, contributing 16,013.50 thousand kg, which accounted for approximately 47.53% of the total national output of 33.69 million kg. The state’s arid climate and vast grazing lands create favourable conditions for sheep rearing. Indigenous breeds such as Marwari and Chokla produce mainly coarse wool, widely used in carpet and blanket manufacturing. With India ranking ninth globally in wool production and second in sheep population, Rajasthan’s dominant contribution significantly strengthens rural livelihoods, textile industries, and the broader wool economy.

questions.question: Which banker has been appointed as the new Chairman of GIFT City by the Gujarat government?
questions.explanation: The Gujarat government has appointed Uday Kotak, founder of Kotak Mahindra Bank, as the new Chairman of GIFT City, India’s first operational Smart City and maiden International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) located in Gandhinagar. He replaces Hasmukh Adhia in this role. GIFT City plays a significant role in strengthening India’s global financial presence, with substantial banking assets and international participation. The appointment of an experienced banking leader like Uday Kotak is expected to enhance the city’s global competitiveness, attract more international financial institutions, and support the continued expansion of India’s IFSC ecosystem.

questions.question: How many friendly foreign countries will participate in the International Military Adventure Challenge Cup (IMACC) 2026 hosted by the Indian Army?
questions.explanation: The International Military Adventure Challenge Cup (IMACC) 2026 will witness participation from seven friendly foreign nations alongside Indian armed forces teams. These countries include Bhutan, Brazil, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Saudi Arabia. The event is scheduled to take place in the challenging terrain of the Eastern Himalayas and is aimed at enhancing defence cooperation and operational coordination. By bringing together multiple nations, the competition promotes interoperability, endurance, and camaraderie among participating forces. The presence of seven foreign nations highlights India’s expanding defence diplomacy and growing engagement in international military sports cooperation initiatives.

questions.question: Which private aerospace company has signed an agreement with NASA for its first private astronaut mission to the International Space Station (ISS)?
questions.explanation: Vast Space has signed an agreement with NASA for its first private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, targeting a launch no earlier than summer 2027. The mission will use SpaceX’s Falcon 9 rocket and Dragon spacecraft, with astronauts expected to spend up to 14 days aboard the ISS. This collaboration marks a major milestone in the transition toward commercial space stations. Vast is also developing Haven-1, projected to become the world’s first commercial space station, supporting long-term goals of expanding the orbital economy and enabling a sustained human presence in space.

questions.question: Which state hosted the 18th Foundation Day celebration of the National Statistical Systems Training Academy (NSSTA)?
questions.explanation: The 18th Foundation Day of the National Statistical Systems Training Academy (NSSTA) was organised at Mahalanobis Auditorium, Knowledge Park-II, Greater Noida, in Uttar Pradesh. NSSTA operates under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation and serves as a premier training institute for strengthening India’s official statistical system. The event emphasized AI-driven capacity building, digital transformation, and improved data governance aligned with the vision of Viksit Bharat@2047. The inauguration of the Digital Data Lab and focus on advanced analytics highlighted efforts to modernize statistical infrastructure and enhance evidence-based policymaking in India.

questions.question: Which country received a USD 175 million Special Economic Package announced by India during President Patrick Herminie’s visit?
questions.explanation: Seychelles received a USD 175 million Special Economic Package from India during the visit of President Patrick Herminie to New Delhi. The package covers social housing, e-mobility, health infrastructure, vocational training, defence, and maritime security. In addition, seven Memoranda of Understanding were signed to enhance cooperation in digital transformation, meteorology, governance, and health sectors. The initiative reflects India’s strategic focus on strengthening partnerships in the Indian Ocean region under Vision MAHASAGAR, reinforcing maritime security cooperation and deepening long-standing diplomatic ties between the two nations.

questions.question: Which company received the FIPI Award 2025 as ‘Oil & Gas Production Company of the Year’ during India Energy Week held in Goa?
questions.explanation: Oil India Limited was honoured with the FIPI Award 2025 for ‘Oil & Gas Production Company of the Year’ (more than 1 MTOE) during India Energy Week in Goa. The award was presented by the Federation of Indian Petroleum Industry in recognition of the company’s strong production growth and contribution to India’s energy security. As a Maharatna Central Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, OIL has expanded exploration activities, enhanced recovery from mature fields, and adopted advanced technologies to improve operational efficiency and strengthen domestic energy production.

questions.question: Which vaccine has been additionally prequalified by the World Health Organisation to strengthen the global response against circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus type 2 (cVDPV2)?
questions.explanation: The World Health Organisation has prequalified an additional novel oral polio vaccine type 2 (nOPV2) to enhance global outbreak response against circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus type 2 (cVDPV2). This vaccine is genetically more stable than older oral polio vaccines and carries a lower risk of mutating into virulent strains while maintaining its ability to quickly stop virus transmission. The prequalification confirms that it meets international standards for safety, quality, and effectiveness, enabling global procurement and distribution. It also diversifies vaccine supply and strengthens worldwide efforts aimed at the final eradication of polio.

questions.question: Which company has secured a U.S. license allowing it to import crude oil from Venezuela, enhancing India’s energy security and refining flexibility?
questions.explanation: Reliance Industries Limited has obtained a general licence from the United States government that enables it to directly import crude oil from Venezuela without violating U.S. sanctions, a significant development for India’s energy sector. This move diversifies Reliance’s crude supply options, especially for heavy crude suited to its Jamnagar refinery, which is among the world’s most complex refining facilities. The licence boosts refining flexibility and supports India’s energy security by allowing the company to access discounted heavy Venezuelan crude, improving feedstock options and potentially lowering refining costs, while navigating shifting global geopolitics.

questions.question: Which state signed a Letter of Intent with Starlink, a subsidiary of SpaceX, to provide satellite-based internet connectivity in remote and tribal areas?
questions.explanation: Gujarat signed a Letter of Intent with Starlink to deploy satellite-based broadband connectivity in remote, tribal, and underserved regions. The agreement, signed in Gandhinagar, aims to bridge the digital divide by strengthening e-governance, healthcare, education, disaster management, and industrial connectivity. Pilot projects will cover tribal and Aspirational Districts such as Narmada and Dahod. The initiative aligns with the Digital India vision and seeks to ensure last-mile digital access, enabling efficient public service delivery, telemedicine, smart classrooms, and improved emergency response systems across the state.

questions.question: Which individual has been approved by the Reserve Bank of India as the new Managing Director and CEO of City Union Bank from May 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: R Vijay Anandh has been approved by the Reserve Bank of India as the new Managing Director and CEO of City Union Bank for a three-year term beginning May 1, 2026. His appointment is subject to shareholder approval and comes with a fixed annual remuneration of ₹2.50 crore. With over 28 years of experience in banking, including roles in risk management, credit appraisal, and retail assets, he brings extensive expertise to the position. Having served as Executive President and later Executive Director at the bank, his elevation ensures leadership continuity and stability during the transition.

questions.question: Which country jointly announced the SESEL (Sustainability, Economic Growth and Security through Enhanced Linkages) Vision with India during President Patrick Herminie’s State Visit?
questions.explanation: Seychelles partnered with India to unveil the SESEL Joint Vision aimed at strengthening sustainability, economic growth, and security cooperation. The announcement was made during President Patrick Herminie’s State Visit to India on 9 February 2026. The vision outlines collaboration in maritime security, digital public infrastructure, renewable energy, climate action, trade, and capacity building. It also coincided with 50 years of diplomatic relations between the two nations. The initiative reflects Seychelles’ strategic importance in the Western Indian Ocean and reinforces a long-standing partnership rooted in development cooperation and regional stability.

questions.question: Which Indian shooter won the silver medal in the 25m pistol event at the Asian Shooting Championships 2026 held in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Manu Bhaker secured the silver medal in the 25m pistol event at the Asian Shooting Championships 2026 in New Delhi. She delivered a strong comeback performance after finishing seventh in the 10m air pistol event earlier in the tournament. Manu qualified with an impressive score of 584 and later scored 35 points in a tense final, which included two shoot-offs. Although she narrowly missed gold, the silver marked her first individual silver medal in the 25m pistol category, reflecting improved consistency, mental resilience, and growing confidence ahead of major events like the Asian Games and Olympic qualification rounds.

questions.question: Which country signed a Joint Declaration of Intent with India to strengthen defence industrial cooperation and establish a five-year roadmap?
questions.explanation: Greece signed a Joint Declaration of Intent with India to enhance defence industrial cooperation and outline a structured five-year roadmap. The agreement aligns India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative with Greece’s Agenda 2030 defence reforms, promoting co-development, co-production, and technology partnerships. Both nations also exchanged the Bilateral Military Cooperation Plan for 2026 to expand joint exercises, training, and strategic engagement. Additionally, Greece announced the deployment of a liaison officer to the Information Fusion Centre–Indian Ocean Region in Gurugram, strengthening maritime security collaboration and reinforcing their growing Indo-Mediterranean strategic partnership.

questions.question: Which individual topped the Civil Judge (Junior Division) examination merit list among candidates with benchmark disabilities in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Thanya Nathan topped the Civil Judge (Junior Division) examination merit list among candidates with benchmark disabilities in Kerala, marking a historic moment in the state’s judicial system. A visually impaired lawyer from Kannur district, she excelled academically, topping her LLB programme at Kannur University and preparing for the judicial exam using Braille materials, screen-reading software, and audio resources. Her achievement reflects growing judicial inclusivity, supported by a landmark Supreme Court ruling affirming that disability cannot be grounds for disqualification from judicial service. Her appointment symbolises progress toward a more accessible and representative judiciary in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the proposed national museum into which the North and South Blocks will be converted?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet has approved the conversion of North Block and South Block into the ‘Yuge Yugeen Bharat’ National Museum, marking a historic transformation in India’s administrative landscape. These iconic buildings, constructed during British rule, have served as key centres of governance since Independence. With the inauguration of the new Prime Minister’s Office, Seva Teerth, by Narendra Modi, Cabinet meetings will shift from South Block. The proposed museum will showcase India’s civilisational journey, freedom struggle, post-independence development, and modern global aspirations, symbolising the transition from colonial legacy to citizen-centric governance.

questions.question: In which state was the CBDC-based Digital Food Coupon Pilot launched to ensure secure and transparent subsidy distribution?
questions.explanation: The pilot project for distributing digital food coupons using Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) was launched in Gujarat. This initiative was introduced by the Government of India to enhance targeted welfare delivery through secure and transparent digital payments. By issuing coupons in digital rupee, the scheme ensures leakage-free subsidy transfers directly to beneficiaries. The pilot also promotes financial inclusion and strengthens India’s digital payment ecosystem. The move supports broader CBDC adoption efforts led by the Reserve Bank of India, aiming to modernise welfare distribution through innovative financial technology solutions.

questions.question: In which state did the Prime Minister inaugurate the Kumar Bhaskar Varma Setu over the Brahmaputra River?
questions.explanation: The Kumar Bhaskar Varma Setu was inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the state of Assam. The six-lane extradosed PSC bridge has been constructed over the Brahmaputra River in Guwahati and provides a direct connection between Guwahati and North Guwahati. It is the first extradosed bridge in the Northeast region, marking a significant milestone in regional infrastructure development. The project is expected to improve connectivity, facilitate smoother transportation, and boost trade, tourism, and overall socio-economic integration across Northeast India.

questions.question: Which company signed a pact with the Centre for Development of Telematics to develop a tool for detecting quantum-vulnerable cryptographic algorithms?
questions.explanation: The Centre for Development of Telematics (C-DOT) entered into an agreement with Synergy Quantum India Private Ltd. to enhance cybersecurity preparedness against emerging quantum threats. The collaboration focuses on developing a specialised tool capable of scanning digital devices, identifying existing security mechanisms, and classifying cryptographic algorithms as either quantum-safe or vulnerable. As quantum computing advances, traditional encryption methods may become susceptible to attacks. This initiative aims to safeguard critical sectors such as defence, telecommunications, banking, and government systems by proactively strengthening encryption standards and improving national cybersecurity resilience in the evolving technological landscape.

questions.question: Who won the 2026 FIDE Freestyle Chess World Championship by defeating Fabiano Caruana in the final?
questions.explanation: Magnus Carlsen secured the 2026 FIDE Freestyle Chess World Championship title after defeating Fabiano Caruana 2.5–1.5 in the final held in Weissenhaus. A draw in the fourth game was sufficient for him to clinch the championship. This victory marked his 21st world title across various chess formats and his first official FIDE-recognised title in the Freestyle (Chess960) category. The tournament carried historic significance as it was the first official FIDE-recognised Freestyle Chess World Championship, further cementing Carlsen’s legacy as one of the greatest chess players in history.

questions.question: Which country is hosting the first-ever Global South AI Impact Summit 2026 at Bharat Mandapam?
questions.explanation: The Global South AI Impact Summit 2026 is being hosted by India at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi. This historic five-day summit marks the first global AI gathering held in the Global South, bringing together leaders, policymakers, industry experts, and international organisations from over 20 countries. The event focuses on shaping global Artificial Intelligence governance through three guiding pillars—People, Planet, and Progress—and seven thematic “Chakras.” By organising this large-scale international summit, India reinforces its position as a bridge between developed and developing nations and strengthens its leadership role in global digital governance and responsible AI development.

questions.question: In which city was the CBDC-based Public Distribution System (PDS) launched by Amit Shah?
questions.explanation: The CBDC-based Public Distribution System (PDS) was launched in Gandhinagar by Union Home Minister Amit Shah as part of a major reform in India’s food security framework. The initiative integrates Central Bank Digital Currency with ration distribution to ensure transparent, cashless, and leakage-free delivery of food grains. It aligns with the broader vision of Digital India and the governance principle of ‘Minimum Government, Maximum Governance’ promoted by Narendra Modi. The system enhances accountability, eliminates middlemen, and strengthens welfare delivery for millions of beneficiaries across the country.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution grants plenary powers to the Election Commission over electoral rolls and the conduct of elections?
questions.explanation: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission of India (ECI). These plenary powers enable the Commission to oversee electoral rolls and conduct elections to Parliament, state legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President. The authority under Article 324 supplements statutory provisions such as the Representation of the People Act, 1950, ensuring that elections remain free, fair, and credible. It also empowers the ECI to adjust procedural timelines, including Special Intensive Revision processes, to maintain transparency and effective electoral roll management.

questions.question: In which city will India’s first National Cow Culture Museum be established?
questions.explanation: India’s first National Cow Culture Museum will be set up in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, within the campus of Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya Veterinary Science University. The museum aims to present a comprehensive perspective on cattle by integrating cultural heritage with scientific understanding. It will showcase indigenous breeds, dairy production, sustainable agriculture practices, and cattle conservation techniques. Through digital and physical exhibits, the initiative seeks to highlight the economic and agricultural importance of cattle while also strengthening the cultural identity of the Braj region. The project is expected to promote education, tourism, and rural development.

questions.question: What was India’s Wholesale Price Index (WPI) inflation rate in January 2026, marking a 10-month high?
questions.explanation: India’s WPI inflation rose to 1.81% in January 2026, reaching a 10-month high and registering the third consecutive monthly increase. The rise was primarily driven by higher prices of food articles, non-food items, and manufactured products such as basic metals and textiles. Food inflation turned positive, and manufacturing inflation strengthened, indicating increasing producer-level price pressures. However, fuel and power remained in deflation, which helped moderate the overall surge. While WPI reflects wholesale-level price changes, the Reserve Bank of India mainly targets CPI inflation for monetary policy decisions, making this divergence significant for economic analysis.

questions.question: According to the latest DFS survey, what percentage of people prefer UPI as their primary transaction mode in India?
questions.explanation: The latest survey conducted by the Department of Financial Services highlights that 57% of respondents prefer UPI as their primary payment method, making it the most favoured transaction mode in India. This surpasses cash, which stands at 38%. The widespread adoption is driven by instant fund transfers, ease of use, and attractive cashback incentives. A significant portion of users, particularly those aged 18–25, perform multiple digital transactions daily. Additionally, 94% of small merchants now accept UPI, reflecting its deep penetration and the success of government-backed digital payment initiatives.

questions.question: In which city did President Droupadi Murmu inaugurate the centenary celebrations of the Ol Chiki script?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu inaugurated the centenary celebrations of the Ol Chiki script in New Delhi, marking 100 years since its creation in 1925 by Pandit Raghunath Murmu. The event, organised by the Ministry of Culture, celebrated the script’s role in preserving the Santhali language and strengthening Santal cultural identity. A commemorative coin and postage stamp were also released to honour this milestone. The Ol Chiki script has been instrumental in standardising the written form of Santhali and promoting linguistic pride among the Santal community in India and abroad.

questions.question: In which city will the DRDO-awarded Electronic Warfare project be executed?
questions.explanation: The ₹30.01 crore Electronic Warfare project awarded by the Defence Research and Development Organisation to Unistring Tech Solutions will be executed in Hyderabad. The project focuses on developing an advanced EW system with RF signal sensing and processing capabilities tailored to the operational needs of the Indian Armed Forces. Carried out in technical coordination with DRDO laboratories, the initiative supports the Atmanirbhar Bharat vision by strengthening indigenous defence manufacturing and enhancing the country’s electronic warfare preparedness and strategic capabilities.

questions.question: On which date is World Human Spirit Day observed annually?
questions.explanation: World Human Spirit Day is observed annually on February 17. Established in 2003 by philosopher Michael Levy, the day promotes self-reflection, mindfulness, and a deeper spiritual connection. It emphasises the importance of nurturing inner peace, building resilience, and recognising the human spirit’s strength in overcoming adversity. Individuals are encouraged to participate in activities such as meditation, retreats, and mindful practices to reduce stress and enhance emotional well-being. The observance serves as a reminder to focus on personal growth and spiritual awakening in today’s fast-paced world.

questions.question: Who won the BBC Indian Sportswoman of the Year 2025 award?
questions.explanation: Smriti Mandhana was honoured with the BBC Indian Sportswoman of the Year 2025 award in recognition of her exceptional performance during India’s Women’s World Cup triumph. At 29, she delivered a historic season, setting remarkable records including the fastest 50-over international century by an Indian and ranking among the top run-scorers in women’s cricket. The award ceremony took place in New Delhi, celebrating excellence across various sports disciplines. Her achievement highlights the rapid growth of women’s cricket in India and underscores her role as an inspiration for aspiring young athletes across the country.

questions.question: At which city was the MILAN Village inaugurated as part of Exercise MILAN 2026?
questions.explanation: The MILAN Village was inaugurated at the Eastern Naval Command in Visakhapatnam during Exercise MILAN 2026, a flagship multinational naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy. The initiative brings together naval personnel and delegates from more than 70 countries to strengthen maritime cooperation and mutual understanding. The village showcases Indian cultural performances, regional cuisine, and naval-themed exhibits, fostering camaraderie and cultural exchange among participating nations while enhancing collaborative maritime security efforts in the Indo-Pacific region.

questions.question: At which organisation did Raksha Mantri Rajnath Singh inaugurate the new Missile Integration Facility?
questions.explanation: Raksha Mantri Rajnath Singh inaugurated the Missile Integration Facility at Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) in Bengaluru. The facility strengthens indigenous missile system integration and supports faster deployment of advanced air defence systems like Akash.
