During the event, the Akash 3rd and 4th Regiment Combat Systems were flagged off, and the Mountain Fire Control Radar was unveiled. BEL also launched its AI Policy and inaugurated a Centre of Excellence for Artificial Intelligence in Pune, enhancing India’s self-reliant defence manufacturing capabilities and promoting advanced technologies such as AI-driven threat detection and network-centric warfare systems.

questions.question: Who will launch SAHI and BODH at the India AI Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: JP Nadda will launch SAHI and BODH at the India AI Summit 2026 at Bharat Mandapam. SAHI (Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare for India) provides a governance framework for ethical and responsible AI adoption in healthcare. BODH (Benchmarking Open Data Platform for Health AI), developed in collaboration with Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur and the National Health Authority, enables privacy-preserving evaluation of AI models using real-world health data under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, strengthening India’s digital health ecosystem.

questions.question: What rank did India achieve in the Henley Passport Index 2026?
questions.explanation: In the Henley Passport Index 2026, India secured the 75th position, marking a significant improvement from its 85th rank in 2025. This 10-place jump represents one of the country’s strongest performances in recent years. The ranking reflects the number of destinations Indian passport holders can access without a prior visa, including visa-free, visa-on-arrival, and e-visa options. Indian citizens now have access to 56 countries, up from 52 the previous year, enhancing global mobility for tourism and business travel while improving India’s standing in international travel freedom.

questions.question: Who was sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Bangladesh after the 13th Parliamentary Elections?
questions.explanation: Tarique Rahman was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Bangladesh following a decisive victory in the 13th Parliamentary Elections, where his party secured 209 out of 297 seats. The oath of office was administered by Mohammed Shahabuddin at the South Plaza of the Jatiya Sangsad in Dhaka. His appointment marks a significant political shift, ending the interim leadership of Muhammad Yunus and beginning a new chapter focused on economic recovery, institutional stability, and national reconciliation.

questions.question: Who won the gold medal in the men’s heptathlon at the Asian Indoor Athletics Championships 2026, setting a new national indoor record with 5,993 points?
questions.explanation: Tejaswin Shankar secured a historic gold medal in the men’s heptathlon at the Asian Indoor Athletics Championships 2026 held in Tianjin. He accumulated 5,993 points, establishing a new national indoor record and surpassing his previous best of 5,650 points set in 2021. His performance marked India’s only gold medal at the championship and played a crucial role in the country finishing sixth overall with five medals. The achievement highlights India’s growing strength in multi-event athletics on the continental stage.

questions.question: Which public sector bank launched an Online Death Claim Settlement Portal with a 15-day timeline and no surety requirement for claims up to ₹15 lakh?
questions.explanation: Indian Overseas Bank introduced an Online Death Claim Settlement Portal to provide a simplified, transparent, and time-bound solution for families and legal heirs. The digital platform enables online submission of claimant details, document uploads, and real-time tracking of claim status, eliminating the need for repeated branch visits. For claims up to ₹15 lakh, the bank has removed the requirement of third-party surety and reduced documentation, ensuring settlement within 15 days. Alongside this initiative, the bank also launched a new corporate website, reinforcing its commitment to digital transformation while maintaining a compassionate, customer-centric approach.

questions.question: Which country held talks with India to boost cooperation in telecom, digital economy, and emerging technologies?
questions.explanation: India held high-level discussions with Ireland to strengthen cooperation in telecom and emerging technologies. The talks involved Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia and Ireland’s Jack Chambers, focusing on innovation-driven partnerships. Key areas included India’s Digital Public Infrastructure, such as UPI and DBT, rapid 5G expansion, and rural broadband development. Both nations also explored collaborative opportunities in advanced sectors like Quantum Communications, Artificial Intelligence, and regulatory innovation, highlighting their shared commitment to deepening bilateral relations through technology and digital transformation initiatives.

questions.question: Who was named the BBC Indian Sportswoman of the Year 2025 after leading India to a historic Women’s World Cup victory?
questions.explanation: Smriti Mandhana was honoured as the BBC Indian Sportswoman of the Year 2025 in recognition of her exceptional leadership and performance. She guided India to a historic Women’s World Cup triumph, marking a significant milestone in Indian women’s cricket. Other notable contenders included Divya Deshmukh, a Chess World Cup winner, and Preethi Pal, a Paralympic medalist. Veteran shooter Anjali Bhagwat received the Lifetime Achievement Award, highlighting her long-standing contribution to Indian shooting sports.

questions.question: Which city hosted the 23rd BioAsia 2026 Summit?
questions.explanation: The 23rd BioAsia 2026 Summit was inaugurated in Hyderabad by Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy. The summit, themed ‘TechBio Unleashed – AI, Automation, and Revolutionary Changes in Life Sciences,’ brought together global experts and industry leaders. Over two days, participants discussed AI-driven research and development, next-generation biologics, digital transformation, and the evolving future of life sciences. The event also highlighted Telangana’s strategic vision to position itself as a world-class biopharma hub by 2047, reinforcing its growing prominence in biotechnology and pharmaceutical innovation.

questions.question: In which city was the International Conference on Dam Safety 2026 held, bringing together global experts and regulators?
questions.explanation: The International Conference on Dam Safety 2026 was concluded in Bengaluru, attracting global experts, regulators, and practitioners in the field of dam management and water resources. The conference focused on strengthening institutional and technical frameworks under the Dam Safety Act, 2021 and the Dam Rehabilitation and Improvement Project (DRIP). Key discussions revolved around rehabilitation of ageing dams, sediment management strategies, real-time monitoring systems, risk-based decision-making, coordinated flood operations, and enhancing climate resilience, reflecting India’s commitment to ensuring dam safety and sustainable water infrastructure management.

questions.question: Which initiative was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Emmanuel Macron to establish a helicopter assembly line in Karnataka?
questions.explanation: The H125 Project was launched by Narendra Modi and Emmanuel Macron as part of an upgraded India-France partnership. The initiative establishes India’s first private-sector helicopter manufacturing facility in Karnataka, featuring a Final Assembly Line for H125 helicopters. The project supports domestic production and exports, with an initial capacity of 10 helicopters per year. Known for its high-altitude performance, including landing on Mount Everest, the H125 will serve civil and potential military needs, strengthening India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat mission and deepening strategic aerospace collaboration between the two nations.

questions.question: Which Indian state is home to the 1,750 MW Demwe Lower hydel project that recently received an environmental clearance extension till 2037?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh hosts the 1,750 MW Demwe Lower hydel project located on the Lohit River. The project has been granted an 11-year extension of environmental clearance, allowing operations to continue until 2037. The approval was given by the Expert Appraisal Committee under the Ministry of Environment after considering litigation delays. However, the extension has generated debate due to earlier interventions by the National Green Tribunal regarding wildlife clearance and biodiversity concerns in the ecologically sensitive Lohit basin region.

questions.question: Which quick commerce platform partnered with Bizom to enhance retail execution and brand fulfilment across consumer goods companies?
questions.explanation: Bizom entered into a strategic partnership with Blinkit to strengthen retail execution and improve brand fulfilment capabilities. The collaboration enables brands to enhance fulfilment speed, minimise stock-outs, and access unified real-time inventory data across quick commerce platforms. By offering a single, reliable source of truth for inventory and sales information, the partnership allows brands to scale their reach without investing in new supply chain infrastructure. This initiative supports planning and execution for more than 750 consumer goods companies, contributing to more efficient, technology-driven, and future-ready retail operations across India.

questions.question: Which organisation partnered with GalaxEye to globally promote and sell its SyncFused OptoSAR satellite imagery?
questions.explanation: Space startup GalaxEye entered into a strategic agreement with NewSpace India Limited, commonly known as NSIL, which functions as the commercial arm of the Indian Space Research Organisation. This partnership makes GalaxEye the first private Indian satellite operator to establish a data reseller collaboration of this kind. Under the agreement, NSIL will globally market and distribute GalaxEye’s SyncFused OptoSAR satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to provide high-precision data solutions for sectors such as agriculture, disaster management, environmental monitoring, and other critical applications, enhancing India’s presence in the global space-based data market.

questions.question: Which country received its first high-speed ferry from India under the HICDP Phase III agreement to enhance maritime connectivity?
questions.explanation: The Maldives received its first high-speed ferry from India as part of the High Impact Community Development Project (HICDP) Phase III agreement. The ferry will operate under the Raajje Transport Link (RTL) network, improving connectivity between Faafu and Dhaalu Atolls. This initiative enhances inter-island transport, supports tourism and fisheries, and strengthens local livelihoods. Backed by Indian grant assistance, the project reflects India’s Neighbourhood First policy and its strategic commitment to maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region, reinforcing bilateral ties through infrastructure and community-focused development support.

questions.question: Which Indian state unanimously passed the Shops and Establishments (Amendment) Bill, 2026, allowing night shifts for women and increasing overtime limits?
questions.explanation: Gujarat unanimously passed the Shops and Establishments (Amendment) Bill, 2026, to expand employment opportunities for women and introduce greater flexibility in working conditions. The legislation permits women to work night shifts between 9 pm and 6 am with mandatory safeguards such as consent, transportation, rest facilities, creche provisions, and protection against harassment. It also increases the daily working hour limit from 9 to 10 hours while retaining the 48-hour weekly cap, and raises the overtime limit from 125 to 144 hours per quarter with double wage compensation. The Bill replaces a previous ordinance and retains government regulatory oversight powers.

questions.question: Which company announced the $15 billion ‘America-India Connect’ subsea cable initiative to enhance AI connectivity between India and the United States at the India AI Impact Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: Google announced the $15 billion America-India Connect initiative at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 to significantly strengthen digital and artificial intelligence connectivity between India and the United States. The project includes the establishment of a new international subsea gateway in Vizag, along with three new subsea cable routes connecting India to Singapore, South Africa, and Australia, and four strategic fibre-optic land routes. This large-scale infrastructure investment is designed to increase bandwidth, enhance network redundancy, support AI model training, improve cloud computing capabilities, and promote secure cross-border digital services.

questions.question: Which technology company partnered with Larsen & Toubro (L&T) to build India’s first sovereign gigawatt-scale AI factory infrastructure?
questions.explanation: NVIDIA partnered with Larsen & Toubro (L&T) to develop India’s first sovereign gigawatt-scale AI factory infrastructure. The collaboration focuses on building production-grade AI capacity within the country by expanding high-density GPU clusters and deploying accelerated computing systems. Planned data centre expansions in Chennai and Mumbai will support large-scale AI model training and enterprise-level AI workloads. By integrating NVIDIA’s GPUs, CPUs, networking solutions, and AI Enterprise software with L&T’s engineering expertise, the initiative strengthens India’s digital infrastructure, aligns with the IndiaAI Mission, and promotes secure, scalable, and domestically anchored AI development.

questions.question: From which year will the Unique Transaction Identifier (UTI) become mandatory for all OTC derivatives trades as per the RBI’s new regulation?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India has mandated that a Unique Transaction Identifier (UTI) must be used for all over-the-counter (OTC) derivatives transactions beginning January 1, 2027. This requirement applies to rupee interest rate derivatives, foreign currency derivatives, credit derivatives, and certain government securities contracts. The UTI, a globally recognised tracking code, will uniquely identify each transaction, reduce duplication in reporting, and improve transparency. By introducing this mandate, the RBI aims to strengthen regulatory oversight, enhance systemic risk monitoring, improve data accuracy, and align India’s OTC derivatives reporting framework with global financial market standards.

questions.question: Which private Indian satellite operator signed a landmark data reseller partnership with NSIL to globally market its SyncFused OptoSAR imagery solutions?
questions.explanation: GalaxEye entered into a significant data reseller partnership with NewSpace India Limited (NSIL), becoming the first private Indian satellite operator to formalise such collaboration. Under this agreement, NSIL will globally resell GalaxEye’s satellite imagery portfolio, particularly its advanced SyncFused OptoSAR technology, which integrates optical imagery with Synthetic Aperture Radar data. This enables high-precision, all-weather, day-and-night Earth observation. The partnership strengthens India’s New Space economy by combining public-sector commercialisation channels with private innovation, enhancing global access to Indian geospatial intelligence solutions across agriculture, disaster management, infrastructure planning, and environmental monitoring sectors.

questions.question: Which country partnered with India to launch the Offshore Wind Taskforce under Vision 2035 to strengthen clean energy cooperation?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom partnered with India to establish the Offshore Wind Taskforce under Vision 2035 during the Fourth India-UK Energy Dialogue. The initiative aims to accelerate offshore wind development in India by combining UK expertise in scaling offshore wind farms and supply chains with India’s expanding renewable energy market. The task force focuses on ecosystem planning, infrastructure development, financing models, and risk mitigation strategies. It also supports offshore wind projects in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with Viability Gap Funding assistance, while aligning with India’s National Green Hydrogen Mission to enhance clean energy capacity and long-term energy security.

questions.question: When is World Day of Social Justice observed annually across the world?
questions.explanation: February 20 is observed annually as World Day of Social Justice, following a declaration by the United Nations General Assembly in 2007. The day highlights the importance of poverty eradication, decent work, full employment, and social inclusion as essential pillars of sustainable development. In 2026, the theme focuses on renewed commitment in the post-Doha context, encouraging countries to translate political declarations into measurable policy actions. The observance also emphasizes addressing global challenges such as income inequality, informal labour markets, gender disparities, youth unemployment, and the digital divide to promote inclusive growth and long-term global stability.

questions.question: Which country formally joined the US-led Pax Silica partnership to strengthen AI and semiconductor supply chains?
questions.explanation: India formally joined the US-led Pax Silica coalition to enhance cooperation in artificial intelligence infrastructure, semiconductor manufacturing, and critical mineral supply chains. The partnership aims to build a secure and resilient “full silicon stack” ecosystem by reducing dependency on unreliable sources and promoting trusted industrial collaboration. With ongoing development of semiconductor fabrication facilities and advanced chip design capabilities, India’s participation strengthens global supply chain resilience. The initiative also deepens India-US strategic alignment in emerging technologies, supporting innovation-driven growth, economic security, and long-term competitiveness in advanced manufacturing and AI sectors.

questions.question: Which state cabinet approved a 3% reservation in Class I and II government jobs for tea tribes and Adivasi communities within the OBC quota?
questions.explanation: Assam approved a 3% reservation for tea tribes and Adivasi communities in Class I and Class II government jobs within the OBC quota. This decision expands reservation benefits beyond Grade III and IV posts to higher administrative and academic positions, including state civil services and professorships. The move aims to improve representation and social mobility for historically marginalized tea tribe and Adivasi populations who have significantly contributed to the state’s tea economy. Additionally, the cabinet expanded the Mukhyamantri Mahila Udyamita Abhiyaan (MMUA) scheme to include over one lakh more women beneficiaries, strengthening women’s entrepreneurship and rural economic empowerment.

questions.question: Which northeastern state celebrates its Foundation Day on 20 February to mark becoming the 23rd state of India in 1987?
questions.explanation: Mizoram celebrates its Foundation Day on 20 February to commemorate its attainment of full statehood in 1987, becoming the 23rd state of India. The milestone followed the signing of the historic Mizoram Accord in 1986, which brought lasting peace after years of insurgency and political unrest. Prior to statehood, the region functioned as a Union Territory from 1972. The day symbolises reconciliation, unity, and cultural pride among the Mizo people. Celebrations include official ceremonies, cultural performances such as the Cheraw (Bamboo Dance), and reflections on the state’s progress in education, infrastructure, and social development.

questions.question: Which state secured India’s largest ₹8,175 crore lithium-ion battery Gigafactory by Waaree Energies?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh secured India’s largest integrated lithium-ion battery Gigafactory with an investment of ₹8,175 crore by Waaree Energies. The 16 GWh facility will be established at Rambilli in Anakapalli district and is expected to generate around 3,000 direct jobs. The project strengthens India’s clean-energy manufacturing ecosystem by supporting renewable energy storage, electric vehicle expansion, and grid stability. It also reduces dependence on imported battery cells and promotes self-reliance in advanced energy storage technologies, aligning with broader national goals such as Make in India and the transition toward sustainable energy solutions.

questions.question: Which country is partnering with India to establish the National Centre of Excellence in Aeronautics and Defence at Kanpur under the PM-SETU scheme?
questions.explanation: France is collaborating with India to establish the National Centre of Excellence in Aeronautics and Defence at Kanpur under the PM-SETU scheme. The initiative was announced during the India–France Year of Innovation, reflecting deepening bilateral cooperation in advanced technologies, defence manufacturing, and skill development. The Centre will focus on aeronautics, maintenance and overhaul services, airport operations, and space-related training. This partnership builds upon the India–France Strategic Partnership and follows a 2025 Memorandum of Understanding in vocational education and training, aiming to create globally competitive aviation professionals aligned with international standards.

questions.question: Who became the youngest keynote speaker at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 held in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Ranvir Sachdeva, an eight-year-old tech enthusiast, emerged as the youngest keynote speaker at the India AI Impact Summit 2026. He impressed global CEOs, policymakers, and AI experts by confidently explaining complex artificial intelligence concepts in simple terms. Having started coding at the age of three, he has studied machine learning models and spoken at international forums. During the summit, he also interacted with Google CEO Sundar Pichai and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. His emphasis on inclusive and ethical AI, guided by Indian values, highlighted India’s growing role in shaping responsible global AI development.

questions.question: Which company signed an MoU with the Gujarat government to conduct a feasibility study for a 250 MW green AI-ready data centre at Dholera SIR?
questions.explanation: L&T Vyoma signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Gujarat government during the India AI Impact Summit 2026 to study the feasibility of establishing a 250 MW green AI-ready data centre at Dholera Special Investment Region. The proposed ₹25,000 crore project aims to strengthen AI infrastructure, cloud computing capabilities, and sustainable digital ecosystems in the state. The study will evaluate land suitability, infrastructure readiness, availability zones, and sustainability aspects before final execution. The initiative aligns with Gujarat’s IT/ITeS Policy and India’s broader digital transformation and renewable energy goals, targeting operational readiness by 2028.

questions.question: Who became the first woman technocrat to be appointed as Director (Commercial) of Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL)?
questions.explanation: Harsharan Kaur Trehan was appointed as Director (Commercial) of Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL), becoming the first woman technocrat to reach board level in the organisation. Her appointment marks a significant milestone in gender representation within India’s public sector utilities. With a career spanning over three decades since joining the erstwhile Punjab State Electricity Board in 1987, she has handled key roles in transmission, substation, hydel, and thermal operations. She also previously served as the first woman Engineer-in-Chief in PSPCL, contributing extensively to regulatory reforms and large-scale procurement management.

questions.question: When is International Mother Language Day observed every year to promote linguistic and cultural diversity worldwide?
questions.explanation: International Mother Language Day is observed annually on February 21 to promote multilingualism, cultural diversity, and the preservation of endangered languages. Proclaimed by UNESCO in 1999 and later adopted by the UN General Assembly, the day highlights the importance of mother-tongue education in improving learning outcomes and fostering inclusion. It also commemorates the Bengali Language Movement of 1952. The observance supports Sustainable Development Goal 4 on Quality Education and aligns with global efforts such as the International Decade of Indigenous Languages (2022–2032) to protect linguistic heritage worldwide.

questions.question: What was India’s core sector growth rate in January 2026 according to the Index of Core Industries (ICI)?
questions.explanation: India’s eight core industries recorded a growth rate of 4% in January 2026, reflecting a moderation compared to the revised 4.7% growth in December 2025. The Index of Core Industries, which tracks coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement, and electricity, serves as a key indicator of industrial momentum. While sectors like steel and cement continued to show strong performance due to sustained infrastructure and construction activities, others experienced slower growth or contraction. Despite the slowdown in core sectors, overall industrial production remained strong during the period.

questions.question: Which state cabinet approved the construction of a new international airport at Sonepur with a 4.2 km runway capable of handling Airbus A380 aircraft?
questions.explanation: Bihar approved the construction of a major international airport at Sonepur in Saran district as part of a landmark infrastructure initiative. The project includes a 4,200-metre runway designed to handle wide-body aircraft such as the Airbus A380, enabling direct long-haul international flights. Spread over 4,228 acres, the airport aims to enhance connectivity in north Bihar and reduce congestion at Patna’s existing airport. With a land acquisition allocation of ₹1,302 crore and a completion target of 2030, the project is expected to boost trade, tourism, employment, and regional economic development significantly.

questions.question: Which Indian city will host Prince William’s Earthshot Prize 2026 during Climate Week?
questions.explanation: Mumbai has been selected to host The Earthshot Prize 2026, a prestigious global environmental award founded by Prince William. The announcement was made during the city’s first Mumbai Climate Week, highlighting India’s expanding leadership in climate action and sustainable innovation. The Earthshot Prize recognizes five global innovators annually, awarding each £1 million to scale transformative solutions addressing climate change, biodiversity, air pollution, ocean restoration, and waste reduction. Hosting the event underscores Mumbai’s growing role as a hub for climate dialogue and green investment, while reinforcing India’s commitment to achieving long-term environmental and sustainability goals.

questions.question: Which institution hosted the Indo-French Centre for AI in Health (IF-CAIH) inaugurated during the RUSH 2026 meetings?
questions.explanation: AIIMS Delhi hosted the Indo-French Centre for AI in Health (IF-CAIH), inaugurated jointly by India and France during the RUSH 2026 meetings. The centre serves as a dedicated hub for advancing artificial intelligence applications in healthcare, brain health, neuroscience, and clinical decision-support systems. It reflects a strong collaboration between leading Indian and French institutions to promote interdisciplinary research, academic exchange, and innovation in digital health. The initiative strengthens sovereign AI capabilities, supports responsible health data usage, and enhances global cooperation in medical research, education, and technology-driven healthcare solutions.

questions.question: Which United Nations agency signed a five-year MoU with the Food Corporation of India (FCI) for the annual supply of 2 lakh metric tonnes of rice for humanitarian operations?
questions.explanation: The World Food Programme entered into a five-year Memorandum of Understanding with the Food Corporation of India to receive 200,000 metric tonnes of rice annually for global humanitarian operations. As the United Nations’ leading food assistance agency, it works to combat hunger and provide emergency relief in vulnerable regions. The agreement strengthens global food security efforts and supports Sustainable Development Goal 2, which aims to achieve Zero Hunger. This collaboration enhances supply stability for humanitarian missions while reinforcing international cooperation in addressing malnutrition and food insecurity worldwide.

questions.question: Which company announced a ₹10 trillion investment to build India’s sovereign AI compute infrastructure over the next seven years?
questions.explanation: Reliance announced a ₹10 trillion investment in artificial intelligence to develop sovereign compute infrastructure across India. The plan includes building multi-gigawatt AI data centres, deploying edge-compute layers integrated with telecom networks, and delivering affordable AI services nationwide. A major facility is under development in Jamnagar, with phased capacity expansion planned. The initiative emphasises reducing dependence on foreign cloud providers and ensuring national data security. Supported by green energy projects, the investment aligns with long-term goals of strategic resilience, digital self-reliance, and scalable AI adoption across industries and government services.

questions.question: Who was formally reappointed as Japan’s Prime Minister after securing a two-thirds majority in the February 8 snap election?
questions.explanation: Sanae Takaichi was formally reappointed as Japan’s Prime Minister after achieving a historic two-thirds majority in the snap lower house elections. Her victory provided a strong political mandate to advance constitutional reforms, strengthen national defence, and expand intelligence capabilities. As Japan’s first woman Prime Minister, she has emphasized proactive fiscal policies, suspension of food consumption tax to manage inflation, and the creation of a National Intelligence Agency. Takaichi has also proposed updates to the Free and Open Indo-Pacific strategy, reflecting Japan’s evolving security posture amid rising geopolitical tensions in East Asia.

questions.question: Which date is observed as World Peace and Understanding Day 2026, commemorating the founding of Rotary in Chicago?
questions.explanation: World Peace and Understanding Day 2026 is observed on February 23, commemorating the first Rotary meeting held in Chicago in 1905 by Paul P. Harris. This historic event marked the foundation of Rotary International, which later grew into one of the world’s largest humanitarian service networks operating in over 200 countries and regions. Celebrated during World Understanding Month, the day promotes ethical leadership, international cooperation, peacebuilding, and service above self. It emphasises dialogue, empathy, and collaborative action to address global challenges such as poverty, disease, inequality, and conflict, thereby fostering sustainable harmony worldwide.

questions.question: Which state has been officially declared Naxal-free after the surrender of the last armed Maoist in Munger?
questions.explanation: Bihar has been officially declared Naxal-free following the surrender of the last armed Maoist, Suresh Koda, in Munger. This marks the end of a long-standing insurgency that began in the 1970s and once severely affected the state, with as many as 23 districts impacted in 2012. Sustained security operations, development initiatives, and strong rehabilitation policies played a significant role in restoring normalcy. Authorities reported 220 arrests and zero Naxal-related incidents during 2025, reflecting the effectiveness of coordinated efforts to eliminate extremist activities and promote peace and stability across the state.

questions.question: Which state will host the 16th edition of the India-US Exercise Vajra Prahar 2026?
questions.explanation: The 16th edition of Exercise Vajra Prahar 2026 will be conducted in Himachal Pradesh at the Special Forces Training School in Bakloh. This joint military exercise between India and the United States aims to enhance defence cooperation, interoperability, and counter-terrorism capabilities. The drill includes close-quarter battle exercises, urban warfare simulations, high-altitude training, and realistic combat scenarios. Conducted alternately in both countries, Vajra Prahar reflects the deepening strategic partnership between the two democracies. Such exercises strengthen mutual trust, improve joint operational readiness, and reinforce shared commitments toward regional stability and security cooperation.

questions.question: Which date is observed as World Pangolin Day 2026?
questions.explanation: World Pangolin Day 2026 is observed on February 21, which falls on the third Saturday of February. This international observance highlights the importance of protecting pangolins, unique mammals belonging to the order Pholidota and commonly known as scaly anteaters. Pangolins are among the most trafficked mammals in the world due to illegal trade in their scales and meat. Reports have identified 74 nations and 178 distinct trade routes connected to pangolin trafficking, with major corridors such as Nigeria to Vietnam and Mozambique to Malawi. The day aims to spread awareness, strengthen conservation initiatives, and encourage global cooperation to curb illegal wildlife trade.

questions.question: What were the total forex reserves of India for the week ended February 13, reaching a record high?
questions.explanation: India’s total forex reserves reached a historic high of $725.727 Billion for the week ended February 13. This significant milestone reflects strong macroeconomic stability and effective currency management by the Reserve Bank of India. The surge was primarily driven by an increase in Foreign Currency Assets (FCA), which stood at $573.603 billion, and a substantial rise in gold reserves, which reached $128.466 billion. Additionally, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) contributed to the overall reserves. The record level enhances India’s external sector strength, supports currency stability, and provides a strong buffer against global economic volatility and financial uncertainties.

questions.question: Which state has proposed the Nativity Card Bill to introduce a permanent photo ID for those born in the state or to its ancestors?
questions.explanation: Kerala has proposed the Nativity Card Bill to introduce a permanent photo identity card for individuals born in the state or those with Keralite ancestry. The proposed card aims to serve as authoritative proof of identity for accessing various state services, particularly amid concerns related to citizenship and documentation. The Bill also includes strict penalties for providing false information, including up to three months of imprisonment, a fine of ₹5,000, or both. It is set to be tabled in the State Assembly and is intended to streamline identification processes and strengthen administrative clarity within the state.

questions.question: In which Union Territory was the new 3mm crab species Galathea balasubramaniani discovered?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
questions.explanation: The new 3mm crab species, Galathea balasubramaniani, was discovered in Lakshadweep, specifically in the Agatti reefs. The discovery was made by researchers from the National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources, Kochi. This tiny reef-dwelling squat lobster was named after T. Balasubramanian and plays an important ecological role in recycling organic matter within coral reef ecosystems. Its identification was confirmed through detailed DNA and morphological analysis. The finding underscores the rich yet under-explored marine biodiversity of Lakshadweep and highlights the importance of continued scientific research and conservation efforts in fragile reef habitats.

questions.question: In which state was the 78th Zomi National Day celebrated?
questions.explanation: The 78th Zomi National Day was celebrated in Manipur, specifically in the Churachandpur district. The event highlighted the cultural unity, resilience, and identity of the Zomi community. During the celebration, Deputy Chief Minister Losi Dikho announced the development of a new turf ground funded by ONGC and assured continued support for the Zomi Council. The occasion also emphasised the importance of rehabilitating displaced persons as a crucial measure to restore peace and stability in the region. The celebration reflected the community’s commitment to preserving its heritage while working toward social harmony and progress.

questions.question: Which country has launched landmark pediatric gene therapy projects to treat autism and rare brain disorders?
questions.explanation: The UAE has launched landmark pediatric gene therapy projects through Emirates Health Services to address the root causes of autism and rare neurological disorders. In collaboration with the Children’s Hospital of Philadelphia, the initiative focuses on precision gene delivery techniques for conditions such as BPAN and epilepsy. The projects utilize advanced genetic engineering methods along with the Emirati Genome Program and artificial intelligence to identify biomarkers, monitor disease progression, and enable early intervention. This initiative reflects a major advancement in personalized medicine and demonstrates the country’s commitment to innovative healthcare solutions and cutting-edge biomedical research.

questions.question: In which state did the Union Home Minister lay the foundation stone of the 10th Police Battalion headquarters in Kamrup?
questions.explanation: The foundation stone of the 10th Police Battalion headquarters was laid in Assam, specifically in Kamrup district. The project includes modern infrastructure such as barracks for male and female personnel, residential and operational facilities, a hospital, training center, firing range, surveillance systems, and a sports complex. The development is part of broader efforts to strengthen internal security, curb infiltration, and enhance law and order. Alongside security initiatives, major development plans, including industrial investments and job creation, aim to transform the state into a leading industrial hub in eastern and North-Eastern India.

questions.question: In which city is the Palamu Tiger Reserve located, where the ‘Vanjeevi Didi’ initiative was launched?
questions.explanation: Palamu Tiger Reserve is located in Ranchi, where the ‘Vanjeevi Didi’ initiative was launched to promote forest and wildlife conservation. The programme aims to train educated women from 17 villages as ambassadors for environmental awareness and community engagement. Each selected participant receives ₹3,000 per month and works to spread awareness about conservation while also encouraging school enrollments. The initiative strengthens the connection between local communities and forest authorities, empowering women to actively contribute to sustainable environmental protection and grassroots development efforts.

questions.question: Who became the first Indian woman since 2009, after Sania Mirza, to reach a major ITF Women’s singles final?
questions.explanation: Vaishnavi Adkar became the first Indian woman since Sania Mirza in 2009 to reach a major International Tennis Federation Women’s singles final. She achieved this milestone at the Bengaluru W100 tournament, part of the ITF Women’s World Tennis Tour, which features higher prize money and strong international competition. Although Adkar finished runner-up after losing to Belgium’s Hanne Vandewinkel, her impressive performance helped her break into the WTA Top 500 rankings for the first time, marking a significant achievement in Indian women’s tennis.

questions.question: Which village in Maharashtra has formally declared itself as India’s first caste-free village?
questions.explanation: Soundala village in the Ahilyanagar district of Maharashtra has officially declared itself caste-free by adopting the principle “Amchi jaat manav,” meaning “my caste is humanity.” The decision was unanimously approved by the gram sabha, reflecting collective agreement among the villagers. This historic move positions Soundala as Maharashtra’s and possibly India’s first caste-free village. The initiative represents a progressive step toward eliminating caste-based discrimination and promoting social equality. By prioritising humanity over caste identity, the village sets an inspiring example for broader societal reform and unity across communities.

questions.question: What position did Delhi Airport achieve among the busiest airports in the Asia Pacific region in 2025, according to OAG data?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi International Airport secured the 5th position among the busiest airports in the Asia Pacific region in 2025, based on OAG data measuring one-way departing seat capacity. The airport recorded 46.18 million seats, marking a significant rise from its 9th rank in 2019. This growth reflects India’s expanding domestic aviation market, increasing middle-class travel demand, and improved airport infrastructure. A major contributor to this surge has been IndiGo, whose aggressive route expansion and fleet growth have boosted overall seat capacity and strengthened Delhi’s standing in regional aviation rankings.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the National Highway Quality Monitoring Portal being developed by the government?
options: To track vehicle traffic on National Highways
To digitise the monitoring process with real-time GPS tracking of vans
To register complaints about road accidents
questions.answer: To digitise the monitoring process with real-time GPS tracking of vans
questions.explanation: The National Highway Quality Monitoring Portal is being developed to digitise the quality monitoring process. It will upload test reports generated by the Mobile Quality Control Vans and feature real-time GPS tracking of these vans. This system ensures transparency in inspections, prevents misuse, and allows officials to remotely monitor van movements and quality checks. The goal is to make the entire quality assurance system more accountable and data-driven for maintaining National Highway standards.

questions.question: Which state signed an MoU with Digital Bharat Nidhi for the expedited rollout of the Amended BharatNet Programme?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Digital Bharat Nidhi to accelerate the implementation of the Amended BharatNet Programme. This ambitious project, valued at $16.9 billion (₹1.39 lakh crore), aims to extend Optical Fibre Cable broadband connectivity to every village, strengthening rural digital infrastructure. The agreement ensures state support in providing right of way and infrastructure access to facilitate smooth execution. The initiative is designed to digitally empower rural communities, enhance socio-economic development, and promote digital inclusion by enabling improved connectivity for government institutions, businesses, and citizens across the state.

questions.question: On which date is Central Excise Day observed in India to commemorate the Central Excise Act, 1944?
questions.explanation: Central Excise Day is observed every year on February 24 to mark the enactment of the Central Excise and Salt Act, 1944, later known as the Central Excise Act, 1944. The day recognises the contributions of the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) in administering indirect taxes, including customs and excise duties. It highlights the importance of revenue collection, prevention of smuggling, and tax reforms in strengthening India’s financial system. Even in the GST era, excise duty continues to apply to specific goods such as petroleum and tobacco, maintaining its relevance in the country’s taxation framework.

questions.question: Which Indian naval ship made a historic port call at Safaga, Egypt, under the Lokayan-26 mission?
questions.explanation: INS Sudarshini made a historic port call at Safaga, Egypt, after sailing 1,832 nautical miles from Salalah as part of the Lokayan-26 mission. The voyage demonstrated the crew’s endurance, seamanship, and operational capability across challenging waters, including transit through the Gulf of Aden and the Red Sea. The visit included high-level meetings and community outreach activities, strengthening maritime cooperation and training ties between India and Egypt. The deployment aligns with the Indian Navy’s “Bridges of Friendship” initiative, aimed at enhancing international maritime collaboration and goodwill.

questions.question: Which country signed an MoU with India to enhance cooperation in the postal sector?
questions.explanation: India signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen bilateral cooperation in postal services with Brazil. The agreement focuses on collaboration in areas such as digital transformation, modernisation of postal systems, and improving service delivery. It was signed by India’s Minister Scindia and Brazil’s Minister Frederico de Siqueira Filho, highlighting high-level engagement between the two nations. The MoU aims to promote strategic partnership, encourage knowledge-sharing, and enhance inclusive postal services. This cooperation reflects the growing diplomatic and economic ties between India and Brazil, supporting innovation and efficiency in their postal sectors.

questions.question: Who became the youngest-ever Prime Minister of the Netherlands?
questions.explanation: Rob Jetten became the youngest Prime Minister of the Netherlands at the age of 38. A leader of the Democrats' 66 (D66) party, he assumed office after a closely contested election. His leadership marks a historic milestone in Dutch politics, breaking the previous record held by Ruud Lubbers, who became Prime Minister at 43. Jetten heads a minority coalition government and faces significant challenges, including negotiations on defence spending, asylum policy, healthcare reforms, and economic measures, requiring strong consensus-building and political coordination.

questions.question: In which city will the ‘Rah-Veer’ scheme be implemented to reward Good Samaritans helping road accident victims?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the city where the ‘Rah-Veer’ scheme will be implemented to encourage citizens to assist seriously injured road accident victims. Under this initiative, individuals who ensure victims receive medical care within the crucial golden hour will be awarded ₹25,000 along with a certificate of appreciation. The scheme also provides legal protection under the Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019, addressing fears of police questioning or legal hassles. By combining financial incentives and legal safeguards, the government aims to promote timely intervention, compassion, and reduce fatalities caused by road accidents.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Managing Director (MD) and CEO of Slice Small Finance Bank after receiving RBI approval?
questions.explanation: Rajan Bajaj has been appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Slice Small Finance Bank following approval from the bank’s board, shareholders, and the Reserve Bank of India. He is the founder of Slice, which began as a fintech startup in 2016 and later merged with North East Small Finance Bank in 2024 to become a regulated small finance bank. Under his leadership, the bank aims to strengthen AI-driven digital banking services and promote financial inclusion, particularly for underserved and rural populations across India.

questions.question: What GDP growth rate has ICRA projected for India in Q3 FY26?
questions.explanation: 7.2% is the GDP growth rate projected by ICRA for India in the third quarter of FY26. This estimate reflects a moderation compared to the 8.2% growth recorded in the second quarter. The slowdown has been attributed to weaker performance in services and agriculture, even though the industrial sector showed improvement. Key contributing factors include a high base effect, a significant decline in central government capital expenditure, nearly flat state spending, subdued exports, and reduced services export growth. However, festive demand supported economic activity, helping maintain overall growth above the 7% mark during the quarter.

questions.question: At which institution were Odisha’s first comprehensive Liver Transplant Unit and a Robotic Surgery System inaugurated?
questions.explanation: AIIMS Bhubaneshwar is the institution where Odisha’s first comprehensive Liver Transplant Unit and a Robotic Surgery System were inaugurated by Health Minister JP Nadda through a virtual event. This development marks a significant healthcare milestone for eastern India by enabling advanced organ transplantation and minimally invasive robotic surgeries. The new facilities are expected to greatly benefit patients from Odisha and neighbouring states by providing access to world-class treatment closer to home. The initiative also aligns with the broader vision of promoting organ donation awareness and strengthening healthcare innovation across the region.

questions.question: In which city was IBM’s AI GovTech Innovation Centre inaugurated by Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath?
questions.explanation: Lucknow is the city where IBM’s AI GovTech Innovation Centre was inaugurated by Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath. The centre aims to promote AI-driven governance, enhance transparency, and improve public service delivery across the state. It focuses on emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, deep tech, robotics, drone technology, medical technology, and quantum computing. The initiative includes collaborations with IIT Kanpur and the signing of MoUs with state departments to develop AI-based applications and introduce AI literacy in schools, supporting the vision of developing the city as an “AI City.”

questions.question: Under which initiative has India reintroduced cheetahs, leading to a rise in their population to 38 at Kuno National Park?
questions.explanation: Project Cheetah is the initiative under which India reintroduced cheetahs to restore the species after it was declared extinct in 1952. The programme involved the intercontinental translocation of 20 cheetahs from Namibia and South Africa during 2022–23, marking the world’s first such effort for a large carnivore. Prime Minister Narendra Modi released the first batch at Kuno National Park in September 2022. The recent birth of three cubs to the South African cheetah Gamini has raised India’s cheetah population to 38, reflecting steady progress in wildlife conservation and the long-term goal of establishing a sustainable population.

questions.question: Where was the 8th edition of Advantage Healthcare India (AHCI) 2026 organised?
questions.explanation: Greater Noida hosted the 8th edition of Advantage Healthcare India (AHCI) from 23–25 February 2026. The event was organised by the Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry (FICCI) and was inaugurated by Health Minister JP Nadda. Recognised as Asia’s largest event focused on Medical Value Travel, it featured a comprehensive exhibition, high-level conference sessions, and a Reverse Buyer-Seller Meet. The platform aimed to promote patient mobility, encourage knowledge exchange, and strengthen India’s position as a global hub for affordable and quality healthcare services.

questions.question: Where is the National Arogya Fair 2026 being held, showcasing India’s diverse Ayush systems under one roof?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra is the state where the National Arogya Fair 2026 is being organized in Shegaon from February 25–28. The event is hosted by the Ministry of Ayush and highlights major traditional systems of medicine including Ayurveda, Yoga, Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, Sowa-Rigpa, and Homoeopathy at a single venue. It also features free health check-ups, medical consultations, live demonstrations, exhibitions, and special sessions for farmers focusing on agroforestry and the cultivation of medicinal plants, making it a significant public health and awareness initiative.

questions.question: Where did the Indian Army successfully conduct the Strela-10 VSHORADS test firing exercise?
questions.explanation: Pokhran was the location where the Indian Army’s Konark Corps carried out the successful test firing of the Strela-10 Very Short Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS). The exercise took place at the Pokhran Field Firing Range in Rajasthan’s desert sector. The missile system, launched from a BMP-based mobile platform, achieved direct hits on simulated low-altitude aerial targets such as aircraft, helicopters, and drones. The drill demonstrated operational readiness, effective surveillance integration, strong crew coordination, and accurate engagement under challenging desert conditions, reinforcing India’s air defence preparedness in strategically sensitive regions.

questions.question: Where did the Ministry of MSME organise the National SC-ST Hub Mega Awareness Programme to promote entrepreneurship among SC-ST communities?
questions.explanation: Itanagar was the venue for the National SC-ST Hub Mega Awareness Programme organised by the Ministry of MSME. The programme aimed to promote entrepreneurship among SC and ST communities and to spread awareness about various central government schemes available for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. Entrepreneurs were also provided the opportunity to obtain Udyam Registration during the event. Additionally, the Entrepreneurship Development Institute (EDI) at Jote, constructed with assistance from the Ministry of MSME, was inaugurated, further strengthening support infrastructure for aspiring and existing entrepreneurs in the region.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the ‘Blockchain India Challenge’ launched by MeitY?
questions.explanation: To Boost Digital Governance is the central objective behind the launch of the ‘Blockchain India Challenge’ by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. The initiative encourages Indian startups to build secure, permissioned blockchain solutions tailored for government use cases. It focuses on enhancing regulatory control, transparency, auditability, and tamper-proof record management across sectors such as healthcare, agriculture, education, land records, and public distribution systems. Implemented with support from C-DAC, the programme provides stage-wise funding to selected startups, aiming to create scalable, field-ready solutions that strengthen efficiency and trust in public service delivery systems across India.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution can Parliament alter the name, area, or boundaries of a State, as in the proposal to rename Kerala as “Keralam”?
questions.explanation: Article 3 of the Constitution empowers Parliament to alter the name, area, or boundaries of a State by law. In the case of renaming Kerala as “Keralam,” the constitutional procedure requires the President to refer the Bill to the concerned State Legislature for expressing its views. After receiving those views, the Central Government may proceed to introduce the Bill in Parliament with the President’s recommendation. The proposal also involves amending the First Schedule of the Constitution, where the names of States are officially listed, ensuring that any change follows due constitutional process.

questions.question: In which city will the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change organise ‘Him-CONNECT’ during WSDS 2026?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the host city for ‘Him-CONNECT’, which will be organised by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change on the sidelines of the World Sustainable Development Summit 2026. The platform aims to connect researchers from the Indian Himalayan Region with startups, investors, and policymakers to scale climate-resilient innovations. More than 24 technologies developed under the National Mission on Himalayan Studies will be showcased, focusing on water security, renewable energy, waste-to-wealth solutions, sustainable building, and nature-based livelihoods, reinforcing India’s commitment to sustainable mountain development and climate action.

questions.question: What Minimum Support Price (MSP) has been approved for Raw Jute (TD-3 grade) for the 2026-27 marketing season?
questions.explanation: ₹5,925 is the Minimum Support Price approved for Raw Jute (TD-3 grade) for the 2026-27 marketing season. The decision was taken by the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs to ensure better remuneration for jute farmers. This price guarantees a return of 61.8% over the all-India weighted average cost of production, in line with the government’s commitment to fix MSP at least 1.5 times the production cost. The revised MSP represents an increase over the previous season and aims to promote jute cultivation, strengthen rural incomes, and support the overall jute industry supply chain.

questions.question: Which country conferred the Speaker of the Knesset Medal, its highest parliamentary honour, upon Prime Minister Narendra Modi?
questions.explanation: Israel is the nation that awarded Prime Minister Narendra Modi the Speaker of the Knesset Medal in Jerusalem, marking the first time this highest parliamentary honour was presented to a global leader. The recognition highlights his contribution to strengthening strategic relations, expanding cooperation in defence, agriculture, water management, cybersecurity, and innovation, and advancing negotiations on a Free Trade Agreement. The award symbolizes deep mutual trust and growing diplomatic engagement, reflecting the steady expansion of bilateral ties since full diplomatic relations were established in 1992.

questions.question: Which country signed a Statement of Intent with India to launch the Technology and Artificial Intelligence Corridor (SITAC)?
questions.explanation: Sweden is the country that partnered with India by signing a Statement of Intent during the India AI Impact Summit 2026 to strengthen cooperation in Artificial Intelligence and digital technologies. The agreement led to the launch of the Sweden–India Technology and Artificial Intelligence Corridor (SITAC), a structured framework aimed at promoting collaboration among governments, industries, startups, and research institutions. The partnership focuses on developing, deploying, and scaling AI solutions across sectors while encouraging trade, investment, innovation, and responsible AI practices aligned with sustainable development goals.

questions.question: Which railway zone has launched the next-generation ‘Rail Parcel App’ offering doorstep freight booking and real-time tracking?
questions.explanation: South Central Railway is the zone that launched the next-generation Rail Parcel App to modernise parcel booking and freight logistics. The app functions as a digital freight e-marketplace, enabling customers to book shipments online with features such as doorstep pickup and delivery, real-time tracking, automated notifications, and secure digital payments. Initially rolled out as a pilot project in seven cities, the initiative supports the broader national goal of achieving 3,000 million tonnes of cargo loading by 2030. It also aligns with Indian Railways’ digital transformation strategy to improve efficiency, transparency, and customer convenience in freight services.

questions.question: Which organisation is responsible for overseeing and implementing India’s first comprehensive Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS)?
options: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
questions.explanation: The Bureau of Energy Efficiency is the nodal agency responsible for overseeing and implementing India’s first compliance-based Carbon Credit Trading Scheme. The programme aims to regulate and monitor industrial emissions by assigning emission targets to nearly 490 industrial units across seven sectors for the 2025–26 period. Units emitting below prescribed limits can earn carbon credits, while those exceeding limits must purchase credits or face penalties. The initiative is designed to promote energy efficiency, encourage adoption of cleaner technologies, enhance transparency through verification processes, and strengthen India’s commitment to climate action and sustainable industrial growth.

questions.question: In which state is the 7th edition of the India–Japan Joint Military Exercise ‘Dharma Guardian’ being conducted?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand is the state where the 7th edition of the India–Japan Joint Military Exercise ‘Dharma Guardian’ is being held at the Foreign Training Node in Chaubattia. The exercise involves participation from the Indian Army and the Japan Ground Self-Defence Force, with 120 personnel from each side. The drill focuses on enhancing interoperability, strengthening military collaboration, and improving joint operational capabilities in semi-urban environments. Activities such as heliborne operations, cordon and search missions, intelligence grid development, and house intervention drills are conducted to ensure combat readiness and coordinated response to regional security challenges.

questions.question: In which state is the 16th edition of the India–US Joint Special Forces Exercise ‘Vajra Prahar’ being conducted?
questions.explanation: Himachal Pradesh is the host state for the 16th edition of the India–US Joint Special Forces Exercise ‘Vajra Prahar’, being held at the Special Forces Training School in Bakloh from 24 February to 16 March 2026. The exercise involves personnel from the Indian Army’s Special Forces and the United States Army’s Green Berets. It focuses on enhancing interoperability, strengthening joint operational capabilities, and exchanging expertise in special operations tactics. Special emphasis is placed on conducting coordinated missions in mountainous terrain, improving combat readiness and reinforcing the growing defence partnership between the two nations.

questions.question: Which state government launched the Banglar Yuva Sathi Scheme 2026 to provide monthly financial assistance to unemployed youth?
questions.explanation: West Bengal launched the Banglar Yuva Sathi Scheme 2026 to provide ₹1,500 per month to eligible unemployed youth aged 21–40 years through Direct Benefit Transfer into Aadhaar-linked bank accounts. The scheme offers support for up to five years or until beneficiaries secure employment or self-employment. Announced during the interim budget for 2026–27 and administered by the Department of Sports and Youth Services, it aims to provide interim financial relief amid rising joblessness. With a significant budget allocation and large-scale registrations, the initiative targets lakhs of beneficiaries across the state.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Chief People Officer of OpenAI to lead its HR functions?
questions.explanation: OpenAI appointed Arvind KC as its new Chief People Officer to oversee key human resource functions such as hiring, onboarding, and employee development. His appointment comes at a time when the organisation is experiencing rapid global expansion following the widespread success of ChatGPT and other AI products. Arvind KC brings significant experience from senior leadership roles at major technology companies, including Roblox, Google, Palantir Technologies, and Meta Platforms. His expertise in scaling high-growth technology firms and managing large workforces positions him to support OpenAI’s expanding operations and workforce development strategies.

questions.question: Which state launched the Homestay (Paying Guest House) Scheme 2026 to promote rural tourism and self-employment?
questions.explanation: The Rajasthan Homestay (Paying Guest House) Scheme 2026 was introduced to decentralise tourism and stimulate rural economic growth. The initiative encourages self-employment, particularly among women and small investors, while offering tourists authentic local cultural experiences. The scheme features a fully digital and simplified registration process aligned with Ease of Doing Business and Ease of Living priorities. It increases accommodation capacity, removes the mandatory owner-residence requirement, and provides faster approvals, including deemed approval provisions. With silver and gold registration categories and statewide implementation, the scheme broadens participation and strengthens Rajasthan’s rural tourism infrastructure.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the nationwide HPV vaccination campaign launched in February 2026?
questions.explanation: To prevent cervical cancer is the primary objective of the nationwide HPV vaccination campaign launched by the Government of India. The initiative targets 14-year-old girls and provides a free single dose of the Gardasil 4 vaccine, which protects against high-risk HPV types 16 and 18 responsible for most cervical cancer cases, as well as types 6 and 11. Cervical cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths among women in India. By focusing on early immunization, the campaign emphasizes preventive healthcare, aiming to significantly reduce future cancer incidence and mortality rates across the country.

questions.question: When is International Polar Bear Day observed every year to raise awareness about polar bear conservation?
questions.explanation: February 27 is observed annually as International Polar Bear Day to highlight the urgent need for polar bear conservation and climate action. The date coincides with the critical denning period when polar bear mothers and their newborn cubs remain sheltered in snow dens, making them highly vulnerable. Organised by Polar Bears International, the day focuses on raising awareness about climate change, shrinking Arctic sea ice, and habitat loss. It promotes research, education, and policy advocacy aimed at protecting polar bear populations and ensuring the survival of future generations in the rapidly warming Arctic region.

questions.question: Which institute signed an MoU with NMDC’s R&D Centre to advance mineral processing and green technologies?
questions.explanation: IIT Hyderabad entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with NMDC’s R&D Centre to promote long-term academic and industry collaboration in mineral and metals research. The partnership integrates NMDC’s practical field expertise with the institute’s academic strength and technological innovation. The collaboration focuses on critical areas such as iron ore beneficiation, green steelmaking technologies, alternate ironmaking processes using indigenous raw materials, and advanced modeling and simulation in mining and metallurgy. This initiative aims to support India’s mineral security, encourage sustainable industrial practices, and enhance self-reliance in strategic materials through research-driven solutions.

questions.question: After whom will Pakyong Airport in Sikkim be renamed to honour his contribution to India’s freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Trilochan Pokhrel is being honoured through the renaming of Pakyong Airport in Sikkim in recognition of his significant role in India’s freedom movement. Popularly known as “Gandhi Pokhrel,” he is regarded as Sikkim’s first independence activist and actively participated in major national movements such as the Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement, and Quit India Movement. Inspired by Mahatma Gandhi, he worked to spread nationalist ideas within Sikkim. The renaming symbolises the state’s effort to preserve its historical legacy and acknowledge local heroes who contributed to India’s independence.

questions.question: Which Argentine player won his maiden ATP 500 title at the Rio Open by defeating Alejandro Tabilo in the final?
questions.explanation: Tomas Martin Etcheverry secured his first ATP Tour title and the biggest victory of his career at the Rio Open ATP 500 event in Brazil. The 26-year-old Argentine defeated Alejandro Tabilo in a hard-fought three-set final, demonstrating resilience after overcoming a rain-delayed schedule and winning two crucial matches on the same day. This triumph marked a breakthrough moment following three previous runner-up finishes. The victory significantly improved his global ranking, pushing him up 18 places to World No. 33 and returning him to the Top 40, strengthening his position ahead of the clay-court season.

questions.question: Which location hosted Exercise Kalari Leap led by Southern Air Command?
questions.explanation: Exercise Kalari Leap was conducted as a high-impact joint maritime operation led by the Southern Air Command in coordination with the Armed Forces Special Operations Division and the Indian Coast Guard. The exercise took place in the Lakshadweep and Minicoy islands, focusing on rapid force projection, precision strikes, and multi-domain coordination in a challenging maritime environment. It aimed to validate operational readiness, enhance interoperability among forces, and strengthen India’s maritime security posture. The operation highlighted the importance of jointness, technological integration, and swift response capabilities in strategically significant island territories.

questions.question: Which company launched an AI-powered natural language search feature built on Microsoft Foundry in February 2026?
questions.explanation: PhonePe introduced an AI-powered natural language search feature on 20 February 2026, leveraging Microsoft Foundry to enhance user experience within its application. The feature allows users to perform tasks such as payments, recharges, navigation, and transaction tracking through simple voice or text-based commands. By using intent-based queries, the platform transitions from a purely transactional interface to a more intelligent and personalised system. Built on a hybrid on-device and cloud model, the solution ensures data security while improving accessibility, convenience, and discoverability for millions of users across India through a phased rollout.

questions.question: Which company secured a ₹36 crore order to supply fabricated steel products for a BHEL project?
questions.explanation: BMW Ventures Limited secured a ₹36 crore contract to supply fabricated steel products for a major project executed by Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL). The order signifies the company’s strategic expansion into large-scale infrastructure and power sector projects, moving beyond its traditional operational scope. The contract includes the supply of structural steel fabrication and related critical components essential for thermal or industrial infrastructure initiatives. The execution timeline is estimated at 12–18 months, enhancing the company’s order book strength, revenue visibility, and positioning within India’s rapidly growing infrastructure and energy sector.

questions.question: Which vessel was commissioned as the fourth ship of the Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC) project into the Indian Navy in February 2026?
questions.explanation: INS Anjadip was commissioned into the Indian Navy on 27 February 2026 at Chennai Port as the fourth ship under the Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft project. The ceremony was presided over by the Chief of the Naval Staff, marking a major boost to India’s littoral and coastal defence capabilities. The 77-meter vessel, with a displacement of 1,400 tons, is specially designed for shallow-water anti-submarine operations. With over 80% indigenous content, including defence electronics and weapon systems, it highlights India’s strong commitment to self-reliance in defence manufacturing and maritime security preparedness.

questions.question: Which country launched the ‘Zero Prize’, the first-ever results-based environmental award aimed at reducing pollution?
questions.explanation: India launched the ‘Zero Prize’ as the country’s first results-based environmental award focused on achieving measurable reductions in air, water, and land pollution. The initiative carries a total corpus of ₹5 crore, with ₹1 crore allocated to each of the three categories. It emphasises verified and quantifiable outcomes through third-party audits and scientific monitoring rather than mere declarations or planned actions. The award aligns with broader national initiatives such as Mission LiFE, Swachh Bharat, and net-zero ambitions, promoting accountability and outcome-driven environmental governance across multiple sectors.

questions.question: Which country’s central bank launched the world’s first sovereign financial cloud platform called “Financial Cloud”?
questions.explanation: The UAE launched the world’s first sovereign financial cloud platform through its Central Bank, marking a significant milestone in digital financial infrastructure. The platform, named Financial Cloud, is a fully regulated and secure cloud environment exclusively designed for licensed financial institutions within the country. It ensures strict compliance with data sovereignty, cybersecurity, and regulatory standards while allowing institutions to host critical workloads and applications. The initiative supports the nation’s broader vision of becoming a global fintech and digital economy hub under UAE Vision 2031 and the Dubai Economic Agenda D33, reducing dependence on global hyperscalers.

questions.question: Which Indian airport signed a cargo partnership MoU with Frankfurt Airport to enhance air cargo connectivity between Europe and India?
questions.explanation: Kempegowda International Airport entered into a strategic cargo partnership with Frankfurt Airport to strengthen air cargo connectivity between Europe and India. The Memorandum of Understanding was signed on 25 February 2026 on the sidelines of Air Cargo India 2026 in Mumbai. The collaboration focuses on digitalisation, regulatory optimisation, pharma cargo integrity standards, joint trade lane analytics, and knowledge exchange. This partnership aims to create a more integrated and efficient cargo corridor, reduce dwell times, enhance predictability for airlines and freight forwarders, and support the growing trade volumes between the two regions.

questions.question: Which country did the Indian delegation visit to advance cooperation on transboundary rivers and hydropower projects in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Bhutan was the country visited by an Indian delegation led by the Secretary of the Department of Water Resources, River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation to strengthen cooperation on trans-boundary river management and hydropower projects. The discussions included reviewing ongoing joint initiatives, enhancing hydro-meteorological data sharing, improving flood forecasting systems, and addressing climate-related challenges such as glacial lake outburst floods and extreme weather events. The engagement reinforced the long-standing hydropower partnership between the two nations, which dates back to 1961 and was further strengthened through a bilateral agreement signed in 2006, emphasising sustainable and mutually beneficial resource management.

questions.question: From which date has the Government of India mandated the nationwide sale of E20 petrol with a minimum RON 95 at all retail outlets?
questions.explanation: 1 April 2026 marks the official nationwide rollout of E20 petrol with a minimum Research Octane Number (RON) of 95 across all petrol pumps in India. The mandate requires every retail outlet, including public sector units and private oil marketing companies, to replace regular E10 petrol with E20 as the standard grade. This move is a major milestone under the Ethanol Blending Programme aimed at reducing crude oil imports, lowering carbon emissions, supporting farmers through higher ethanol demand, and enhancing engine performance with higher-octane fuel. Higher ethanol blends and special fuels remain outside this mandate.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer (MD & CEO) of Hindustan Oil Exploration Company Limited (HOEC) effective 27 February 2026?
questions.explanation: Baroruchi Mishra was appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Hindustan Oil Exploration Company Limited (HOEC), effective 27 February 2026. He succeeded Ramasamy Jeevanandam, who had served in the role since 2019 and stepped down as part of the leadership transition. Mishra brings significant expertise in upstream oil and gas operations, with extensive experience in exploration, production, and business development across Indian and multinational energy firms. His appointment marks an important strategic development for HOEC as it continues to strengthen its position in the independent upstream energy sector.

questions.question: What was India’s global rank among 84 countries in mental health and well-being for young adults (18–34 years) according to the Global Mind Health 2025 report?
questions.explanation: 60th rank was secured by India among 84 countries in the Global Mind Health 2025 assessment focusing on young adults aged 18–34 years. The ranking places the country in the lower-middle tier globally, highlighting significant mental health challenges such as stress, anxiety, depression, and loneliness among youth. The report, prepared by the Sapien Labs Centre for the Future of Mental Health, is based on responses from over one million adults using the Mental Health Quotient (MHQ) tool. It emphasises socioeconomic pressures, cultural stigma, limited access to care, and modern lifestyle factors influencing declining mental well-being among younger generations.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched ‘Operation Crackdown’ to dismantle organised cybercrime networks?
questions.explanation: Telangana initiated ‘Operation Crackdown’ as a statewide, multi-agency drive to aggressively target and dismantle organised cybercrime networks. The campaign focuses on offences such as call centre fraud, digital arrest scams, investment fraud, sextortion, and job fraud. Led by the Telangana Police Cyber Security Bureau in coordination with local police, CID, SIT, and other specialised units, the operation involves coordinated raids, arrests, asset seizures, and intelligence-based actions across districts. The initiative aims to disrupt the entire cybercrime ecosystem, from front-end fraudsters to financial handlers, reinforcing a strict zero-tolerance approach toward cyber offenders.

questions.question: Which country imposed a temporary ban on poultry meat and table egg imports from India and 39 other nations as a precaution against avian influenza?
questions.explanation: Saudi Arabia imposed a temporary ban on the import of poultry meat and table eggs from 40 countries, including India, as a preventive measure to safeguard public health. The decision was announced by the Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA) following global risk assessments and reported outbreaks of highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) and Newcastle disease. The restriction excludes heat-treated or fully processed poultry products that meet SFDA certification standards.
