Additionally, partial restrictions were placed on specific regions in 16 other countries, with authorities committing to periodic reviews and updates based on evolving disease situations worldwide.

questions.question: Which digital payments company launched an AI-powered natural language search feature built on Microsoft Foundry?
questions.explanation: PhonePe introduced an AI-powered natural language search feature built on Microsoft Foundry, transforming how users interact with the app. The feature enables customers to make payments, recharge FASTag, check gold prices, and track transactions using simple voice or text commands. Designed with a hybrid AI model combining on-device and cloud inferencing, it ensures strong privacy safeguards and secure data handling. The conversational interface replaces traditional menu-based navigation with intent-driven routing, making digital payments more intuitive and personalised. With millions of users and merchants across India, this innovation significantly enhances convenience and accessibility in fintech services.

questions.question: Which state government announced the launch of CM Shri Schools on the lines of the PM Shri Schools initiative?
questions.explanation: Haryana has announced the launch of CM Shri Schools, modelled on the central government’s PM Shri Schools initiative to enhance the quality of state-run education. The new schools will follow the CBSE curriculum pattern, ensuring structured academic standards, skill-based learning, and improved preparation for competitive examinations. The initiative also emphasizes modern infrastructure and better learning outcomes. Additionally, the state continues to implement 25% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections under the Right to Education Act, promoting inclusive access to quality education. This combined reform approach aims to strengthen educational standards and equity across the state.

questions.question: Which state government launched the ‘Meri Rasoi’ scheme to provide free food kits to 40 lakh blue card holder families?
questions.explanation: Punjab launched the ‘Meri Rasoi’ scheme to provide free food kits to nearly 40 lakh economically weaker families holding blue cards. The initiative, announced by Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann ahead of the state’s Budget Session, aims to address nutritional gaps among low-income households. Under the first phase from April to June 2026, beneficiaries will receive essential items such as chana dal, sugar, iodized salt, turmeric powder, and mustard oil. The scheme focuses on improving child health, reducing malnutrition, strengthening food security, and ensuring that vulnerable families receive balanced and nutritious food supplies regularly.

questions.question: On which date is National Science Day observed every year in India to honour the discovery of the Raman Effect?
questions.explanation: February 28 is observed annually as National Science Day in India to commemorate the discovery of the Raman Effect by Sir C. V. Raman in 1928. This groundbreaking work explained the scattering of light through transparent materials and later earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930, making him the first Asian Nobel laureate in science. The Government of India officially designated this date as National Science Day in 1986. The occasion promotes scientific awareness, innovation, and research culture, while celebrating India’s significant contributions to global scientific advancement and encouraging future generations in STEM fields.

questions.question: Who has taken additional charge as the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: Nidhi Chhibber has taken additional charge as the Chief Executive Officer of NITI Aayog following the completion of BVR Subrahmanyam’s three-year term in February 2026. A 1994-batch IAS officer of the Chhattisgarh cadre, she currently serves as Director General of the Development Monitoring and Evaluation Office (DMEO) under NITI Aayog. She has also previously held the position of Chairperson of the Central Board of Secondary Education. Her extensive experience in policy monitoring, evaluation, and education sector reforms positions her to strengthen data-driven governance and performance assessment within India’s premier policy think tank.

questions.question: Which country signed a new MoU with India to strengthen cooperation in forests, wildlife, biodiversity conservation, and climate change?
questions.explanation: Nepal signed a new Memorandum of Understanding with India to enhance bilateral cooperation in forests, wildlife protection, biodiversity conservation, and climate change mitigation. The agreement focuses on restoring transboundary wildlife corridors, protecting key species such as tigers, elephants, rhinoceroses, and snow leopards, and strengthening protected area management. It also emphasises combating wildlife crime, improving coordination between enforcement agencies, and promoting capacity building for frontline forest staff. Given the shared ecosystems and migratory wildlife habitats across borders, the partnership supports integrated conservation strategies and climate resilience efforts in the Himalayan and adjoining landscapes.

questions.question: Which city will host the AI Summit 2027, as announced?
questions.explanation: Geneva will host the AI Summit 2027, marking Switzerland’s significant role in advancing global dialogue on artificial intelligence governance. The announcement was made by Swiss President Guy Parmelin during the AI Impact Summit in New Delhi. Known for being a hub of major international organisations, Geneva provides a neutral platform for multilateral discussions and cooperation. The summit is expected to focus on ethical and inclusive AI frameworks, strengthening partnerships between countries such as Switzerland and India. The hosting also reflects growing global collaboration in AI diplomacy, with the UAE set to host the summit in 2028.

questions.question: Which organisation successfully conducted three flight trials of the indigenous VSHORADS missile system, making it ready for induction into the Indian armed forces?
questions.explanation: DRDO successfully carried out three consecutive flight trials of the indigenous VSHORADS missile system from Chandipur, Odisha, demonstrating its operational readiness. The Very Short Range Air Defence System is a man-portable missile designed to intercept low-flying aircraft, helicopters, drones, and high-speed aerial threats. Developed under the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative, the system was tested in its final deployment configuration with trained field operators handling target acquisition and firing. Data from telemetry, radar, and electro-optical tracking confirmed its precision and effectiveness, strengthening India’s short-range and layered air defence capabilities significantly.

questions.question: Which former India captain will have a stand named after him at Mumbai’s Wankhede Stadium?
questions.explanation: Ravi Shastri will have a stand named after him at the iconic Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai following approval by the Mumbai Cricket Association during its Apex Council meeting. The Level 1 Stand below the Press Box will bear his name, recognising his long and multifaceted contribution to Indian cricket as a player, captain, head coach, and commentator. A former all-rounder, he was part of India’s 1983 World Cup era and later guided the national team to significant overseas Test victories as coach. The tribute highlights Mumbai’s rich cricketing legacy and honours his enduring association with the sport.

questions.question: On which date will World NGO Day 2026 be observed globally?
questions.explanation: February 27 is observed globally as World NGO Day in 2026, recognizing the vital contributions of non-governmental organisations across sectors such as education, healthcare, environmental protection, human rights, and disaster relief. The day serves as an international platform to appreciate NGOs for their grassroots efforts and to promote awareness, advocacy, and collaboration. It highlights the role of civil society in addressing social inequalities, poverty reduction, and sustainable development. The observance encourages citizens, institutions, and governments to actively participate through volunteering, donations, partnerships, and community initiatives that strengthen the global NGO ecosystem.

questions.question: Which Indian state has established its first Dark Sky Park at Ariyur Shola Reserve Forest in Kolli Hills to promote astrotourism and preserve the night sky?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has set up its first Dark Sky Park at Ariyur Shola Reserve Forest in the Kolli Hills of Namakkal district. The initiative focuses on preserving the natural darkness of the night sky by reducing light pollution, thereby creating ideal conditions for astronomical observation. It also aims to promote astrotourism, educate visitors about celestial bodies such as constellations and planets, and raise awareness about the adverse effects of artificial lighting. Additionally, the project encourages sustainable eco-tourism that supports local tribal communities while ensuring the protection of the surrounding forest ecosystem.

questions.question: Which country’s first-ever Blue Bond, issued by DFCC Bank, was dually listed on NSE International Exchange at GIFT City?
questions.explanation: Sri Lanka is the country whose DFCC Bank issued the first-ever Blue Bond that was dually listed on the NSE International Exchange at GIFT City. The LKR 3 billion bond was initially listed on the Colombo Stock Exchange before being admitted to trading under GIFT City’s offshore regulatory framework. Blue bonds are a specialised subset of green bonds that channel investments into ocean conservation, sustainable fisheries, marine ecosystem protection, and water security projects. The dual listing enhances global investor participation and strengthens the country’s presence in sustainable and innovative financial markets.

questions.question: In which city was Micron’s first India ATMP (Assembly, Test, Mark & Pack) semiconductor plant inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi?
questions.explanation: Sanand is the city where Micron Semiconductor Technology India Pvt Ltd established its first Assembly, Test, Mark & Pack (ATMP) facility in India. The plant was inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and marks the beginning of commercial production of made-in-India semiconductor memory modules. With an investment exceeding ₹22,500 crore, the project is a major milestone under the India Semiconductor Mission. The facility includes around 500,000 square feet of cleanroom space and is designed to process imported DRAM and NAND wafers into finished memory products, strengthening India’s role in the global semiconductor value chain.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the new blind groundwater fish species ‘Gitchak nakana’ discovered in a dug-out well?
questions.explanation: Assam is the state where the new blind groundwater fish species ‘Gitchak nakana’ was discovered in Goalpara district. This remarkable finding marks the first recorded instance of a groundwater-dwelling fish from Northeast India and the broader Eastern Himalayan region. The tiny, translucent red fish measures about 2 cm in length and exhibits classic subterranean adaptations such as complete blindness, lack of pigmentation, and absence of a skull roof. Found in underground aquifers rather than caves, the discovery highlights the rich yet fragile biodiversity of the Brahmaputra Valley and underscores the need for groundwater conservation.

questions.question: In which city was the National Conference on UDISE+ organised by the Department of School Education & Literacy in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Bhubaneswar hosted the two-day National Conference on Unified District Information System for Education Plus (UDISE+), organised by the Department of School Education & Literacy under the Ministry of Education in collaboration with the Government of Odisha. The conference focused on enhancing the reliability, real-time accuracy, and strategic use of UDISE+, which serves as India’s flagship education data platform. Discussions centred on strengthening data integrity, resolving implementation challenges faced by states and union territories, and expanding the system into an AI-compliant decision support framework to enable evidence-based policymaking and effective implementation of Samagra Shiksha and NEP 2020 initiatives.

questions.question: Which team won their maiden Ranji Trophy title in the 2025-26 season by defeating Karnataka in the final?
questions.explanation: Jammu & Kashmir secured its first-ever Ranji Trophy title in the 2025–26 season, marking a historic achievement after 67 years of participation in India’s premier domestic first-class cricket tournament. The team defeated Karnataka in the final held at the KSCA Rajnagar Stadium in Hubballi. This victory is regarded as a transformative milestone for cricket in the Union Territory, symbolising resilience, consistent talent development, and competitive growth. The Ranji Trophy, organized annually by the BCCI since 1934–35, features teams from states and union territories across India, making this triumph especially significant.

questions.question: Which city set a new Guinness World Record by planting 2,51,446 saplings in one hour on 1 March 2026?
questions.explanation: Varanasi achieved a historic milestone by planting 2,51,446 saplings within a single hour, surpassing the previous record held by China for eight years. The large-scale plantation drive was conducted on 350 bighas of land in Domari village along the banks of the Ganga. Around 20,000 participants took part in the synchronised effort, which was officially verified by a Guinness World Records judge. The area has been structured into 60 sectors named after prominent Kashi ghats, creating a symbolic urban forest using the Miyawaki method to promote rapid ecological restoration and biodiversity conservation.

questions.question: Which payments company partnered with Gnani.ai to launch an agentic AI platform enabling in-call UPI payments during live customer conversations?
questions.explanation: Gnani.ai collaborated with Razorpay to introduce an innovative agentic AI collections platform that enables customers to complete UPI payments during live calls without disconnecting or switching to external links. The platform combines Gnani.ai’s advanced AI systems, capable of handling over 10 million calls daily, with Razorpay’s Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to create a seamless, real-time payment interface. This deployment represents one of India’s first production-level uses of autonomous AI agents executing financial transactions, significantly enhancing operational efficiency for enterprises in sectors such as BFSI, telecom, utilities, and e-commerce.

questions.question: Which organisation provided a USD 150 million direct loan and acted as the mandated lead arranger for IIFL Home Finance’s USD 300 million financing package?
questions.explanation: The USD 300 million financing agreement was structured to expand affordable housing loans, particularly targeting women borrowers from economically weaker and lower-middle-income groups. Out of the total amount, USD 150 million was provided directly by the Asian Development Bank, while the remaining USD 150 million was raised through parallel loans from institutions such as MUFG, Emirates NBD, Sampath Bank, and Hatton National Bank. The Asian Development Bank also served as the mandated lead arranger and bookrunner for the entire package, highlighting its key role in promoting financial inclusion, women’s empowerment, and sustainable urban development in India.

questions.question: In which Indian state has GAIL approved a ₹1,736 crore greenfield wind power project as part of its renewable energy expansion?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra is the state where GAIL (India) Limited has approved a ₹1,736 crore investment for a greenfield wind power project. This initiative represents the company’s largest single renewable energy investment so far and reflects its strategic transition toward becoming an integrated energy player. The proposed wind farm is expected to have a capacity of around 300–350 MW, with construction likely to begin in 2026–27 and commercial operations targeted for 2028–29. The project supports GAIL’s goal of achieving nearly 2 GW of renewable capacity by 2030 and contributes to India’s broader non-fossil fuel energy ambitions.

questions.question: Which bank received RBI approval for SBI Mutual Fund to acquire up to a 9.99% stake through its various schemes?
questions.explanation: Bandhan Bank is the institution in which SBI Mutual Fund has been permitted by the Reserve Bank of India to acquire up to a 9.99% stake. The approval allows SBI Mutual Fund to become one of the largest non-promoter shareholders in the bank, signalling strong institutional confidence in its performance and future prospects. The investment reflects optimism regarding improvements in asset quality, expansion of its retail franchise, and overall growth strategy. This development is expected to strengthen the bank’s shareholding structure, improve investor sentiment, and support its long-term capital-raising and expansion initiatives.

questions.question: Which institution formalised a Framework for Action with the International Solar Alliance (ISA) to advance solar skills and capacity building across member countries?
questions.explanation: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) signed a Framework for Action with IIT Delhi to strengthen collaboration in renewable energy, particularly in human resource development, academic programmes, joint research, and knowledge exchange. This agreement builds upon five years of successful partnership, including the ISA-supported M.Tech programme in Renewable Energy Technologies & Management, which has trained over 100 mid-career professionals from regions such as Africa, Asia-Pacific, and Small Island Developing States. The expanded framework introduces new training models, trainer development initiatives, and broader capacity-building measures to accelerate solar adoption and promote clean energy transitions across ISA’s member countries.

questions.question: Which state government signed an MoU with GIZ and the State Mission for Clean Ganga (SMCG) to develop basin-based masterplans for the Ichhamati and Jalangi rivers?
questions.explanation: The Government of West Bengal entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with GIZ, the German development agency, and the State Mission for Clean Ganga (SMCG) to prepare comprehensive basin-based masterplans for the Ichhamati and Jalangi river basins. These rivers are significant transboundary distributaries in the Ganga delta, flowing between India and Bangladesh. The initiative aims to ensure sustainable river basin management by addressing ecological degradation, water security challenges, and socio-economic concerns. It also aligns with the objectives of the National Mission for Clean Ganga, while incorporating German expertise in climate resilience, integrated river basin management, and environmental sustainability.

questions.question: When will World Wildlife Day 2026 be observed globally?
questions.explanation: World Wildlife Day 2026 will be observed on March 3, marking the anniversary of the signing of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) in 1973. The day is dedicated to celebrating wild animals and plants while promoting global biodiversity conservation efforts. The 2026 theme focuses on “Medicinal and Aromatic Plants: Conserving Health, Heritage and Livelihoods,” emphasising the importance of plant species in healthcare, cultural traditions, and sustainable economies. The observance highlights the urgent need to protect ecosystems, regulate wildlife trade, and address threats such as habitat destruction and climate change.

questions.question: Which country announced a Strategic Energy Partnership with India and agreed on a CAD 2.6 billion uranium supply deal in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Canada entered into a Strategic Energy Partnership with India as part of a broader roadmap to reset bilateral relations. The partnership covers cooperation in clean energy, conventional energy, civil nuclear power, and critical minerals. A major highlight was a CAD 2.6 billion uranium supply agreement between Cameco and India’s Department of Atomic Energy, strengthening India’s nuclear energy capacity. Both nations also relaunched ministerial energy dialogues and initiated negotiations for a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to boost bilateral trade to CAD 70 billion by 2030, reinforcing long-term economic and strategic collaboration.

questions.question: Which state issued a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Heat Wave Management for Higher Education Institutions ahead of the summer season?
questions.explanation: The Higher Education Department of Odisha released a Standard Operating Procedure for Heat Wave Management applicable to colleges and universities across the state as part of its summer preparedness strategy. The directive focuses on safeguarding students, faculty, and staff from extreme heat conditions. Key measures include rescheduling classes and examinations to morning hours, restricting outdoor academic and sports activities during peak afternoon heat, ensuring uninterrupted access to safe drinking water, and maintaining first aid readiness for heat-related illnesses. The initiative reflects proactive governance aimed at minimising health risks during intense summer heatwaves.

questions.question: Which institution signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with HCLTech to drive deep tech innovation for Global Capability Centres (GCCs)?
questions.explanation: HCLTech collaborated with IIT Kanpur through a Memorandum of Understanding to promote research-led innovation in deep technology domains for Global Capability Centres (GCCs). The partnership aims to convert cutting-edge academic research into practical pilots and scalable solutions. Key focus areas include artificial intelligence, robotics, advanced engineering, and next-generation technologies. This collaboration positions HCLTech as a strategic enabler for GCCs, helping them accelerate research and development, gain access to specialised expertise, and reduce time-to-market without the need to establish dedicated in-house research facilities.

questions.question: Where did the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) unveil the indigenous 30 kW Wide Band Gap-based Integrated Drive System for electric vehicles on March 3, 2026?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) unveiled the indigenously developed 30 kW Wide Band Gap-based Integrated Drive System for electric vehicles in Chennai. The launch took place at IIT Madras under the National Mission on Power Electronics Technology (NaMPET). The system was jointly developed by C-DAC Thiruvananthapuram, IIT Madras, and industry partner Lucas TVS. This innovation enhances India’s capabilities in advanced power electronics and supports the rapidly growing electric passenger vehicle market. It also contributes to reducing dependence on imported high-performance electric vehicle components, strengthening the country’s push toward technological self-reliance.

questions.question: Which state exported its first consignment of GI-tagged Karbi Anglong Ginger to London, United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Assam marked a significant milestone in agricultural exports by dispatching its first-ever shipment of GI-tagged Karbi Anglong Ginger to London. The consignment was flagged off by the state’s Agriculture Minister at Krishi Bhawan in Guwahati. Karbi Anglong Ginger, which received its Geographical Indication tag in 2015, is known for its distinct aroma, flavour, and superior quality resulting from the region’s unique agro-climatic conditions. Cultivated using traditional Jhum and Tila farming systems in the Singhasan Hills, this export initiative aims to boost farmer incomes, strengthen global market access, and enhance the international recognition of the state’s agricultural produce.

questions.question: Who assumed charge as the Director General of Naval Armament (DGONA) at Naval Headquarters on March 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: B.P. Singh took over as the Director General of Naval Armament (DGONA) at Naval Headquarters in New Delhi on March 1, 2026. A senior officer from the 1994 batch of the Indian Naval Armament Service, he succeeded Divakar Jayant following his retirement. With over three decades of experience in naval armament management, procurement, logistics, and guided weapon systems, he has held key positions at multiple Naval Armament Depots and at Naval Headquarters. An alumnus of the National Defence College, he brings strategic and operational expertise to strengthen India’s naval weapon systems management and maritime defence preparedness.

questions.question: Who was appointed by the Government of India as the Interim Chairman & Managing Director (CMD) of Engineers India Limited (EIL) effective March 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: The Government of India appointed Praveen M. Khanooja to hold additional charge as Chairman & Managing Director of Engineers India Limited (EIL) beginning March 1, 2026. The appointment, approved by the Minister of Petroleum & Natural Gas, is for three months or until a regular CMD assumes office. A 1994-batch officer of the Indian Audit & Accounts Service, he continues serving as Additional Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas while handling CMD responsibilities. His extensive experience spans defence audit, railways audit, central government finance, and leadership roles in key petroleum and economic intelligence institutions.

questions.question: Which country has overtaken the United States to become the second-highest country globally for childhood obesity according to the World Obesity Atlas 2026?
questions.explanation: India has surpassed the United States to become the country with the second-highest number of children affected by obesity globally, according to the World Obesity Atlas 2026 released by the World Obesity Federation. The report indicates that around 41 million Indian children had a high Body Mass Index (BMI) by 2025, including about 14 million living with obesity. Projections suggest that by 2040, nearly 20 million children in India may be living with obesity, and around 56 million could be overweight or obese. Rising consumption of sugary beverages, low physical activity among adolescents, and limited access to nutritious food are among the key factors contributing to this growing public health challenge.

questions.question: In whose presence were the ₹5,083 crore defence contracts for ALH Mk-III helicopters and VL-Shtil missiles signed at South Block, New Delhi, in March 2026?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Defence signed major defence procurement contracts worth ₹5,083 crore on March 3, 2026, aimed at strengthening India’s maritime security and air defence capabilities. The agreements included the purchase of six ALH Mk-III (Maritime Role) helicopters for the Indian Coast Guard and VL-Shtil surface-to-air missiles for the Indian Navy. These contracts were signed at South Block, New Delhi, in the presence of Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh. The ALH Mk-III deal with Hindustan Aeronautics Limited supports indigenous manufacturing under the “Buy (Indian-IDDM)” category, while the VL-Shtil missile agreement with Russia’s Rosoboronexport enhances the layered air defence system of Indian naval warships.

questions.question: On which date is National Safety Day observed annually in India to promote workplace safety and accident prevention?
questions.explanation: National Safety Day is observed every year in India on March 4 to promote awareness about workplace safety, occupational health, and accident prevention. The day is organised by the National Safety Council (NSC), which was established on March 4, 1966. National Safety Day was first celebrated in 1972 and has since grown into a nationwide campaign encouraging industries and organizations to adopt better safety practices. The observance now extends into National Safety Week and even National Safety Month, during which seminars, safety drills, training programs, and awareness campaigns are conducted to strengthen safety culture across workplaces and protect employees from occupational hazards.

questions.question: On which date is World Obesity Day observed globally to raise awareness about the growing obesity crisis?
questions.explanation: World Obesity Day is observed annually on March 4 to raise awareness about the global obesity crisis and encourage collective action to address this major public health challenge. Established by the World Obesity Federation in 2015, the observance was originally held on October 11 but was moved to March 4 in 2020 to align with other global health initiatives. The day focuses on promoting healthier lifestyles, improving access to nutritious food, and encouraging physical activity. It also highlights that obesity is a complex chronic disease influenced by genetic, environmental, social, and economic factors, requiring coordinated efforts from governments, healthcare professionals, and communities.

questions.question: Which country became the first in the United Kingdom to legalise water cremation (alkaline hydrolysis) as a greener alternative to traditional burial and cremation?
questions.explanation: Scotland became the first country in the United Kingdom to legalise water cremation, also known as alkaline hydrolysis. This method is considered an environmentally friendly alternative to traditional cremation and burial because it produces significantly lower carbon emissions. The process involves placing the body in a pressurized chamber with water and potassium hydroxide, which breaks down soft tissues while leaving bones that are later returned to families as powdered remains. The reform represents one of the most significant changes to cremation laws since 1902, and the first water cremations are expected to begin by summer 2026 after necessary approvals.

questions.question: The Indian Army signed an MoU with the Forest Department of which region on World Wildlife Day 2026 to protect the Snow Leopard and other high-altitude wildlife?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army and the Ladakh Forest Department signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen wildlife conservation efforts in fragile high-altitude ecosystems. The initiative focuses on protecting species such as the Snow Leopard, Himalayan Wolf, Bharal (Blue Sheep), Ibex, Himalayan Marmot, and the Black-necked Crane. The agreement also includes the establishment of a Joint Snow Leopard Conservation Cell to monitor wildlife and coordinate conservation actions. Ladakh’s cold desert environment, characterised by low oxygen levels, extreme temperatures, and sparse vegetation, makes its biodiversity highly vulnerable, making this collaborative conservation effort particularly important.

questions.question: Who assumed charge as the Director General of Naval Armament (DGONA) at Naval Headquarters, New Delhi, on March 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: B.P. Singh, a 1994-batch officer of the Indian Naval Armament Service (INAS), assumed charge as the Director General of Naval Armament (DGONA) at Naval Headquarters in New Delhi on March 1, 2026. He succeeded Divakar Jayant, who retired on February 28, 2026, after completing a distinguished career. Before taking up this position, B.P. Singh served as the Chief General Manager of the Naval Armament Depot (NAD) Mumbai, where he handled operational, logistics, and infrastructure responsibilities. He also held key roles at NAD Aluva, Visakhapatnam, Mumbai, and Trombay, contributing significantly to naval armament management and operational efficiency.

questions.question: Which country renewed a $75 billion bilateral currency swap agreement with India for an additional three years starting March 4, 2026?
questions.explanation: Japan renewed its $75 billion bilateral currency swap agreement with India for another three years beginning March 4, 2026. The arrangement was formalised through an exchange of letters between the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the Bank of Japan (BoJ). This financial mechanism allows either country to access the other’s currency—Japanese yen or Indian rupee—during times of balance-of-payments pressure or liquidity shortages. The agreement, originally established in 2018 and renewed earlier in 2023, strengthens financial cooperation between the two nations and serves as a safeguard to maintain macroeconomic stability and enhance investor confidence during global economic uncertainties.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the first Kashmiri woman Superintendent of Srinagar Central Jail in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Saba Shawl was appointed as the Superintendent of Srinagar Central Jail in March 2026, becoming the first Kashmiri woman to hold this position in the history of the facility. Her appointment represents an important step toward greater gender representation in Jammu and Kashmir’s prison administration. Srinagar Central Jail is one of the oldest and most significant correctional institutions in the Union Territory, housing both undertrial prisoners and convicts. As a senior officer in the J&K Prison Department, Saba Shawl has previously served in several roles, gaining extensive experience in jail administration, inmate welfare, and security management.

questions.question: In which city was the 11th edition of the Raisina Dialogue held from March 5–7, 2026?
questions.explanation: The 11th edition of the Raisina Dialogue was held from March 5–7, 2026, in New Delhi, India. It is organised by the Observer Research Foundation (ORF) in partnership with the Ministry of External Affairs. The Raisina Dialogue is regarded as Asia’s premier conference on geopolitics and geoeconomics, bringing together global leaders, policymakers, scholars, and experts from various sectors. The 2026 theme was “Saṁskāra – Assertion, Accommodation, Advancement.” The event featured discussions on global governance, climate challenges, technological developments, and economic resilience. Finland’s President Alexander Stubb attended as the chief guest and delivered the keynote address.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the ₹12.66 crore Challenge Fund to accelerate rural entrepreneurship, focusing on women and youth?
questions.explanation: Telangana introduced a ₹12.66 crore Challenge Fund to promote rural entrepreneurship, particularly among women and youth at the grassroots level. The initiative is implemented through the Society for Elimination of Rural Poverty (SERP) in collaboration with TBIS BITS Pilani Hyderabad, T-Hub, and WE Hub, with support from the Ministry of Rural Development. It aims to transition about 65 lakh Self-Help Group (SHG) women from microfinance participation to formal entrepreneurship and scalable ventures. Around 300 selected entrepreneurs will receive mentoring, enterprise diagnostics, branding support, market access, and help in accessing finance. The programme will be implemented across all 33 districts through a decentralised outreach model.

questions.question: Who was named to TIME’s Women of the Year 2026 list for her transformative work in advancing girls’ education in rural India?
questions.explanation: Safeena Husain, the founder of the non-profit organisation Educate Girls, was included in TIME magazine’s Women of the Year 2026 list for her impactful efforts in promoting girls’ education in rural India. Her initiative has helped bring more than 2 million out-of-school girls back into the education system. What began in 2007 with work in about 50 villages in Rajasthan has expanded significantly to over 25,000 villages across Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh. In 2025, Educate Girls also received the Ramon Magsaysay Award for community leadership. Her work highlights the importance of grassroots initiatives in improving educational access for marginalised girls.

questions.question: In which city was the AI-powered ‘Saksham Skill Survey’ launched to map and upskill youth through a door-to-door drive?
questions.explanation: The AI-powered ‘Saksham Skill Survey’ was launched in Mumbai, specifically in the H-West ward covering areas such as Bandra, Khar, and Santacruz. The initiative aims to assess the education levels, skills, and employment potential of youth aged between 18 and 40 years. Around 55,000 households will be covered through door-to-door enumeration. The project intends to train at least 15,000 individuals and help about 5,000 achieve self-reliance through employment or entrepreneurship. Conducted by Sapio Analytics, the survey collects data on educational qualifications, skills, and training needs, and uses artificial intelligence to develop a decentralised workforce intelligence model.

questions.question: On which date is the International Day for Disarmament and Non-Proliferation Awareness observed every year?
questions.explanation: The International Day for Disarmament and Non-Proliferation Awareness is observed annually on March 5. The day was proclaimed by the United Nations General Assembly through Resolution A/RES/77/51 to raise awareness about the importance of disarmament, arms control, and non-proliferation in maintaining global peace and security. It encourages governments, UN agencies, civil society organisations, educational institutions, and the media to conduct awareness activities. The observance also emphasises educating young people about the risks associated with weapons of mass destruction, illegal arms trade, and emerging military technologies, while promoting dialogue, peacebuilding, and international cooperation to reduce armed conflict worldwide.

questions.question: Which institution signed a five-year Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Survey of India for geospatial research and innovation?
questions.explanation: Survey of India (SoI) signed a five-year Memorandum of Understanding with Kerala University of Digital Sciences, Innovation and Technology, popularly known as Kerala Digital University. The collaboration aims to strengthen geospatial research and promote digital innovation by leveraging advanced technologies such as geoinformatics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning. The partnership focuses on improving the collection, processing, analysis, and application of geospatial data. It also includes joint research and development activities, capacity-building programs, training initiatives, and higher education opportunities. This initiative is expected to support technological advancement and enhance expertise in geospatial technologies and digital sciences in India.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the ‘HANUMAN’ project launched to mitigate human–wildlife conflict?
questions.explanation: The ‘HANUMAN’ project (Healing and Nurturing Units for Monitoring, Aid and Nursing of Wildlife) was launched in Andhra Pradesh on March 3, 2026, by Deputy Chief Minister and Forest and Environment Minister Pawan Kalyan. The initiative aims to address the growing issue of human–wildlife conflict caused by expanding human settlements near forest areas and increased wildlife movement into rural regions. As part of the rollout, more than 100 specially equipped vehicles, including rapid response units and wildlife ambulances, were deployed. The project focuses on early warning systems, real-time monitoring, quick rescue operations, and rehabilitation efforts to reduce casualties, crop damage, and retaliatory harm to wildlife.

questions.question: Which international organisation partnered with NITI Aayog to improve nutrition and health outcomes in Aspirational Districts and Blocks across India?
questions.explanation: UNICEF partnered with NITI Aayog to improve nutrition and health outcomes in Aspirational Districts and Aspirational Blocks across India. The collaboration focuses on strengthening maternal and child nutrition, improving Anganwadi services, and enhancing health systems in underserved regions. The partnership also aims to promote multi-stakeholder engagement involving government agencies, private sector organisations, and local communities. Through this initiative, UNICEF will leverage its IMPAct4Nutrition platform to mobilise businesses and channel Corporate Social Responsibility funds toward nutrition programmes. The initiative supports the broader goals of the Aspirational Districts Programme, which seeks to improve socio-economic indicators in India’s most underdeveloped districts through better governance, monitoring, and service delivery.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the MeHEALTH mobile app to help citizens access information about nearby hospitals, laboratories, and healthcare services?
questions.explanation: Kerala launched the MeHEALTH mobile application to improve public access to healthcare information and services. The app was introduced by the state Health Department to help citizens easily locate nearby hospitals, speciality departments, laboratories, blood banks, and pharmacies. It also provides features such as online doctor appointment booking and OP ticket generation, reducing waiting time at hospitals. Users can log in with their mobile number and generate a unique health ID using Aadhaar details. The app also integrates navigation features to guide users to healthcare institutions. This initiative strengthens digital healthcare access and enhances the efficiency of public health services across the state.

questions.question: In which Indian state did Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah dedicate the IFFCO Sulphuric Acid Plant (SAP-III) and launch multiple cooperative sector initiatives on 6 March 2026?
questions.explanation: Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah visited Paradip and Bhubaneswar on 6 March 2026 to dedicate the IFFCO Sulphuric Acid Plant (SAP-III) and launch several major cooperative sector initiatives. The programmes were organised in the state of Odisha and focused on strengthening cooperative institutions, promoting rural development, and boosting the agricultural and dairy sectors. The visit included the release of the State Cooperative Policy, launch of digital platforms, inauguration of Common Service Centres operated by PACS and LAMPS, and foundation stones for projects worth more than ₹3,275 crore. These initiatives aim to enhance rural prosperity, improve governance in cooperatives, and support farmers and producers through modern infrastructure and digital services.

questions.question: Which country became the first in the Americas to be verified by the WHO for eliminating leprosy as a public health problem?
questions.explanation: Chile achieved a major public health milestone by becoming the first country in the Americas and the fourth globally to receive official verification from the World Health Organisation for eliminating leprosy as a public health problem. This recognition was granted after the country maintained a prevalence rate of less than one case per 10,000 people at the national level for several consecutive years. The achievement reflects decades of consistent government commitment, strong integration of leprosy services into the public health system, and effective strategies such as early detection, multidrug therapy treatment coverage, surveillance, and contact tracing. High treatment completion rates and a very low proportion of cases among children further demonstrated successful control of disease transmission.

questions.question: By which year does the report project a major surge in obesity-related health conditions among Indian youth?
questions.explanation: A report warns that India may face a significant rise in obesity-related health problems among children and adolescents by the year 2030. The projections indicate a sharp increase in metabolic and cardiovascular conditions such as MASLD (Metabolic dysfunction–associated steatotic liver disease), high triglycerides, hypertension, and hyperglycemia. These conditions are linked to lifestyle changes, including increased consumption of ultra-processed foods, reduced physical activity, and rising sedentary habits due to screen time and urban lifestyles. India is experiencing a rapid nutrition transition along with a “double burden” where undernutrition and overnutrition coexist. If preventive measures are not strengthened, these early health risks could lead to a substantial increase in heart disease, diabetes, and liver disorders in adulthood.

questions.question: On which river will India build its first riverine lighthouses under the National Waterway-2 project?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra River is the river where India will establish its first riverine lighthouses as part of the National Waterway-2 project. The initiative includes the construction of four solar-powered lighthouses at Pandu, Bogibeel, Silghat, and Biswanath Ghat with an investment of ₹84 crore. Each lighthouse will be approximately 20 metres tall and will help guide vessels during nighttime and challenging weather conditions. The project is implemented by the Directorate General of Lighthouses and Lightships in partnership with the Inland Waterways Authority of India. It aims to improve navigation safety, boost cargo movement, such as tea and fertiliser, and promote tourism along the Brahmaputra river corridor in Northeast India.

questions.question: Which Indian state government announced a ₹25,000 incentive for couples having a second or third child to address declining fertility rates?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh introduced a policy providing a ₹25,000 financial incentive for couples who have a second or third child. The initiative was announced by Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu to address the state’s declining Total Fertility Rate (TFR), which is currently around 1.5, below the replacement level of 2.1 required for stable population growth. The policy aims to encourage families to have more children and maintain demographic balance while addressing concerns about an ageing population and future workforce shortages. It also reflects a shift from earlier family planning policies toward population growth management, ensuring long-term socio-economic stability in the state.

questions.question: Which Indian company was selected as the official partner for UNESCO’s World Engineering Day for Sustainable Development 2026?
questions.explanation: Adani Group was selected as the official partner for UNESCO’s World Engineering Day for Sustainable Development (WED) 2026. This recognition marks the first time an Indian organization has been chosen for this global partnership coordinated by the World Federation of Engineering Organizations under UNESCO. The selection highlights the company’s contributions to renewable energy and sustainable engineering solutions. A major factor behind this recognition is the massive Khavda Renewable Energy Project in Gujarat, being developed by Adani Green Energy Limited. The project aims to reach a capacity of 30 GW by 2029, making it one of the largest renewable energy projects in the world and supporting India’s clean energy goals.

questions.question: Who was reappointed as the Drugs Controller General of India (DCGI) for an additional one-year term as approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajeev Raghuvanshi received approval from the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) for reappointment as the Drugs Controller General of India (DCGI) for an additional one-year term in March 2026. He was initially appointed in November 2023 on a contractual basis for two years with the possibility of extension. The extension ensures continuity in leadership at the Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO), India’s apex drug regulatory authority. With prior experience as Joint Drugs Controller (India) and other senior roles within CDSCO, Dr. Raghuvanshi brings significant expertise in regulatory science, pharmacovigilance, and biologics regulation, supporting ongoing reforms and global harmonisation efforts in India’s pharmaceutical regulatory system.

questions.question: In which city was the official logo of the world’s first franchise-based “Cycling League of India” unveiled by Union Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya on March 5, 2026?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, unveiled the official logo of the Cycling League of India (CLI) on March 5, 2026, in New Delhi. The league is recognised as the world’s first franchise-based professional cycling league and aims to promote cycling as a major competitive sport in India. Inspired by franchise models similar to those used in popular cricket leagues, the initiative is designed to encourage grassroots talent, improve professional opportunities for cyclists, and strengthen India’s presence in global cycling competitions. The league will feature city-based teams, professional riders, youth talents, women’s categories, and multiple race formats, including road races, criteriums, and time trials.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the 5th cohort of the HerSTART initiative launched by the Chief Minister to support women-led startups?
questions.explanation: Gujarat is the state where the 5th cohort of the HerSTART initiative was launched by Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel to strengthen women entrepreneurship and innovation-driven growth. The programme supports women-led startups by providing mentoring, funding opportunities, and connections to investor networks. Selected entrepreneurs also receive assistance such as co-working spaces, legal and intellectual property advisory, branding support, and market linkages. Along with the launch of the cohort, a modern Research Park was inaugurated to promote collaboration between academia and industry. The initiative focuses on sectors including technology, health-tech, agri-tech, sustainability, and social enterprises to accelerate the growth of scalable women-led ventures.

questions.question: Which public sector bank raised ₹10,000 crore through its maiden green infrastructure bonds issuance on March 6, 2026?
questions.explanation: Bank of Baroda successfully raised ₹10,000 crore through its first-ever green infrastructure bonds issuance, marking one of the largest such issuances by an Indian public sector bank. The bonds were issued on March 6, 2026, in two tranches of ₹5,000 crore each with maturities of seven years and ten years, carrying coupon rates of 7.74% and 7.85% respectively. The bonds were listed on the BSE and NSE and received AAA (stable) ratings from CRISIL, ICRA, and India Ratings, indicating strong creditworthiness. The funds raised will be used exclusively for green and sustainable infrastructure projects such as renewable energy, energy efficiency, clean transportation, green buildings, pollution control, and climate change adaptation initiatives.

questions.question: Who set the Guinness World Record for the fastest 50m limbo skating with a time of 7.46 seconds in February 2025?
questions.explanation: Shrishti Dharmendra Sharma from India achieved a remarkable feat by setting the Guinness World Record for the fastest 50-meter limbo skating with a time of 7.46 seconds on February 28, 2025. The record was set at Sai Dham City in Maharashtra and involved skating under a series of descending horizontal poles without touching them. Limbo skating is a challenging sport that demands exceptional flexibility, balance, and speed. Sharma broke her own previous record of 9.59 seconds set in Italy in 2023. By improving her time by 2.13 seconds, she demonstrated extraordinary skill and control, further strengthening her position as a world leader in this unique sporting discipline.

questions.question: Which country announced a regional-first social media ban for minors under the age of 16 starting March 28, 2026?
questions.explanation: Indonesia has announced a new policy to restrict access to several social media platforms for minors under the age of 16. The initiative will begin on March 28, 2026, and will be implemented by the country’s Communications and Digital Ministry. The regulation targets major platforms such as TikTok, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, X, Bigo Live, Roblox, and YouTube, with a phased process of account deactivations for underage users. The move was introduced after the government declared a digital emergency to address issues such as cyberbullying, pornography, online fraud, and addiction caused by algorithm-driven content. The policy will initially operate through ministerial guidelines before permanent legislation establishing a minimum age requirement is introduced.

questions.question: Which Bengaluru-based space startup signed an MoU with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) to advance satellite technologies for Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO)?
questions.explanation: Bellatrix Aerospace, a Bengaluru-based space startup, signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) in March 2026 to collaborate on the development of satellite systems and payloads designed for Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO). This partnership focuses on building advanced satellite platforms capable of operating at altitudes between 100 and 450 kilometers above Earth. Satellites operating in VLEO offer several advantages, including higher-resolution Earth observation, lower communication latency, and improved atmospheric and climate data collection. Additionally, the natural orbital decay in VLEO reduces long-term space debris risks and lowers launch and operational costs compared to traditional orbits like LEO, MEO, and GEO.

questions.question: Which city deployed the AI-based “G-SPIDER” robot for hazardous canal maintenance to eliminate manual scavenging?
questions.explanation: The municipal corporation of Thiruvananthapuram introduced the AI-powered “G-SPIDER” robotic system to carry out hazardous canal maintenance and eliminate manual scavenging. Developed by Genrobotic Innovations, the robot operates using Cable-Driven Parallel Robotics along with a biomimetic claw that can grasp and remove irregular debris from canals. The initiative is part of the Swachh Bharat Mission–Urban 2.0, aiming to achieve completely human-free cleaning of the Amayizhanchan canal. The robot is equipped with artificial intelligence that identifies different types of waste in real time and transfers the extracted materials directly to collection vehicles, ensuring safer and more efficient waste management while preventing human exposure to toxic substances.

questions.question: Which accessibility technology company announced a global partnership with Accessibility Cloud to deliver AI-augmented, human-centred accessibility solutions at scale?
questions.explanation: AccessifyLabs, an accessibility technology company, announced a global strategic partnership with Accessibility Cloud, a Switzerland-based platform that provides detailed accessibility data through its API. The collaboration aims to improve digital accessibility by combining AI-driven automation with human-centered validation and remediation. AccessifyLabs contributes advanced tools such as AI-powered accessibility scanning, automated fixes, continuous monitoring, enterprise dashboards, and compliance reporting. Accessibility Cloud brings community-sourced data and API-based infrastructure that helps integrate accessibility information into websites, mobile applications, and physical-digital systems. Together, the partnership seeks to help organizations meet international accessibility standards such as WCAG 2.2, EN 301 549, ADA, and Section 508 while ensuring inclusive digital experiences.

questions.question: In which city did Shri Sanjay Seth inaugurate the Advanced Aadhaar Seva Kendra on March 6, 2026?
questions.explanation: The Advanced Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) was inaugurated by Minister of State for Defence Shri Sanjay Seth on March 6, 2026, at Estate Plaza, Kantatoli in Ranchi. The centre was established to improve Aadhaar enrolment and update services by introducing upgraded hardware, high-speed connectivity, and better infrastructure. It aims to provide faster processing, greater transparency, and a more user-friendly experience for residents. Operated by Protean eGov Technologies Ltd under UIDAI guidelines, the centre supports both new enrolments and demographic or biometric updates. Its establishment also helps reduce waiting time and improve Aadhaar-related services for people in Ranchi and nearby districts.

questions.question: Which state has been announced as the host for the inaugural Khelo India Tribal Games 2026?
questions.explanation: The inaugural Khelo India Tribal Games 2026 will be hosted by the state of Chhattisgarh. The tournament is scheduled to take place from March 25 to April 6, 2026, across three major locations in the state of Raipur, Jagdalpur, and Sarguja. The event will feature seven medal sports, including athletics, football, hockey, weightlifting, archery, swimming, and wrestling, along with two demonstration sports, Mallakhamb and Kabaddi. The official mascot, named ‘Morveer’, symbolises the courage and cultural pride of India’s tribal communities. The games have also been declared an Event of National Importance and will follow international technical standards set by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.

questions.question: Which country approved the world’s first iPS stem-cell therapies for treating Parkinson’s disease and heart failure?
questions.explanation: Japan achieved a significant medical breakthrough by approving the world’s first commercial therapies derived from induced Pluripotent Stem (iPS) cells. One treatment, called Amchepry and developed by Sumitomo Pharma, focuses on Parkinson’s disease by transplanting stem-cell precursors into the brain to replace dopamine-producing cells that are lost in patients. Another therapy named ReHeart, developed by the startup Cuorips, uses heart muscle sheets to promote the formation of new blood vessels and improve heart function in patients with heart failure. These innovations build on the Nobel Prize-winning discovery of scientist Shinya Yamanaka in 2012, and both therapies are expected to become available to patients by Summer 2026.

questions.question: Which Indian cricketer became the first from India to be honoured with her own Barbie doll and join the Barbie Dream Team ahead of International Women’s Day 2026?
questions.explanation: Smriti Mandhana, the vice-captain of the Indian women’s cricket team, received a unique honour when Mattel created a Barbie doll in her likeness ahead of International Women’s Day on 8 March 2026. The doll was introduced as part of the Barbie Dream Team initiative and serves as a non-commercial tribute celebrating her achievements and role as an inspiration for young athletes. Mandhana has also made history by becoming the first Indian woman cricketer to score centuries in all three international formats—Tests, One Day Internationals, and T20 Internationals. This recognition highlights the growing global influence of Indian women’s cricket and encourages more girls to pursue sports professionally.

questions.question: What is the name of the Indian Navy’s maritime security mission launched in 2019 to protect merchant ships and vital trade routes in the Gulf of Oman and the Strait of Hormuz?
questions.explanation: Operation Sankalp is a major maritime security mission launched by the Indian Navy on June 19, 2019, to safeguard Indian merchant vessels and ensure the security of vital sea routes in the Gulf of Oman and the Strait of Hormuz. The operation was initiated after several attacks on commercial ships raised global concerns about maritime safety in the region. Under this mission, Indian Navy warships such as frigates and destroyers maintain continuous deployment in strategic waters. The initiative also includes maritime surveillance and rapid response units like MARCOS commandos. Operation Sankalp plays a vital role in protecting India’s trade routes and securing energy supplies, as a large portion of India’s oil imports passes through these sensitive maritime corridors.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the Divyang Sahayak Portal, India’s first integrated online platform for disability welfare schemes?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra launched the Divyang Sahayak Portal as India’s first fully integrated digital platform designed to simplify access to welfare schemes for persons with disabilities. Introduced in February 2026, the portal allows users to apply for schemes online, track application status in real time, and receive benefits through a transparent and streamlined process. The platform eliminates the need for physical paperwork and visits to government offices. It integrates with systems such as Aadhaar, the Unique Disability ID (UDID), and Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to ensure efficient service delivery. The portal also uses AI-based tools for grievance redressal and document verification, supporting the effective implementation of the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.

questions.question: Who created history by scoring the fastest century in T20 World Cup history with a 33-ball hundred in the semi-final against South Africa?
questions.explanation: Finn Allen, the explosive New Zealand opener, made history by scoring the fastest century in T20 World Cup history with a remarkable 33-ball hundred during the semi-final against South Africa in Kolkata. This extraordinary innings broke the previous men’s record of a 47-ball century set by Chris Gayle in the 2016 T20 World Cup. Allen also became the first player ever to score a century in a T20 World Cup knockout match. His innings featured a strike rate above 300 and included 18 boundaries, equaling the record for the most boundaries in a men’s T20 World Cup innings. The powerful performance helped New Zealand dominate the match and secure a place in the tournament final.

questions.question: In which U.S. state was the first Mahatma Gandhi bust unveiled at the University of Montana’s Mike Mansfield Centre?
questions.explanation: The first bust of Mahatma Gandhi in the U.S. state of Montana was unveiled at the Mike Mansfield Centre at the University of Montana in Missoula on March 4, 2026. The statue was presented as a gift by the Government of India and symbolises Gandhi’s philosophy of peace, non-violence, and moral leadership. The unveiling ceremony was attended by Montana Governor Greg Gianforte, India’s Consul General in Seattle Prakash Gupta, and several other dignitaries. The installation highlights the growing cultural and diplomatic ties between India and the United States, particularly at the state level, and emphasises the role of educational institutions in promoting international understanding and cooperation.

questions.question: Which university signed an MoU with the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to promote accessibility and inclusion for persons with disabilities?
questions.explanation: Pondicherry University signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Office of the Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities under the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. The collaboration aims to strengthen accessibility, promote inclusion, and support the rights of persons with disabilities through academic and policy engagement. The agreement focuses on conducting joint research on disability rights, accessibility standards, inclusive education, employment opportunities, and social integration. It also encourages academic exchanges, conferences, workshops, and awareness programs to promote understanding of the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 and to build capacity for creating a more inclusive society.

questions.question: Who was appointed as President of Jio Platforms to lead the company’s international expansion efforts?
questions.explanation: Dan Bailey, a veteran in the telecommunications industry with more than 35 years of experience, was appointed as the President of Jio Platforms to lead its global expansion initiatives. Based in London, he reports directly to Akash Ambani, Chairman of Reliance Jio Infocomm Limited, and is responsible for driving the company’s international business strategies. Bailey has previously held senior positions at major financial institutions such as Schroders/Citi, Morgan Stanley, HSBC, and Deutsche Bank, where he chaired the Technology, Media, and Telecom practice. His appointment reflects Jio’s commitment to expanding its digital platforms and telecom services beyond India as part of its long-term global growth strategy.

questions.question: Who assumed charge as the Chairman and Managing Director of GAIL (India) Limited on March 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: Deepak Gupta assumed charge as the Chairman and Managing Director (CMD) of GAIL (India) Limited on March 1, 2026, succeeding Sandeep Kumar Gupta, who retired on February 28, 2026. His tenure is scheduled to continue until February 28, 2029. Prior to this role, he served as Director (Projects) at GAIL from February 2022. A mechanical engineer from Delhi College of Engineering, he has extensive experience in natural gas transmission, LNG infrastructure, and large-scale energy projects. Before joining GAIL, he worked for over three decades at Engineers India Limited. His leadership is expected to support the expansion of India’s natural gas infrastructure and strengthen the country’s long-term energy strategy.

questions.question: Which Indian state proposed a ban on social media use for children under the age of 16 in its Budget 2026–27?
questions.explanation: Karnataka proposed a policy in its Budget 2026–27 to ban social media usage for children under the age of 16. The announcement was made by Chief Minister Siddaramaiah as part of efforts to address growing concerns about digital addiction and exposure to harmful online content among children. The proposal aims to create a safer digital environment by limiting unrestricted access to social media platforms. Policymakers and experts believe that excessive screen time and exposure to inappropriate content can negatively affect children’s mental health, academic performance, and overall well-being. If implemented, the measure would make Karnataka the first Indian state to introduce such a restriction, while other governments are also considering similar regulations.

questions.question: Which country will host the Bharat Steel 2026 summit starting from April 16, 2026?
questions.explanation: India will host the Bharat Steel 2026 summit in New Delhi beginning on April 16, 2026. The two-day international event will bring together leaders from the global steel, mining, technology, and trading sectors to discuss the development of the next-generation steel ecosystem. The summit aims to strengthen collaboration between policymakers and industry leaders while promoting sustainable and green steel production. It will also highlight technological innovation and encourage global investment in the steel sector. As one of the world’s largest steel producers, India aims to use this summit as a platform to enhance its position in the global steel market and promote environmentally responsible industrial growth.

questions.question: On which date is International Women’s Day celebrated worldwide every year?
questions.explanation: International Women’s Day is celebrated globally on March 8 each year to recognize the achievements and contributions of women in various fields such as education, politics, science, sports, and business. The day also promotes gender equality and women’s empowerment across societies. The 2026 celebration highlights themes like “Give To Gain” and “Rights. Justice. Action.” which emphasize cooperation, fairness, and equal opportunities for women and girls. International Women’s Day also draws attention to ongoing challenges, including equal pay, access to education, healthcare, safety, and representation in leadership roles. By raising awareness and encouraging collective action, the day supports progress toward a more inclusive and equitable world for women.

questions.question: Who was approved by the Reserve Bank of India as the interim CEO of Fino Payments Bank?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India approved Ketan Merchant as the interim Chief Executive Officer of Fino Payments Bank for a temporary period of three months. The decision was taken after the arrest of the bank’s Managing Director and CEO, Rishi Gupta, in connection with an alleged GST-related case. Ketan Merchant was serving as the Chief Financial Officer of the bank at the time of his appointment. His role as interim CEO ensures leadership continuity and smooth functioning of the bank’s operations during the transition period. The appointment allows the bank’s board and regulatory authorities to review the situation and decide on the long-term leadership arrangement.

questions.question: Which bank launched the specialised “YES Essence Women’s Salary Account” designed to meet the financial and lifestyle needs of working women in India?
questions.explanation: YES Bank introduced the “YES Essence Women’s Salary Account” as a dedicated banking product aimed at supporting the financial, health, and lifestyle needs of working women. The account integrates multiple benefits such as a complimentary safe deposit locker for the first year, ₹5 lakh top-up health insurance coverage for the first year, and an annual preventive health check-up to promote wellness. It also provides an Elegance Debit Card offering cashback benefits on lifestyle categories like shopping, dining, and wellness. Through this initiative, the bank seeks to empower women with convenient financial services while also supporting their long-term financial security and overall well-being.

questions.question: Which company launched the campaign “Chuniye Bharosa, Apno Sa” and appointed Pankaj Tripathi as its brand ambassador?
questions.explanation: SBI General Insurance launched the campaign titled “Chuniye Bharosa, Apno Sa” and appointed well-known actor Pankaj Tripathi as its brand ambassador. The campaign highlights the company’s focus on building trust, reliability, and emotional connection with customers. Through this initiative, the insurer aims to position itself as a dependable partner that provides family-like protection and reassurance to policyholders. The messaging focuses on the importance of choosing an insurance provider that people can trust in times of need. By associating with Pankaj Tripathi, known for his authenticity and relatability, the company seeks to strengthen its brand image and connect more deeply with Indian consumers across diverse segments.

questions.question: Who became the first Indian economist to receive the University of Chicago Alumni Award for Professional Achievement?
questions.explanation: KV Subramanian became the first Indian economist to receive the prestigious University of Chicago Alumni Award for Professional Achievement in the award’s 85-year history. The honour recognises his significant contributions to economic policy and research. During his tenure as Chief Economic Adviser to the Government of India from 2018 to 2021, he authored three influential Economic Surveys that provided key insights into India’s economic strategy, emphasising competitive markets, policy autonomy, and inclusive growth. He has also served as Executive Director for India at the International Monetary Fund, focusing on issues affecting emerging markets. Currently, he works as a Professor of Finance at the Indian School of Business (ISB).

questions.question: In which city was the 9th International Santal Conference addressed by President Droupadi Murmu, held on 7 March 2026?
questions.explanation: The 9th International Santal Conference took place in Darjeeling, West Bengal, on 7 March 2026 and was addressed by President Droupadi Murmu. The event highlighted the cultural heritage and historical contributions of the Santal community. During the conference, the President recalled important moments in Santal history such as the revolt led by Tilka Majhi and the famous Santal Hul of 1855 led by Sido, Kanhu, Chand, and Bhairav along with their sisters Phoolo and Jhano. She also emphasised the significance of the Santali language, the Ol Chiki script invented by Pandit Raghunath Murmu, and encouraged Santal youth to preserve their culture while embracing modern opportunities.

questions.question: How many members are part of the high-level committee constituted by the Government of India to propose reforms for the Special Economic Zones (SEZ) policy?
questions.explanation: The Government of India formed a high-level committee to recommend comprehensive reforms for the Special Economic Zones (SEZ) policy with the aim of developing a modern SEZ 2.0 framework. This committee consists of 17 members representing key government institutions and policy bodies. Members include representatives from the Ministry of Commerce, the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC), the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), and NITI Aayog, along with other stakeholders. The panel’s role is to review the SEZ Act of 2005, identify gaps, align SEZ policies with export promotion schemes, evaluate recent reforms, and address operational challenges faced by SEZ developers and units.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the 55th Chief Justice of the Madras High Court by the Central Government in 2026?
questions.explanation: Justice SA Dharmadhikari was appointed as the 55th Chief Justice of the Madras High Court in 2026 after the retirement of Justice Manindra Mohan Srivastava on March 5, 2026. The oath of office was administered by Tamil Nadu Governor RN Ravi. Justice Dharmadhikari began his legal career after enrolling as an advocate in 1992 and primarily practised before the Madhya Pradesh High Court. He also served as standing counsel for the Union of India. In 2016, he was appointed as an Additional Judge of the Madhya Pradesh High Court and later confirmed as a Permanent Judge. In 2025, he was transferred to the Kerala High Court before being elevated as Chief Justice of the Madras High Court in 2026.

questions.question: Under which Government of India initiative was the NHCX Hackathon organised by the National Health Authority?
options: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana Portal
questions.explanation: The NHCX Hackathon was organised by the National Health Authority as part of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), a flagship initiative of the Government of India aimed at creating a comprehensive digital health ecosystem. The hackathon encouraged innovators to develop open-source solutions to streamline and standardise health insurance claims through the National Health Claims Exchange (NHCX). It focused on improving interoperability, converting legacy systems to FHIR standards, and structuring clinical data for efficient claims processing. ABDM also includes important digital components such as ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) and the Health Facility Registry, which together aim to enhance digital healthcare infrastructure, improve transparency, and ensure seamless access to health services across India.

questions.question: In which city did the Indian Army’s Central Command organise its first-ever Strategic Communication Conclave on 7 March 2026?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army’s Central Command organised its inaugural Strategic Communication Conclave on 7 March 2026 in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh. The event marked an important initiative to strengthen strategic communication as a key capability within India’s national security framework. Around 500 participants attended the conclave, including senior military officers, diplomats, government communication experts, media professionals, and specialists from the private sector. Discussions focused on the role of communication in managing the modern information environment shaped by digital platforms, social media, disinformation campaigns, and hybrid threats. The conclave also featured expert sessions and speeches by distinguished personalities, including retired diplomats and senior military officials.

questions.question: What is India’s global rank in the Hurun Global Rich List 2026 in terms of the number of billionaires?
questions.explanation: India achieved the third position globally in the Hurun Global Rich List 2026 with a total of 308 billionaires. This ranking places India behind only the United States and China, reflecting the rapid growth of wealth creation in the country. The combined wealth of Indian billionaires is estimated at around ₹112 lakh crore. Sectors such as renewable energy, consumer internet, fintech, pharmaceuticals, and infrastructure have significantly contributed to this rise. Prominent Indian billionaires include Mukesh Ambani, Gautam Adani, Roshni Nadar Malhotra, Cyrus S. Poonawalla, and Kumar Mangalam Birla. The report highlights India’s increasing influence in the global wealth landscape and its growing entrepreneurial ecosystem.

questions.question: Who became the first woman to be appointed as Chief Superintendent of a functional nuclear power plant in India?
questions.explanation: RM Nachammai created history by becoming the first woman to serve as the Chief Superintendent of an operational nuclear power plant in India. She assumed charge of Units 3 and 4 at the Kaiga Generating Station in Karnataka on 15 March 2026 under the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd (NPCIL). In this role, she is responsible for overseeing critical functions such as operations, maintenance, and engineering support at the reactor complex. The Kaiga plant is known for its strong safety standards and consistent performance in nuclear power generation. Her appointment marks a significant milestone for gender representation in India’s highly technical and traditionally male-dominated nuclear energy sector.

questions.question: Which organisation launched the National Climate Stack Innovation Challenge with a top prize of ₹15 lakh to promote climate resilience solutions for rural India?
options: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
questions.explanation: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) launched the National Climate Stack Innovation Challenge to encourage technological and scientific solutions aimed at improving climate resilience in rural India. The initiative focuses on developing near-term climate hazard forecasting models and user-friendly dashboards that can support agriculture and rural livelihoods. It is built upon NABARD’s existing climate data platform, DiCRA (Data in Climate Resilient Agriculture), which aims to evolve into a comprehensive digital public infrastructure for climate intelligence. The challenge is organised in partnership with the Gates Foundation and Dalberg Advisors and offers prizes of ₹15 lakh, ₹10 lakh, and ₹5 lakh to promote innovative and scalable climate solutions.
