questions.question: Which territory has been the centre of recurring diplomatic tensions between the United States and Denmark that contributed to NATO’s Arctic Sentry mission framework?
questions.explanation: Greenland is an autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark and holds immense strategic importance in the Arctic region. Its geographic position makes it vital for monitoring polar routes, missile defence systems, and military operations in the High North. The United States has historically maintained a military presence there, particularly through installations used for early warning and space surveillance. Because of its geopolitical significance, discussions about influence, security cooperation, and strategic control have occasionally created diplomatic tensions between the United States and Denmark. These tensions, combined with growing Arctic competition, contributed to NATO strengthening its Arctic security posture through initiatives such as the Arctic Sentry mission framework.

questions.question: In which Indian state were sambar deer recorded at unusually high altitudes for the first time in protected wildlife sanctuaries?
questions.explanation: Sambar deer, the largest deer species in South Asia, were recorded at unusually high elevations in the Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh. Traditionally, these deer are found in the lower Shivalik hills, moist deciduous forests, and regions below about 2,000 metres. However, camera trap images documented their presence in higher-altitude areas such as the Kalatop–Khajjiar Wildlife Sanctuary and the Gamgul Wildlife Sanctuary, where elevations exceed 2,500 to 3,000 metres. This observation is significant because it suggests possible shifts in wildlife distribution patterns. Factors such as climate change, habitat changes, and improved protection of forest areas may be influencing the movement and habitat range of species like the sambar deer.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff of India, effective from April 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth has been appointed as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff (VCOAS) of the Indian Army with effect from April 1, 2026. Before this appointment, he served as the General Officer Commanding-in-Chief (GOC-in-C) of the Southern Command based in Pune. The Southern Command is one of the largest geographical commands of the Indian Army and is responsible for military operations and infrastructure across several states in peninsular India. With extensive experience in operational, staff, and command roles, Lt Gen Seth succeeded Lieutenant General PP Singh in this prestigious position. The role of the Vice Chief of the Army Staff is the second-highest rank in the Indian Army hierarchy, just below the Chief of the Army Staff.

questions.question: Which Indian Institute of Technology signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Coventry University Group (UK) to strengthen collaboration in Artificial Intelligence and healthcare research?
questions.explanation: IIT Guwahati signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Coventry University Group (UK) to enhance collaboration in research, innovation, and academic exchange. The partnership focuses particularly on advancing developments in Artificial Intelligence and healthcare through joint initiatives. The agreement includes provisions for academic and research mobility, joint doctoral programmes, and collaborative research projects. A major theme of the partnership is “AI for One Health,” which aims to integrate artificial intelligence with healthcare systems to address societal health challenges. This collaboration builds on the earlier AI Impact Pre-Summit event hosted in January 2026, which emphasised using AI to strengthen healthcare integration and interdisciplinary innovation.

questions.question: In which city did Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurate India’s first fully operational Ring Metro along with a key stretch of the Magenta Line?
questions.explanation: New Delhi witnessed the inauguration of India’s first fully operational Ring Metro with the completion of the Pink Line of the Delhi Metro network. The Majlis Park–Maujpur-Babarpur section completed the entire circular corridor of about 71.56 km, enabling seamless connectivity across various parts of the city without requiring frequent line changes. The project includes significant infrastructure such as a new bridge over the Yamuna River and a double-decker viaduct that carries both metro tracks and a road flyover. Alongside this, a key stretch of the Magenta Line was also inaugurated to improve connectivity in North Delhi. These developments aim to reduce congestion, improve commuting efficiency, and strengthen the capital’s public transportation system.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the head coach of the Sri Lanka Men’s national cricket team starting from April 15, 2026?
questions.explanation: Gary Kirsten, a former South African international cricketer and experienced coach, has been appointed as the head coach of the Sri Lanka Men’s national cricket team beginning on April 15, 2026, for a two-year term ending on April 14, 2028. He is widely respected for his calm leadership style and strong emphasis on team discipline and mental conditioning. Kirsten previously achieved major coaching success with India, guiding the team to victory in the 2011 ICC Men’s Cricket World Cup and helping them reach the No.1 Test ranking. He also coached South Africa to the No.1 ranking across all three formats in 2013, making him one of cricket’s most accomplished coaches.

questions.question: Which financial institution launched the ‘Spoorthi’ programme to provide specialised home loans and loans against property for women entrepreneurs?
questions.explanation: L&T Finance introduced the ‘Spoorthi’ programme to support and empower women entrepreneurs by offering specialised financial products such as home loans and loans against property (LAP). The initiative aims to encourage women-led businesses and improve access to credit for women who are running or co-running enterprises or professional ventures. Under the programme, eligible applicants can benefit from extended loan tenures of up to 25 years for home loans and LAP, along with flexible eligibility norms such as higher debt-to-income ratios. These features are designed to provide greater financial flexibility, enabling women entrepreneurs to invest in property ownership, expand businesses, and strengthen their economic participation.

questions.question: Which Indian Union Territory was reviewed by Dr. Jitendra Singh for the progress of ocean-based desalination and sustainable energy projects?
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep, located in the Arabian Sea, faces persistent freshwater shortages due to limited groundwater reserves, salinity intrusion, and heavy reliance on seasonal rainfall. To address these challenges, the National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT) has implemented advanced solutions such as Low Temperature Thermal Desalination (LTTD) plants across several islands. These plants utilise the temperature difference between warm surface water and cold deep-sea water to produce drinking water. Additionally, India’s first Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC)-powered desalination facility is being developed in Kavaratti. This project aims to generate electricity and potable water simultaneously, reduce dependence on diesel-based power, and enhance long-term water and energy security for the islands.

questions.question: In which country is the joint military exercise “LAMITIYE-2026” being conducted with the participation of the Indian Armed Forces?
questions.explanation: The 11th edition of the joint military exercise “LAMITIYE-2026” is being conducted in Seychelles with the participation of the Indian Armed Forces. The exercise aims to strengthen bilateral defence cooperation, improve interoperability between the two countries, and enhance operational coordination. “Lamitiye,” meaning “Friendship” in the Creole language, reflects the spirit of collaboration between India and Seychelles. The 2026 edition is significant because it includes participation from all three branches of the Indian Armed Forces—Army, Navy, and Air Force. The training program focuses on sub-conventional operations in semi-urban environments, peacekeeping operations, tactical drills, and joint planning exercises conducted over a 12-day period.

questions.question: Which bank partnered with Swiggy to launch the co-branded credit cards BLCK and Ornge, offering savings of up to ₹48,000 annually?
questions.explanation: Swiggy partnered with HDFC Bank to introduce two new co-branded credit cards named BLCK and Ornge. These cards are designed to provide rewards and benefits for customers who frequently use digital platforms for food delivery, grocery purchases, and online shopping. Cardholders can receive a combination of cashback and partner discounts that may lead to savings of up to ₹48,000 annually. The cards specifically target regular users of Swiggy’s services, such as food delivery, Instamart for groceries, and dining experiences. This collaboration between Swiggy and HDFC Bank reflects the growing integration of fintech solutions with digital consumer platforms to enhance convenience and value for users.

questions.question: Who took over as the interim CEO of IndiGo?
questions.explanation: Rahul Bhatia, the Founder and Managing Director of IndiGo, assumed the role of interim CEO after Pieter Elbers resigned on March 10, 2026. The resignation followed a major operational crisis in December 2025 that resulted in over 5,000 flight cancellations and widespread passenger distress. The disruption was mainly due to severe crew shortages linked to the implementation of new Flight Duty Time Limitations (FDTL) rules. The situation also drew strong regulatory scrutiny from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). To address the crisis, Rahul Bhatia stepped in as interim CEO with the aim of restoring operational stability, strengthening company culture, and ensuring better operational performance and reliability for the airline.

questions.question: Where was the International Malaria Conference 2026 held by the ICMR–National Institute of Malaria Research to discuss strategies for malaria elimination?
questions.explanation: The International Malaria Conference 2026 was organized by the ICMR–National Institute of Malaria Research in New Delhi. The three-day conference took place from March 7 to 9, 2026, and brought together scientists, researchers, and public health experts from around the world. The theme of the event was “Discovery, Development and Delivery: Driving Malaria Elimination and Beyond.” Discussions focused on parasite biology, transmission patterns, diagnostic innovations, surveillance technologies, and strategies to translate research findings into public health interventions. The conference also encouraged participation from early-career researchers through turbo talks and poster presentations, promoting collaboration and knowledge sharing in the global fight against malaria.

questions.question: In which city did the Ministry of Panchayati Raj organise the national conference to celebrate one year of the Sashakt Panchayat-Netri Abhiyan?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Panchayati Raj organised a national conference in New Delhi to commemorate one year of the Sashakt Panchayat-Netri Abhiyan, an initiative aimed at strengthening women’s leadership in grassroots governance. The conference brought together Women Elected Representatives (WERs) from across the country and highlighted their contributions to rural development and decision-making at the local level. During the event, key dignitaries, including the Minister of Panchayati Raj and the Minister of Women and Child Development, participated and recognised outstanding women leaders. The conference also showcased achievements such as training more than 7.18 lakh women representatives and identifying 744 Model Women Friendly Gram Panchayats across several states and union territories.

questions.question: Which country is the headquarters of the automation machinery manufacturer ABB that announced a $75 million investment to expand manufacturing and R&D operations in India in 2026?
questions.explanation: ABB is a global automation and electrification company headquartered in Switzerland. The company announced a $75 million investment in India for 2026 as part of its “local-for-local” strategy, which focuses on strengthening domestic manufacturing and research capabilities. This investment builds on a previous $35 million investment in 2025 and contributes to more than $230 million invested in India over the last decade. The expansion will enhance facilities across multiple locations, including Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Nashik, and Vadodara. These investments will support innovation in areas such as sustainable mobility, high-speed rail propulsion, power technologies, and advanced research while also creating around 300 new skilled engineering and research jobs in India.

questions.question: Where will the MSME Symposium 2026, organised by the Indian Foundation for Quality Management, be held on March 20, 2026?
questions.explanation: The MSME Symposium 2026, organised by the Indian Foundation for Quality Management (IFQM), is scheduled to take place on March 20, 2026, at Anna University in Chennai. The event is focused on strengthening India’s Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) and enhancing their global competitiveness. With the theme “India Needs a Resurgent MSME Sector,” the symposium aims to encourage the integration of Indian MSMEs into Global Value Chains (GVCs) as high-tech manufacturing partners. The program will feature discussions by prominent industry leaders such as Venu Srinivasan of TVS Motor Company and Arvind Balaji of Lucas TVS, highlighting innovation, quality, and sustainable growth in the MSME sector.

questions.question: Who was named the new Supreme Leader of Iran by the Assembly of Experts following the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei?
questions.explanation: Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as the new Supreme Leader of Iran by the Assembly of Experts after the assassination of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. At the age of 56, he assumed the country’s highest religious and political authority during a period of significant regional tension and conflict involving the United States and Israel. His appointment quickly received support from powerful institutions such as the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian armed forces, which played a crucial role in stabilising the leadership transition. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian described the decision as a symbol of national unity and the beginning of a new era for the nation.

questions.question: Which country officially ratified the Framework Agreement of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), strengthening global cooperation in renewable energy?
questions.explanation: Romania officially ratified the Framework Agreement of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), a global initiative aimed at promoting the widespread adoption of solar energy. The Instrument of Ratification was presented by Ms. Sena Latif to Shri P.S. Gangadhar at the Ministry of External Affairs in India. The International Solar Alliance, originally initiated by India, focuses on increasing solar energy deployment worldwide and facilitating cooperation in technology transfer, research, and financing for solar projects. By joining the framework agreement, Romania strengthens international collaboration in clean energy and supports global efforts to address climate change while promoting sustainable development and a transition toward renewable energy sources.

questions.question: Which country served as the Partner Country for the 40th edition of AAHAR 2026 held at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi?
questions.explanation: The 40th edition of AAHAR 2026, a major B2B trade exhibition for the food and hospitality industry, was inaugurated by Union Minister Piyush Goyal at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi. Organised by the India Trade Promotion Organisation (ITPO), the event took place from March 10 to 14, 2026. Italy participated as the Partner Country for this edition, emphasising stronger collaboration between India and the European Union in sectors such as gourmet foods, hospitality technology, and food processing innovations. The exhibition featured around 1,800 exhibitors from 17 countries and showcased advancements in packaging technology, cold chain solutions, and hospitality infrastructure.

questions.question: Which organisation launched the Bug Bounty Program to strengthen the digital protection of Aadhaar systems?
questions.explanation: The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) launched its first structured Bug Bounty Program to enhance the security of the Aadhaar digital ecosystem. The initiative invites ethical hackers and cybersecurity researchers to identify vulnerabilities in important digital platforms such as the UIDAI official website, the myAadhaar portal, and the Secure QR Code system. A panel of 20 cybersecurity experts has been selected to conduct systematic testing of these platforms and report any weaknesses. Rewards will be provided depending on the severity of the vulnerabilities discovered. The program aims to strengthen digital identity protection and ensure that sensitive personal data linked to Aadhaar remains secure from emerging cyber threats.

questions.question: Which country officially withdrew from the FIFA World Cup 2026 amid rising geopolitical tensions with the United States and Israel?
questions.explanation: Iran officially announced its withdrawal from the FIFA World Cup 2026, which is scheduled to be hosted jointly by the United States, Canada, and Mexico. The decision was confirmed by Iran’s Sports Minister Ahmad Donyamali and is linked to escalating geopolitical tensions involving Iran, the United States, and Israel. Prior to the withdrawal, Iran had successfully qualified for the tournament and was placed in Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand. The group stage matches were scheduled to take place in cities such as Los Angeles and Seattle in the United States. Iran’s withdrawal has created discussions globally about the influence of political conflicts on international sporting events and competitions.

questions.question: Which organisation partnered with the Directorate General Resettlement (DGR) to launch the fourth batch of the Directors' Certification in Corporate Governance for Defence Officers?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Corporate Affairs (IICA), in collaboration with the Directorate General Resettlement (DGR), launched the fourth batch of the Directors' Certification in Corporate Governance for Defence Officers. The program began on 9th March 2026 at the IICA campus in Manesar, Gurugram. It is a two-week certification course designed for senior serving and recently retired officers from the Indian Armed Forces. The initiative aims to prepare them for effective roles as board members in public and private sector companies. The curriculum focuses on corporate governance principles, regulatory frameworks, and the responsibilities of independent directors while utilising the officers’ expertise in leadership, strategy, cybersecurity, and project management.

questions.question: Which airport has been officially declared as an International Airport by the Union Cabinet?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, approved the declaration of Madurai Airport in Tamil Nadu as an International Airport. Madurai Airport is among the oldest airports in the state and serves as an important gateway to Southern Tamil Nadu. Granting international status is expected to improve regional and global connectivity, facilitating greater trade and economic activity. The decision will also boost tourism and pilgrimage since Madurai is widely known as the “Temple City” and is home to several historically and culturally significant sites. Increased international flights are likely to attract more visitors, businesses, and investors, contributing to the overall economic and infrastructure development of the region.

questions.question: Who has assumed charge as a Member of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC)?
questions.explanation: Dr. Partha Biswas has taken charge as a Member of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC), a constitutional body responsible for safeguarding the rights and interests of Scheduled Castes in India. He holds a Ph.D. in Defence and Strategic Studies and has pursued specialized studies in Human Resources and Human Rights at reputed institutions such as the University of Calcutta, Jadavpur University, and Kanpur University. He also received a Post-Doctoral Fellowship from the Indian Council of Social Science Research at Jawaharlal Nehru University in New Delhi. Dr. Biswas has contributed significantly to academic research through books, publications, and journal articles focusing on defence policy, strategic affairs, and international relations.

questions.question: On which date will World Kidney Day be observed in 2026?
questions.explanation: World Kidney Day 2026 will be observed on March 12, highlighting the importance of kidney health and raising awareness about chronic kidney disease (CKD). This global awareness event is observed every year on the second Thursday of March and encourages people to focus on prevention, early detection, and proper management of kidney-related diseases. The theme for 2026 is “Kidney Health for All – Caring for People, Protecting the Planet,” which emphasises both human health and environmental sustainability. Various organizations, hospitals, and health institutions around the world conduct screening programs, awareness campaigns, and educational initiatives to promote healthy lifestyles and reduce the growing burden of kidney diseases globally.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the Indian Orchid Festival 2026 held after four days of exhibitions and expert sessions?
questions.explanation: The Indian Orchid Festival 2026 concluded in the Pakyong district of Sikkim after four days of activities that included exhibitions, expert discussions, and participation from farmers and entrepreneurs. The event was organised by the ICAR-National Research Centre for Orchids with the theme “Orchids for Beauty & Prosperity.” It brought together orchid growers, nursery owners, researchers, and floriculture experts from across the country. The festival promoted orchid cultivation, sustainable horticulture practices, and floriculture entrepreneurship. Participants exchanged knowledge on modern orchid farming techniques, breeding innovations, and commercial opportunities. Such initiatives support the growth of India’s floriculture industry and highlight the rich orchid diversity found particularly in the northeastern and Himalayan regions.

questions.question: In which city will the NXT Conclave 2026 be held from 12th to 14th March 2026?
questions.explanation: The NXT Conclave 2026 is scheduled to take place from 12th to 14th March 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, which is located in New Delhi, the capital city of India. The event is designed as a global platform bringing together leaders, policymakers, and thinkers from across the world. Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended the conclave as the Chief Guest and emphasised India’s growing role in global innovation and collaboration. Several prominent international leaders, including former prime ministers from Australia, Sweden, and Nepal, are participating as Guests of Honour. The conclave aims to connect global expertise with India’s strengths to shape initiatives for a sustainable and inclusive future for humankind.

questions.question: Which organisation, along with the Indian Navy, conducted successful release trials of the indigenous Air Droppable Container ‘ADC-150’?
questions.explanation: The Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), in collaboration with the Indian Navy, successfully carried out four in-flight release trials of the indigenous Air Droppable Container (ADC-150). The trials were conducted from a P8I aircraft off the coast of Goa between February 21 and March 01, 2026, under various extreme release conditions. The ADC-150 is designed to carry and deliver a payload of 150 kg to naval ships operating in distant waters. This system enables rapid delivery of essential supplies such as equipment, critical stores, and medical aid to vessels in distress. The Naval Science & Technological Laboratory in Visakhapatnam served as the nodal laboratory, with support from other DRDO units for parachute systems, certification, and instrumentation.

questions.question: Who created history by completing the Full Ironman New Zealand triathlon in 11 hours and 50 minutes?
questions.explanation: Anant Rana, a 33-year-old athlete from Jamshedpur, achieved a remarkable milestone by successfully completing the Full Ironman New Zealand triathlon in 11 hours and 50 minutes. The challenging endurance event took place in Taupo, New Zealand, and includes three demanding segments: a 3.8 km swim, a 180 km cycling race, and a 42.2 km marathon run. Rana completed the swimming segment in 1 hour 28 minutes, the cycling portion in 6 hours 19 minutes, and the marathon in 3 hours 46 minutes. Finishing well within the official 17-hour time limit, he demonstrated exceptional endurance and determination. He is associated with the Tata Steel Adventure Foundation and resides in Sonari, Jamshedpur.

questions.question: Which Indian state has earmarked Rs 1,331 crore to upgrade an Olympic-ready city as part of India’s bid to host the 2036 Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The state government of Gujarat has allocated Rs 1,331 crore to develop Ahmedabad into an Olympic-ready city, supporting India’s ambition to host the 2036 Olympic Games. This funding is part of a broader sports and youth activities budget exceeding Rs 2,000 crore. A significant share of the investment will be directed toward infrastructure development at the SVP Sports Enclave located near the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The initiative also focuses on grassroots sports development through the ‘In-School’ programme, which trains more than 1.29 lakh children across 230 schools. These efforts aim to nurture young athletes and position Gujarat as a major international hub for sporting events and infrastructure.

questions.question: In which city did Bharat Forge inaugurate its state-of-the-art Landing Gear Components Machining Facility in collaboration with Liebherr-Aerospace & Transportation SAS?
questions.explanation: Bharat Forge inaugurated a specialised Landing Gear Components Machining Facility in Mundhwa, a locality in Pune, Maharashtra. The facility has been developed in collaboration with Liebherr-Aerospace & Transportation SAS and represents a significant advancement in India’s aerospace manufacturing capabilities. It is among the first facilities in the country to operate OEM-approved landing gear machining at scale. Equipped with high-precision machining centres, the plant will manufacture critical aerospace components. This development strengthens India’s position in the global aerospace supply chain. With this facility, Bharat Forge expands its aerospace portfolio, which already includes aero-engine components, airframe structures, and landing-gear sub-systems, with future plans for an advanced aerospace ring-mill.

questions.question: Which bank launched the “Bob Women Sapphire” savings account offering lifestyle, health, and wellness benefits for women?
questions.explanation: Bank of Baroda introduced the “bob Women Sapphire” savings account to provide exclusive financial and lifestyle benefits for women customers. The account requires maintaining a Monthly Average Balance of ₹1 lakh and includes several health and wellness features. These benefits include a ₹10 lakh cancer care coverage plan, complimentary online doctor consultations for up to six family members, and discounts on medicines and pathology services. Customers also receive the Bob Bhoomi RuPay Select Debit Card, which provides privileges such as airport lounge access, gym memberships, spa or salon services, and OTT subscriptions, along with additional concessions on loans and locker charges.

questions.question: What is the name of India’s first drone capable of operating both in the air and underwater using advanced sonar technology?
questions.explanation: AVATAAR amphibious drone is India’s first drone designed to operate both in the air and underwater. It was developed by AquaAirX Autonomous Systems and features a carbon-fibre structure that provides high resistance to corrosion in marine environments. The drone uses advanced sonar systems, including multibeam imaging sonar, to map the seabed and detect underwater objects. It also includes an acoustic communication modem and an Ultra-Short Baseline navigation system for precise positioning during missions. The name AVATAAR stands for Amphibious Vehicle for Autonomous Tactical Aerial & Aquatic Reconnaissance and is intended for surveillance, reconnaissance, and security operations around ports, harbors, and coastal regions.

questions.question: Which Indian state has launched the ‘Lake and Air Watch’ initiative for real-time monitoring of urban air quality and water bodies?
questions.explanation: Gujarat launched the ‘Lake and Air Watch’ initiative through the Gujarat Urban Development Mission to monitor environmental conditions in urban areas. The program uses satellite technology and advanced sensors to track air quality and the condition of city lakes, including pollution levels, siltation, and algae growth. Real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) data will be gathered from monitoring stations across 17 municipal corporations and 152 municipalities. A centralized digital dashboard will continuously update environmental data and generate alerts if pollution levels or lake conditions change significantly, helping authorities respond quickly and improving environmental transparency for citizens.

questions.question: Which company signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) to support industry–start-up collaboration in cooling and smart appliance technologies?
questions.explanation: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Voltas Limited to promote collaboration between industry and start-ups working in cooling and smart appliance technologies. The initiative aims to support innovation in areas such as HVAC systems, artificial intelligence, machine learning, power electronics, and advanced control technologies. Through programs like the Startup India initiative and the Bharat Start-up Grand Challenge, selected start-ups will receive mentorship, technical support, and access to testing infrastructure. The partnership also encourages the development of energy-efficient cooling solutions, IoT-enabled appliances, air-quality monitoring systems, and predictive maintenance technologies.

questions.question: Who won the 2026 Formula 1 season-opening Australian Grand Prix for Mercedes at Albert Park?
questions.explanation: George Russell won the 2026 season-opening Australian Grand Prix, delivering a strong performance for the Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One Team. Russell started the race from pole position and secured victory with a comfortable lead of about 2.9 seconds over his teammate, achieving his sixth career Formula 1 win. During the race, he briefly lost the lead to Charles Leclerc but regained it after a Ferrari strategy error during a Virtual Safety Car period. The win highlighted Mercedes’ impressive early pace in the 2026 season, making them strong contenders for the championship. Meanwhile, Lewis Hamilton finished fourth in his debut race for Ferrari.

questions.question: Which two countries were added to the FATF “grey list” for increased monitoring due to deficiencies in anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing frameworks in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF), an international body responsible for setting standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing, placed Kuwait and Papua New Guinea on its “grey list” for increased monitoring after its February 2026 plenary meeting. Countries on this list have strategic deficiencies in their financial monitoring systems and must implement specific action plans within a given timeframe to address these gaps. While the grey list expanded, the FATF “black list” remained unchanged and includes Iran, North Korea, and Myanmar. The monitoring aims to safeguard the global financial system while ensuring humanitarian financial flows are not disrupted.

questions.question: In which state has the Union Agriculture Minister approved the procurement of 1,25,855 metric tonnes of Rabi crops at MSP worth over ₹894 crore under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) for the 2026 season?
questions.explanation: The Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan approved the procurement of 1,25,855 metric tonnes of Rabi crops at Minimum Support Price (MSP) under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) in Telangana. The total procurement value is over ₹894 crore and aims to benefit thousands of farmers in the state. This initiative ensures farmers receive fair prices for their produce and are protected from market price fluctuations. By preventing distress sales and encouraging the production of pulses and oilseeds, the program supports agricultural growth, improves nutritional security, and contributes to strengthening the rural economy under the leadership of Narendra Modi.

questions.question: Which medical institution signed a Memorandum of Understanding with ISRO to collaborate on space medicine and research for future human space missions?
questions.explanation: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) signed a Framework Memorandum of Understanding with All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi, to advance research in space medicine for upcoming human space missions. The agreement was signed by Dinesh Kumar Singh, Director of the Human Space Flight Centre, and Srinivas M, Director of AIIMS, in the presence of ISRO Chairman V. Narayanan. The collaboration will focus on studying human health challenges in microgravity, including musculoskeletal atrophy, neuroscience, nutrition, immunity, and metabolic health. This partnership supports India’s long-term goals, such as developing the Bharatiya Antariksh Station and preparing for future crewed missions to the Moon.

questions.question: Which NASA spacecraft re-entered Earth’s atmosphere on March 11 after completing a long mission studying the radiation belts around Earth?
questions.explanation: Van Allen Probe A, part of NASA’s Van Allen Probes mission, re-entered Earth’s atmosphere on March 11 after nearly 14 years since its launch in 2012. The mission was conducted by NASA and managed by the Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory. The spacecraft studied the Van Allen radiation belts, which are regions of charged particles trapped by Earth’s magnetic field. One of the mission’s major discoveries was the identification of a temporary third radiation belt formed during intense solar activity. The data collected helps scientists understand space weather and its effects on satellites, astronauts, communication systems, and power grids on Earth.

questions.question: Which Indian company has partnered with the United States in a $300 billion project to build the “America First Refining” oil refinery at the Port of Brownsville, Texas?
questions.explanation: The United States announced a historic $300 billion partnership with Reliance Industries Ltd to build a major oil refinery called “America First Refining” at the Port of Brownsville in Texas, United States. The project was announced by Donald Trump and represents the first major refinery construction in the U.S. in about 50 years. It is expected to be one of the cleanest refineries in the world and the largest investment in U.S. history. The initiative aims to strengthen energy security, create thousands of jobs, and stabilize global oil markets amid disruptions in supply routes such as the Strait of Hormuz.

questions.question: Which Urban Local Body issued Maharashtra's first public Green Municipal Bonds listed on the National Stock Exchange?
questions.explanation: Nashik Municipal Corporation became the first Urban Local Body in Maharashtra to issue public Green Municipal Bonds and list them on the National Stock Exchange of India. These bonds are fixed-income debt instruments used by local governments to raise funds specifically for environmentally sustainable projects and climate-friendly initiatives. The listing ceremony was marked by Ashish Chauhan, Managing Director of NSE, who presented a replica of the NSE bell to Devendra Fadnavis. The funds generated from these bonds will support green infrastructure, sustainable urban development, and environmentally beneficial public projects in the city.

questions.question: Which digital payments company partnered with GoKwik to launch India’s first Integrated Conversion-to-Completion stack for D2C brands?
questions.explanation: PayU partnered with GoKwik to introduce India’s first Integrated Conversion-to-Completion stack aimed at improving the online shopping experience for Direct-to-Consumer (D2C) brands. The collaboration integrates GoKwik’s conversion intelligence with PayU’s advanced payment infrastructure to reduce revenue loss caused by checkout drop-offs and payment failures. This innovative solution helps merchants create a smoother and more reliable purchase journey for customers. By addressing common issues such as failed payments and abandoned carts, the integrated system supports businesses in scaling efficiently and maximizing revenue while ensuring a seamless transaction process for online shoppers.

questions.question: Which state’s cabinet approved the draft Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, aimed at curbing forced religious conversions?
questions.explanation: The Chhattisgarh Cabinet approved the draft of the Chhattisgarh Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, under the leadership of Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai. The bill aims to prevent religious conversions carried out through force, fraud, misrepresentation, allurement, or undue influence. It is designed to be stronger and more comprehensive than the existing Chhattisgarh Freedom of Religion Act of 1968. The law also focuses on addressing activities such as “Changai Sabha,” which are alleged to mislead people. The decision comes amid tensions in tribal areas like Bastar related to religious conversions. Along with this bill, the cabinet also approved measures related to recruitment reforms and abolishing the certain property registration cess.

questions.question: Which state will benefit from the MoU signed between Oil India Limited and NSTFDC to strengthen healthcare infrastructure in tribal areas?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh will benefit from the Memorandum of Understanding signed between Oil India Limited and the National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation to improve healthcare infrastructure in tribal and underserved regions. Under this initiative, ₹86.27 lakh has been allocated to upgrade facilities at Community Health Centres and Primary Health Centres in the Rajgarh district, which is recognised as an Aspirational District. The project focuses on enhancing healthcare access for tribal communities by providing essential medical equipment such as ventilators, CPAP machines, BiPAP machines, USG machines, ambulances, and other diagnostic tools to strengthen the public healthcare system in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Where was the International Rail Coach Expo (IRCE) 2026 inaugurated on March 12, 2026?
questions.explanation: ICF Chennai served as the venue for the International Rail Coach Expo (IRCE) 2026, which was inaugurated on March 12, 2026. The event was opened by U. Subba Rao, General Manager of the Integral Coach Factory, and organised in association with the Confederation of Indian Industry. The expo brought together railway manufacturers, technology providers, and industry experts from India and other countries. It showcased innovations in railway coach manufacturing, sustainability initiatives, advanced safety systems, and modern rail technologies. ICF Chennai plays a crucial role in India’s railway modernisation and is globally known for manufacturing passenger coaches and producing the Vande Bharat Express train sets.

questions.question: Honeywell partnered with which institution to establish the Centre of Excellence for Future Skills & Innovation in India?
questions.explanation: Honeywell partnered with the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay to establish the IITB-Honeywell Centre of Excellence for Future Skills & Innovation at the institute’s Powai campus. The centre is fully funded by the Honeywell Hometown Solutions India Foundation and focuses on advancing sustainability skills and innovation among students. The initiative aims to train more than 100,000 students by 2030 through specialised programs, research support, and advanced laboratory infrastructure. It will benefit undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral students from across India. The centre will also offer certificate-based programs, hands-on projects, and research grants to address real-world sustainability challenges.

questions.question: Which company unveiled an AI platform called Agent Studio to automate payment operations for businesses?
questions.explanation: Razorpay introduced an artificial intelligence platform known as Agent Studio to transform how businesses manage payment infrastructure. The platform is built on Anthropic’s Claude Agent SDK and enables companies to automate several payment-related operations using AI agents. These agents can intervene when customers abandon purchases, initiate conversations to encourage payment completion, and integrate with platforms like Shopify, Shiprocket, and WhatsApp to access customer and transaction data. The system also supports a no-code tool that allows businesses to create custom AI agents by defining tasks in plain language. Additionally, Razorpay launched an Agentic Experience Platform that allows payment operations to be handled using natural-language commands.

questions.question: Which country will host the second leg of the Asia Cup Archery in 2027 after a gap of 22 years?
questions.explanation: India has been selected to host the second leg of the Asia Cup Archery tournament in 2027, which will take place in Delhi. This marks an important moment for the country’s archery community because it will be the first major international archery event hosted by India in 22 years. The last major international archery competition organised in the country was the Asian Archery Championships held in Delhi in 2005. The decision was confirmed by World Archery Asia after discussions with the Archery Association of India. Hosting this event will provide Indian archers with a home advantage for ranking points and will also help promote and develop local archery talent.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer (MD & CEO) of HDFC ERGO General Insurance effective from April 16, 2026?
questions.explanation: Parthanil Ghosh has been appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of HDFC ERGO General Insurance, with the appointment becoming effective from April 16, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. He will succeed Anuj Tyagi, who stepped down from the role to pursue entrepreneurial opportunities. Ghosh brings more than three decades of professional experience in financial services and the insurance sector, including over 16 years in the general insurance industry. He joined HDFC ERGO in 2016 and has held several leadership roles within the company. Before this appointment, he served as the Executive Director of the company, starting on May 1, 2025.

questions.question: Which Indian port became the first major port in the country to implement a Digital Twin platform for maritime management?
questions.explanation: V.O. Chidambaranar Port became the first major port in India to implement a Digital Twin platform, a cutting-edge digital system that creates a real-time virtual replica of the port’s infrastructure and operations. The platform integrates technologies such as IoT sensors, GPS tracking, LiDAR mapping, drone imaging, and CCTV networks to monitor activities like vessel movement, berth occupancy, and crane utilisation. The system also supports predictive maintenance of cargo-handling equipment and intelligent scheduling to reduce congestion. This initiative at V.O. Chidambaranar Port aims to reduce vessel turnaround time by up to 25% and improve energy efficiency while supporting sustainability goals.

questions.question: In which state was the tribal millet-based food menu ‘Araku Kouni’ launched?
questions.explanation: The tribal millet-based food menu called ‘Araku Kouni’ was launched by Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu in the state secretariat at Guntur. The initiative aims to promote traditional millet-based diets and highlight the rich culinary heritage of tribal communities. The menu features dishes such as Korra Payasam, Ragi Chapati, Samala Pulihora, and Corn Vada, which are sourced from indigenous communities in the Alluri Sitarama Raju district. These ingredients are cultivated naturally without chemical fertilisers or pesticides. The project was developed by the district administration with support from the Indian Culinary Institute in Tirupati and tribal anganwadi workers, and it will be introduced in Haritha restaurants.

questions.question: Which GI-tagged aromatic rice from Assam was exported to the United Kingdom and Italy for the first time?
questions.explanation: Joha Rice is a traditional aromatic rice variety from Assam that received a Geographical Indication (GI) tag in 2017. Known for its distinctive fragrance, fine-grain texture, and rich taste, it is highly valued in regional cuisine. Recently, 25 metric tonnes of Joha Rice were exported for the first time to the United Kingdom and Italy, marking an important milestone for Assam’s agricultural exports. The shipment was facilitated by the Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA) in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, Government of Assam. This development helps promote Joha Rice as a premium product in international markets, particularly in Europe.

questions.question: Which state will host the world’s first autonomous maritime shipbuilding and systems centre near Juvvaladinne fishing harbour?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh will host the world’s first autonomous maritime shipbuilding and systems centre near the Juvvaladinne fishing harbour on India’s eastern coast. The project is being developed by Sagar Defence Engineering with support from the state government. Spread across about 29.58 acres in the first phase, the facility will include autonomous shipbuilding infrastructure, testing facilities, a research and development centre, and a maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) unit. The centre will focus on developing Unmanned Surface Vessels and Autonomous Underwater Vehicles for defence, surveillance, security, and research purposes, while also benefiting the local fishing community through training and technical support.

questions.question: Which AI company launched the ‘Personal Computer’ AI agent designed to automate tasks such as research, drafting emails, and managing files across devices?
questions.explanation: Perplexity launched the ‘Personal Computer’ AI agent, an advanced digital assistant designed to automate everyday productivity tasks. The system operates on a Mac mini and functions as a digital proxy that can research topics, draft emails, prepare daily briefings, and manage files across different applications and devices. It combines local system operations with cloud-based reasoning to handle complex user requests efficiently. The platform also integrates multiple large language models such as Claude, Gemini, and Grok, allowing them to collaborate during task execution. With built-in security safeguards and continuous operation, the AI agent aims to improve productivity by automating routine digital activities.

questions.question: On which date is Pi Day celebrated every year to honour the mathematical constant π?
questions.explanation: Pi Day is celebrated annually on March 14 to recognise the mathematical constant π (pi), which represents the ratio of a circle’s circumference to its diameter. The date 3/14 corresponds to the first three digits of pi, which are 3.14. The day is widely observed by students, mathematicians and science enthusiasts through activities such as math quizzes, puzzles, and pi recitation competitions. Pi Day was first celebrated in 1988 by physicist Larry Shaw at the Exploratorium science museum in San Francisco. Later, in 2009, the U.S. House of Representatives officially recognised the day to encourage interest in mathematics and science education worldwide.

questions.question: Who won the gold medal in the men’s high jump T63 category at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026 held in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shailesh Kumar, an Indian para athlete and reigning world champion, won the gold medal in the men’s high jump T63 category at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026 in New Delhi. He secured the top position with a jump of 1.84 metres, marking a strong start to his competitive season despite recovering from a left knee injury. Although the height was lower than his personal best of 1.91 metres, the performance demonstrated a steady return to competition. His training plan currently focuses on refining his take-off technique and preparing for the Asian Para Games, which is considered one of the major upcoming competitions for para athletes in the region.

questions.question: On which date is CISF Raising Day observed every year to commemorate the establishment of the Central Industrial Security Force in 1969?
questions.explanation: CISF Raising Day is observed every year on March 10 to commemorate the establishment of the Central Industrial Security Force in 1969 under the CISF Act, 1968. The day recognises the dedication, bravery and service of CISF personnel who protect India’s critical infrastructure. Initially formed with just three battalions and about 2,800 personnel, the force has grown significantly over the decades to more than 1,88,000 members. CISF plays a crucial role in safeguarding airports, metro networks, nuclear power plants, seaports and major government establishments. Various events such as ceremonial parades, awards and community programs are organized across the country to honour the contributions of the force.

questions.question: On which date is the International Day to Combat Islamophobia observed every year worldwide?
questions.explanation: The International Day to Combat Islamophobia is observed every year on March 15 to raise awareness about discrimination, prejudice, and hatred faced by Muslims around the world. The day encourages governments, organisations, and communities to promote tolerance, mutual respect, and peaceful coexistence among people of different religions and cultures. The United Nations General Assembly declared this observance in 2022 through Resolution A/RES/76/254. The date was chosen to mark the anniversary of the 2019 Christchurch mosque attacks in New Zealand, where 51 people lost their lives. The observance highlights the importance of combating religious intolerance and promoting human rights, equality, and interfaith dialogue globally.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the Cyber Safety Protocol 2026 to protect students from AI-driven cyber threats in schools?
questions.explanation: Kerala introduced the Cyber Safety Protocol 2026 to ensure a secure digital learning environment for students in the age of artificial intelligence. Developed by Kerala Infrastructure and Technology for Education (KITE) under the state’s General Education Department, the framework addresses emerging cyber risks such as deepfakes, AI-generated misinformation, cyber fraud and digital grooming. The protocol provides guidelines for teachers, students and parents while assigning responsibilities to school authorities to maintain cyber safety standards. It also emphasizes protecting student data through “Privacy by Design” principles and aligns with major Indian legal frameworks such as the Information Technology Act 2000 and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 to encourage safe and responsible digital practices among students.

questions.question: In which city was India’s first microalgae-based PureAir Tower installed to combat vehicular pollution along the Aerocity corridor?
questions.explanation: India’s first microalgae-based PureAir Tower has been installed in New Delhi along the Aerocity highway corridor to address rising vehicular pollution. The innovative biotechnology system uses photosynthetic microalgae to capture pollutants such as carbon dioxide, particulate matter and nitrogen oxides from the surrounding air. These pollutants are then converted into oxygen and algal biomass through natural biological processes. The project was developed through collaboration between C P Arora Private Limited and Carbelim Pvt Ltd, a climate-tech startup incubated at IIT Madras. The tower is designed to provide an air purification effect comparable to more than 15 mature trees, helping improve urban air quality sustainably.

questions.question: Which ministry launched the ‘Say No To Proxy Sarpanch’ campaign to address the ‘Sarpanch Pati’ practice in village governance?
options: Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
questions.explanation: The ‘Say No To Proxy Sarpanch’ campaign was launched to address the issue of proxy leadership in village governance, commonly referred to as the “Sarpanch Pati” practice. In many villages, women are elected as sarpanches due to reservation policies, but their husbands or male relatives often take over decision-making authority. The campaign aims to promote genuine leadership by elected women representatives and encourage communities to respect their authority. It also raises awareness through public participation and social media engagement. Additional measures include advisory recommendations such as penalties for proxy governance, helplines for complaints, and capacity-building programs to strengthen women’s leadership in Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Which bank launched the ‘Safety Centre’ on its mobile banking app to enhance customer control over digital banking security?
questions.explanation: Axis Bank introduced the ‘Safety Centre’ on its mobile banking app to empower customers with real-time control over digital banking functions. The feature allows users to manage internet banking, UPI usage, fund transfer limits, and fraud protection tools directly through the app. Key tools include SMS Shield for verifying genuine messages, options to disable internet banking, and restrictions on fund transfers. By enabling self-managed security, Axis Bank aims to prevent unauthorized transactions, address rising digital fraud cases, and reduce dependency on branches or customer support, thereby promoting safer and more convenient digital banking experiences.

questions.question: In which state was the Sanskaar Shaala, a value-based education programme for children, launched by the Governor?
questions.explanation: The Sanskaar Shaala programme was launched in Assam by Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya to promote value-based education for children aged 4–14. The initiative emphasizes moral education, Indian cultural values, and social responsibility, complementing formal academic learning. By integrating ethics, compassion, and cultural awareness into early education, Assam aims to nurture responsible and value-oriented citizens. The programme balances modern, technology-driven education with traditional moral guidance drawn from ancient Indian texts, highlighting the state’s commitment to preserving cultural heritage while fostering holistic child development.

questions.question: Which Indian private sector bank unveiled its refreshed brand identity called ‘The Fortuna Wave’ in January 2026, emphasizing modernization and digital banking?
questions.explanation: Federal Bank launched its new brand identity, ‘The Fortuna Wave’, on January 7, 2026, in Mumbai, with Vidya Balan as the brand ambassador. The refreshed logo features three waves symbolizing authenticity, togetherness, and prosperity, designed to be digital-friendly and visually appealing across platforms. This brand refresh reflects Federal Bank’s commitment to modernization, innovation, and future-ready digital banking solutions. Established in 1931 in Aluva, Kerala, Federal Bank has evolved into a pan-India private sector bank offering retail, corporate, NRI, lending, and digital banking services, and the rebranding reinforces its contemporary identity and customer-centric vision.

questions.question: Which organization celebrated its 68th Foundation Day in 2026, highlighting achievements in Cyber, Space, and AI, and supporting India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative?
questions.explanation: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) celebrated its 68th Foundation Day on January 1, 2026, marking nearly seven decades of contributions to India’s defence and security infrastructure. DRDO operates 52 laboratories and focuses on key domains such as Cyber, Space, and Artificial Intelligence, aligning with the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative to strengthen India’s self-reliance in defence. In 2025, it secured 22 AoN approvals worth Rs.1.30 lakh crore, signed 11 defence contracts worth Rs.26,000 crore, and completed 245 technology transfer agreements (LAToT). DRDO continues to drive indigenous defence research, industry collaborations, and operational readiness, making it central to India’s strategic and technological advancement.

questions.question: Who has assumed charge as the Chairman of the Council for Leather Exports, bringing over four decades of experience in the leather industry?
questions.explanation: Ramesh Kumar Juneja has taken charge as the Chairman of the Council for Leather Exports (CLE), the apex body promoting Indian leather, footwear, and related products in global markets under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry. With over 40 years of experience, including founding the JC Group and pioneering global collaborations, Juneja brings deep industry knowledge and long-standing institutional involvement with CLE. His leadership is expected to strengthen India’s international leather trade, focus on value addition, innovation, and market diversification, and address challenges such as sustainability standards and global competition, thereby supporting the sector’s growth and export potential.

questions.question: Which bank launched the ‘Zero-Forex Diamond Reserve Credit Card’ offering zero foreign exchange markup along with travel and lifestyle benefits?
questions.explanation: IDFC FIRST Bank introduced the Zero-Forex Diamond Reserve Credit Card in January 2026, targeting frequent international travelers and premium lifestyle users. The card eliminates foreign exchange markup, making overseas spending and online international purchases cost-effective. It also offers extensive benefits, including travel insurance, airport lounge access, meet-and-greet services, and reward points on hotels, flights, and other spending. With a digital-first application process, annual fee waivers on high spends, and a robust rewards ecosystem, this launch strengthens IDFC FIRST Bank’s presence in the premium credit card segment and enhances convenience and value for global and lifestyle-oriented customers.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the new CEO of HSBC Private Bank effective January 5, 2026?
questions.explanation: Ida Liu was appointed as the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of HSBC Private Bank effective January 5, 2026, succeeding Gabriel Castello, who had been serving as Interim CEO. With over 25 years of global experience in private banking and wealth management, including leadership roles at Citi Private Bank, Ida Liu brings expertise in wealth management, strategic advisory, and business transformation. Her appointment underscores HSBC’s strategic focus on strengthening its global private banking operations and managing Ultra-High-Net-Worth (UHNW) client portfolios in an increasingly competitive financial sector.

questions.question: On which date is Earth’s Rotation Day observed, commemorating Léon Foucault’s historic pendulum experiment proving Earth’s rotation?
questions.explanation: Earth’s Rotation Day is observed annually on January 8 to honour the groundbreaking 1851 experiment by French physicist Léon Foucault, who provided the first conclusive proof of Earth’s rotation using a pendulum at the Panthéon in Paris. The Foucault pendulum demonstrated that the Earth rotates on its axis by showing the gradual rotation of the pendulum’s plane of oscillation. This day highlights the importance of Earth’s rotational motion, which governs the day-night cycle and seasonal changes, and celebrates a milestone in humanity’s scientific understanding of planetary dynamics.

questions.question: Which international organization declared 2026 as the International Year for Rangelands and Pastoralists to highlight the importance of grasslands and savannahs in climate action?
questions.explanation: The United Nations (UN) declared 2026 as the International Year for Rangelands and Pastoralists to spotlight ecosystems often overlooked in global climate strategies. Grasslands and savannahs play a critical role in carbon sequestration, biodiversity conservation, and climate resilience, yet have received less attention than forests in UNFCCC negotiations. This UN initiative seeks to address this imbalance by promoting policy integration, scientific recognition, and global awareness of these vital ecosystems. It underscores the UN’s commitment to comprehensive climate action that includes all biomes and supports sustainable pastoralist livelihoods worldwide.

questions.question: In which Indian state was the new reed snake species Calamaria mizoramensis discovered?
questions.explanation: The newly identified reed snake species Calamaria mizoramensis was discovered in Mizoram by a research team from Mizoram University in collaboration with international scientists. The species inhabits humid, forested hill regions between 670 and 1,295 meters elevation and has been confirmed in multiple districts including Aizawl, Reiek, and Mamit. This discovery enhances India’s reptilian biodiversity and raises Mizoram’s herpetofauna count to 169 species, highlighting the state as a hotspot for reptile diversity. The species’ distinct morphology, nocturnal and semi-fossorial behavior, and Least Concern IUCN status make it a significant addition to Indian wildlife records.

questions.question: Which state secured the first position in the ICJS 2.0 national rankings released in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand achieved the top rank in the ICJS 2.0 national rankings with a high score of 93.46, reflecting its strong performance in digital policing and criminal justice system integration. The Inter-Operable Criminal Justice System enables seamless data sharing among police, courts, prisons, and other justice institutions, promoting efficiency and transparency. Uttarakhand’s success is attributed to effective integration of district-level systems with the national platform, extensive training of police personnel, and commitment to technology-driven governance. This achievement sets a benchmark for other states in modernizing law enforcement and strengthening evidence-based justice delivery.

questions.question: Which state is home to India’s first fully paperless district court?
questions.explanation: Kerala has achieved a significant milestone in judicial digitisation with the inauguration of India’s first fully paperless district court. The Kalpetta district court in Wayanad operates entirely in digital mode, ensuring that all judicial processes are conducted electronically. This includes case institution, pre-trial proceedings, evidence recording, interim applications, and final adjudication. Inaugurated virtually by the Chief Justice of India, this initiative reflects the state’s strong emphasis on e-governance and technological integration. The move enhances efficiency, transparency, and accessibility in the justice delivery system while reducing reliance on physical paperwork.

questions.question: How many States signed MoUs with the Ministry of Textiles under the Tex-RAMPS initiative?
questions.explanation: The 15 States that entered into Memorandums of Understanding with the Ministry of Textiles under the Tex-RAMPS scheme. This initiative was highlighted during the National Textiles Ministers’ Conference and aims to strengthen textile-related data systems and promote evidence-based policymaking. A total of 15 States participated by signing MoUs, reflecting a collaborative approach between the Centre and States. This partnership supports improved quality and credibility of textile statistics and aligns with cooperative federalism while contributing to the long-term goal of developing a $350-billion textile industry.

questions.question: How many international organizations did the United States withdraw from under the executive order signed on January 7, 2026?
questions.explanation: The United States announced its withdrawal from a total of 66 international organizations through an executive order signed on January 7, 2026. This decision marked a major escalation in the country’s retreat from multilateral global cooperation, particularly targeting UN-affiliated bodies involved in climate action, labour standards, and social development. The move followed a comprehensive review of U.S. participation and funding, with the administration citing redundancy, inefficiency, and misalignment with national interests as key reasons. The withdrawals have significant implications for global governance, climate response, humanitarian assistance, and the functioning of international institutions worldwide.

questions.question: In which city was the “Viksit Bharat, Viksit Ports” programme held for the launch of ₹235 crore port projects in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Chennai served as the venue for the “Viksit Bharat, Viksit Ports” programme, during which port projects worth ₹235 crore were launched in Tamil Nadu. The initiative was led by the Union Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways and inaugurated by Sarbananda Sonowal. The funding allocation included ₹129.36 crore for the Chennai Port Authority and ₹105.64 crore for Kamarajar Port Limited. The programme also witnessed the launch of the e-port clearance portal under the One Nation–One Port Process, aimed at improving efficiency, transparency, and ease of doing business in India’s port sector.

questions.question: In which city did the National Textiles Ministers’ Conference 2026 commence on January 8?
questions.explanation: Guwahati hosted the National Textiles Ministers’ Conference 2026, which began on January 8 as a two-day event organized by the Ministry of Textiles in collaboration with the Government of Assam. The conference brought together Textile Ministers and senior officials from all States and Union Territories to discuss the future roadmap of India’s textile sector. Held under the theme “India’s Textiles: Weaving Growth, Heritage & Innovation,” the event emphasized cooperative federalism, development of PM MITRA Parks, and the strategic goal of positioning India as a global textile manufacturing hub by 2030, while also highlighting the potential of the North-Eastern region.

questions.question: What is the real GDP growth rate of India for the financial year 2025–26 as per the First Advance Estimates released by NSO?
questions.explanation: The First Advance Estimates released by the National Statistics Office indicate that India’s real GDP growth rate for the financial year 2025–26 is projected at 7.4 percent. Real GDP measures economic growth after adjusting for inflation, reflecting the actual expansion in output. This estimate is significant as it guides policy decisions by the government and the Reserve Bank of India, influences investor confidence, and helps assess economic momentum. The strong growth projection reinforces India’s position as one of the fastest-growing major economies, driven largely by robust performance in the services and manufacturing sectors.

questions.question: Which two countries formally recognised AYUSH in India’s trade pacts finalised in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Oman and New Zealand provided formal recognition to AYUSH through India’s trade pacts finalised in December 2025. These agreements include dedicated annexures focusing on health services and traditional medicine, marking an important step in institutionalising cooperation in the AYUSH sector. The recognition helps improve market access, harmonise standards, and enhance credibility for Indian traditional medicine products abroad. This development has supported the steady growth of AYUSH exports, which increased by over 6 percent between 2023–24 and 2024–25, strengthening India’s position in the global traditional healthcare market.

questions.question: Which institution inaugurated two apex calibration facilities to strengthen India’s national quality and measurement infrastructure?
options: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: National Physical Laboratory plays a crucial role in strengthening India’s quality and measurement ecosystem through the inauguration of two apex calibration facilities. These include the Solar Cell Calibration Facility, which enables highly accurate photovoltaic testing and supports solar manufacturing and exports, and the Environmental Standard Laboratory, which allows domestic calibration of air-quality monitoring instruments. As India’s premier measurement standards institution under CSIR, the laboratory ensures precision, reliability, and global comparability of measurements. Such initiatives reduce dependence on foreign calibration services, improve industrial competitiveness, and enhance environmental monitoring capabilities across the country.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the country’s first dedicated Deeptech Startup Policy for 2025–26?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has taken a pioneering step in strengthening India’s innovation ecosystem by launching the country’s first dedicated Deeptech Startup Policy for 2025–26. The policy aims to convert advanced scientific research into scalable commercial products, with a strong focus on long-gestation, science-driven technologies. Backed by a financial commitment of ₹100 crore to support 100 deeptech startups, it targets sectors such as artificial intelligence, semiconductors, biotechnology, space technologies, and clean energy. Launched at the Umagine Technology Conference in Chennai, the initiative reinforces Tamil Nadu’s ambition to emerge as a global hub for frontier technologies and high-value innovation.

questions.question: Which public sector company awarded major refinery contracts to Technip Energies for projects at Bina and Mumbai refineries?
questions.explanation: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd is the public sector undertaking responsible for awarding major refinery contracts to Technip Energies. These contracts are part of BPCL’s broader petrochemical expansion and refinery modernization strategy. At the Bina refinery in Madhya Pradesh, the EPCC contract covers the establishment of polypropylene and Butene-1 units, enhancing petrochemical output. At the Mumbai refinery, an EPCM contract was awarded for India’s first 3 MMTPA PRFCC unit, which will convert heavy residues into high-value products. These projects strengthen refining efficiency, value addition, and energy infrastructure development.

questions.question: On which date is World Hindi Day observed every year to promote the Hindi language at the global level?
questions.explanation: World Hindi Day is observed annually on January 10 to highlight the growing international presence and importance of the Hindi language. The date holds historical significance as Hindi was first used in the United Nations General Assembly on January 10, 1949, marking its entry into global diplomacy. The observance began officially in 2006 and is celebrated worldwide to promote Hindi in areas such as education, literature, technology, and cultural exchange. By recognizing Hindi as a widely spoken global language, the day supports linguistic diversity and preserves India’s cultural heritage for future generations.

questions.question: Which bank launched the new brand identity named ‘Fortuna Wave’ to position itself as a digital-first, pan-India institution?
questions.explanation: Federal Bank introduced the ‘Fortuna Wave’ brand identity to refresh its image and reinforce its positioning as a digital-first, pan-India banking institution. The launch, held in Mumbai, was aimed at attracting younger and tech-savvy customers while maintaining trust among existing users. Federal Bank emphasized core values such as authenticity, prosperity, and togetherness through this new identity. By creating a unified visual language across both digital platforms and physical branches, Federal Bank seeks to strengthen brand recognition, enhance customer engagement, and align itself with the evolving landscape of modern banking in India.

questions.question: Which Bengaluru-based fintech company received RBI approval to operate as a Payment Aggregator–Cross Border (PA-CB)?
questions.explanation: Skydo received final authorisation from the Reserve Bank of India to operate as a Payment Aggregator–Cross Border, marking a major regulatory milestone in India’s fintech sector. This approval enables Skydo to provide regulated cross-border payment services, particularly benefiting MSMEs, exporters, startups, and freelancers. The licence strengthens trust, transparency, and compliance in international transactions, which involve foreign exchange and regulatory oversight. Skydo supports collections in multiple foreign currencies, offers a flat-fee pricing model without forex markups, and ensures faster settlements. The development also contributes to strengthening India’s digital payments ecosystem and export-oriented financial infrastructure.

questions.question: The ‘Asha Van’ mobile unit launched in Gandhinagar primarily focuses on which area of healthcare?
questions.explanation: The ‘Asha Van’ mobile unit was launched to strengthen early detection and diagnosis of cancers, especially in underserved and rural areas. Equipped with advanced EVA-Pro diagnostic systems, mammography facilities, and teleconsultation services, the unit is capable of screening for ten different types of cancer, including lung, breast, and cervical cancers. By enabling early screening, the initiative aims to improve treatment outcomes and reduce mortality. Handed over to the Indian Red Cross Society, the mobile unit aligns with the broader goal of accessible healthcare and supports the vision of universal health coverage.

questions.question: Which country became the first in the world to commercially produce and use bio-bitumen for road construction?
questions.explanation: India has achieved a global first by commercially producing and using bio-bitumen as an eco-friendly alternative to conventional crude-oil-based bitumen in road construction. This innovation helps reduce environmental pollution by utilizing agricultural crop residue that would otherwise be burnt, contributing to air quality improvement. Announced at a CSIR technology transfer event, the initiative aligns with national priorities such as Viksit Bharat 2047, Waste-to-Wealth, and Atma Nirbhar Bharat. The development highlights India’s growing leadership in sustainable infrastructure, green technologies, and circular economy-driven solutions.

questions.question: In which city was the Third International Conference on Indian Languages inaugurated by the Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: The Third International Conference on Indian Languages was inaugurated in New Delhi by the Vice President of India, highlighting the city’s role as a major centre for national and international academic and cultural dialogue. The conference brought together scholars, language experts, and global delegates to discuss the preservation, promotion, and study of Indian languages. The event emphasized the cultural, constitutional, and democratic importance of linguistic diversity in India, along with the role of education policies and modern technology such as digital archives and AI tools in safeguarding language heritage for future generations.

questions.question: Which state secured the top rank in Inter-Operable Criminal Justice System (ICJS) 2.0, leading India in digital policing and justice system integration?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand achieved the highest ranking in the Inter-Operable Criminal Justice System 2.0 by scoring 93.46, reflecting strong performance in digital policing and integration of the criminal justice system. The NCRB dashboard placed the state ahead of Haryana and Assam, with most ICJS modules completed well before the 2026 target timeline. This accomplishment highlights effective use of technology, coordination among justice institutions, and administrative efficiency. Additionally, the Uttarakhand State Disaster Response Force earned recognition for its exemplary role in rescue operations, safety management, and crowd control during Prayagraj Mahakumbh-2025, further underscoring the state’s institutional competence.

questions.question: Which state, along with Assam, signed a wildlife exchange pact to boost biodiversity and restore locally extinct species?
questions.explanation: The wildlife exchange pact was signed between Assam and Madhya Pradesh with the objective of enhancing biodiversity and reviving species that have disappeared locally. As part of this agreement, Assam will send wild water buffaloes, a pair of one-horned rhinoceroses, and king cobras to Madhya Pradesh. In return, Madhya Pradesh will provide tigers and mugger crocodiles to Assam. This collaborative initiative highlights the importance of inter-state cooperation in wildlife conservation and ecosystem restoration, with Kanha Tiger Reserve playing a key role in reintroducing buffaloes.

questions.question: In which country was the joint military exercise “Gulf Shield 2026” launched by the Gulf Cooperation Council nations?
questions.explanation: The joint military exercise “Gulf Shield 2026” was launched in Saudi Arabia as part of efforts by Gulf Cooperation Council member states to enhance regional security. The exercise focuses on improving collective defence capabilities, operational coordination, and combat readiness among participating forces.
