The Naval Science & Technological Laboratory in Visakhapatnam served as the nodal laboratory, with support from other DRDO units for parachute systems, certification, and instrumentation.

questions.question: Who created history by completing the Full Ironman New Zealand triathlon in 11 hours and 50 minutes?
questions.explanation: Anant Rana, a 33-year-old athlete from Jamshedpur, achieved a remarkable milestone by successfully completing the Full Ironman New Zealand triathlon in 11 hours and 50 minutes. The challenging endurance event took place in Taupo, New Zealand, and includes three demanding segments: a 3.8 km swim, a 180 km cycling race, and a 42.2 km marathon run. Rana completed the swimming segment in 1 hour 28 minutes, the cycling portion in 6 hours 19 minutes, and the marathon in 3 hours 46 minutes. Finishing well within the official 17-hour time limit, he demonstrated exceptional endurance and determination. He is associated with the Tata Steel Adventure Foundation and resides in Sonari, Jamshedpur.

questions.question: Which Indian state has earmarked Rs 1,331 crore to upgrade an Olympic-ready city as part of India’s bid to host the 2036 Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The state government of Gujarat has allocated Rs 1,331 crore to develop Ahmedabad into an Olympic-ready city, supporting India’s ambition to host the 2036 Olympic Games. This funding is part of a broader sports and youth activities budget exceeding Rs 2,000 crore. A significant share of the investment will be directed toward infrastructure development at the SVP Sports Enclave located near the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The initiative also focuses on grassroots sports development through the ‘In-School’ programme, which trains more than 1.29 lakh children across 230 schools. These efforts aim to nurture young athletes and position Gujarat as a major international hub for sporting events and infrastructure.

questions.question: In which city did Bharat Forge inaugurate its state-of-the-art Landing Gear Components Machining Facility in collaboration with Liebherr-Aerospace & Transportation SAS?
questions.explanation: Bharat Forge inaugurated a specialised Landing Gear Components Machining Facility in Mundhwa, a locality in Pune, Maharashtra. The facility has been developed in collaboration with Liebherr-Aerospace & Transportation SAS and represents a significant advancement in India’s aerospace manufacturing capabilities. It is among the first facilities in the country to operate OEM-approved landing gear machining at scale. Equipped with high-precision machining centres, the plant will manufacture critical aerospace components. This development strengthens India’s position in the global aerospace supply chain. With this facility, Bharat Forge expands its aerospace portfolio, which already includes aero-engine components, airframe structures, and landing-gear sub-systems, with future plans for an advanced aerospace ring-mill.

questions.question: Which bank launched the “Bob Women Sapphire” savings account offering lifestyle, health, and wellness benefits for women?
questions.explanation: Bank of Baroda introduced the “bob Women Sapphire” savings account to provide exclusive financial and lifestyle benefits for women customers. The account requires maintaining a Monthly Average Balance of ₹1 lakh and includes several health and wellness features. These benefits include a ₹10 lakh cancer care coverage plan, complimentary online doctor consultations for up to six family members, and discounts on medicines and pathology services. Customers also receive the Bob Bhoomi RuPay Select Debit Card, which provides privileges such as airport lounge access, gym memberships, spa or salon services, and OTT subscriptions, along with additional concessions on loans and locker charges.

questions.question: What is the name of India’s first drone capable of operating both in the air and underwater using advanced sonar technology?
questions.explanation: AVATAAR amphibious drone is India’s first drone designed to operate both in the air and underwater. It was developed by AquaAirX Autonomous Systems and features a carbon-fibre structure that provides high resistance to corrosion in marine environments. The drone uses advanced sonar systems, including multibeam imaging sonar, to map the seabed and detect underwater objects. It also includes an acoustic communication modem and an Ultra-Short Baseline navigation system for precise positioning during missions. The name AVATAAR stands for Amphibious Vehicle for Autonomous Tactical Aerial & Aquatic Reconnaissance and is intended for surveillance, reconnaissance, and security operations around ports, harbors, and coastal regions.

questions.question: Which Indian state has launched the ‘Lake and Air Watch’ initiative for real-time monitoring of urban air quality and water bodies?
questions.explanation: Gujarat launched the ‘Lake and Air Watch’ initiative through the Gujarat Urban Development Mission to monitor environmental conditions in urban areas. The program uses satellite technology and advanced sensors to track air quality and the condition of city lakes, including pollution levels, siltation, and algae growth. Real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) data will be gathered from monitoring stations across 17 municipal corporations and 152 municipalities. A centralized digital dashboard will continuously update environmental data and generate alerts if pollution levels or lake conditions change significantly, helping authorities respond quickly and improving environmental transparency for citizens.

questions.question: Which company signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) to support industry–start-up collaboration in cooling and smart appliance technologies?
questions.explanation: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Voltas Limited to promote collaboration between industry and start-ups working in cooling and smart appliance technologies. The initiative aims to support innovation in areas such as HVAC systems, artificial intelligence, machine learning, power electronics, and advanced control technologies. Through programs like the Startup India initiative and the Bharat Start-up Grand Challenge, selected start-ups will receive mentorship, technical support, and access to testing infrastructure. The partnership also encourages the development of energy-efficient cooling solutions, IoT-enabled appliances, air-quality monitoring systems, and predictive maintenance technologies.

questions.question: Who won the 2026 Formula 1 season-opening Australian Grand Prix for Mercedes at Albert Park?
questions.explanation: George Russell won the 2026 season-opening Australian Grand Prix, delivering a strong performance for the Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One Team. Russell started the race from pole position and secured victory with a comfortable lead of about 2.9 seconds over his teammate, achieving his sixth career Formula 1 win. During the race, he briefly lost the lead to Charles Leclerc but regained it after a Ferrari strategy error during a Virtual Safety Car period. The win highlighted Mercedes’ impressive early pace in the 2026 season, making them strong contenders for the championship. Meanwhile, Lewis Hamilton finished fourth in his debut race for Ferrari.

questions.question: Which two countries were added to the FATF “grey list” for increased monitoring due to deficiencies in anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing frameworks in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF), an international body responsible for setting standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing, placed Kuwait and Papua New Guinea on its “grey list” for increased monitoring after its February 2026 plenary meeting. Countries on this list have strategic deficiencies in their financial monitoring systems and must implement specific action plans within a given timeframe to address these gaps. While the grey list expanded, the FATF “black list” remained unchanged and includes Iran, North Korea, and Myanmar. The monitoring aims to safeguard the global financial system while ensuring humanitarian financial flows are not disrupted.

questions.question: In which state has the Union Agriculture Minister approved the procurement of 1,25,855 metric tonnes of Rabi crops at MSP worth over ₹894 crore under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) for the 2026 season?
questions.explanation: The Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan approved the procurement of 1,25,855 metric tonnes of Rabi crops at Minimum Support Price (MSP) under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) in Telangana. The total procurement value is over ₹894 crore and aims to benefit thousands of farmers in the state. This initiative ensures farmers receive fair prices for their produce and are protected from market price fluctuations. By preventing distress sales and encouraging the production of pulses and oilseeds, the program supports agricultural growth, improves nutritional security, and contributes to strengthening the rural economy under the leadership of Narendra Modi.

questions.question: Which medical institution signed a Memorandum of Understanding with ISRO to collaborate on space medicine and research for future human space missions?
questions.explanation: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) signed a Framework Memorandum of Understanding with All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi, to advance research in space medicine for upcoming human space missions. The agreement was signed by Dinesh Kumar Singh, Director of the Human Space Flight Centre, and Srinivas M, Director of AIIMS, in the presence of ISRO Chairman V. Narayanan. The collaboration will focus on studying human health challenges in microgravity, including musculoskeletal atrophy, neuroscience, nutrition, immunity, and metabolic health. This partnership supports India’s long-term goals, such as developing the Bharatiya Antariksh Station and preparing for future crewed missions to the Moon.

questions.question: Which NASA spacecraft re-entered Earth’s atmosphere on March 11 after completing a long mission studying the radiation belts around Earth?
questions.explanation: Van Allen Probe A, part of NASA’s Van Allen Probes mission, re-entered Earth’s atmosphere on March 11 after nearly 14 years since its launch in 2012. The mission was conducted by NASA and managed by the Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory. The spacecraft studied the Van Allen radiation belts, which are regions of charged particles trapped by Earth’s magnetic field. One of the mission’s major discoveries was the identification of a temporary third radiation belt formed during intense solar activity. The data collected helps scientists understand space weather and its effects on satellites, astronauts, communication systems, and power grids on Earth.

questions.question: Which Indian company has partnered with the United States in a $300 billion project to build the “America First Refining” oil refinery at the Port of Brownsville, Texas?
questions.explanation: The United States announced a historic $300 billion partnership with Reliance Industries Ltd to build a major oil refinery called “America First Refining” at the Port of Brownsville in Texas, United States. The project was announced by Donald Trump and represents the first major refinery construction in the U.S. in about 50 years. It is expected to be one of the cleanest refineries in the world and the largest investment in U.S. history. The initiative aims to strengthen energy security, create thousands of jobs, and stabilize global oil markets amid disruptions in supply routes such as the Strait of Hormuz.

questions.question: Which Urban Local Body issued Maharashtra's first public Green Municipal Bonds listed on the National Stock Exchange?
questions.explanation: Nashik Municipal Corporation became the first Urban Local Body in Maharashtra to issue public Green Municipal Bonds and list them on the National Stock Exchange of India. These bonds are fixed-income debt instruments used by local governments to raise funds specifically for environmentally sustainable projects and climate-friendly initiatives. The listing ceremony was marked by Ashish Chauhan, Managing Director of NSE, who presented a replica of the NSE bell to Devendra Fadnavis. The funds generated from these bonds will support green infrastructure, sustainable urban development, and environmentally beneficial public projects in the city.

questions.question: Which digital payments company partnered with GoKwik to launch India’s first Integrated Conversion-to-Completion stack for D2C brands?
questions.explanation: PayU partnered with GoKwik to introduce India’s first Integrated Conversion-to-Completion stack aimed at improving the online shopping experience for Direct-to-Consumer (D2C) brands. The collaboration integrates GoKwik’s conversion intelligence with PayU’s advanced payment infrastructure to reduce revenue loss caused by checkout drop-offs and payment failures. This innovative solution helps merchants create a smoother and more reliable purchase journey for customers. By addressing common issues such as failed payments and abandoned carts, the integrated system supports businesses in scaling efficiently and maximizing revenue while ensuring a seamless transaction process for online shoppers.

questions.question: Which state’s cabinet approved the draft Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, aimed at curbing forced religious conversions?
questions.explanation: The Chhattisgarh Cabinet approved the draft of the Chhattisgarh Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, under the leadership of Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai. The bill aims to prevent religious conversions carried out through force, fraud, misrepresentation, allurement, or undue influence. It is designed to be stronger and more comprehensive than the existing Chhattisgarh Freedom of Religion Act of 1968. The law also focuses on addressing activities such as “Changai Sabha,” which are alleged to mislead people. The decision comes amid tensions in tribal areas like Bastar related to religious conversions. Along with this bill, the cabinet also approved measures related to recruitment reforms and abolishing the certain property registration cess.

questions.question: Which state will benefit from the MoU signed between Oil India Limited and NSTFDC to strengthen healthcare infrastructure in tribal areas?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh will benefit from the Memorandum of Understanding signed between Oil India Limited and the National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation to improve healthcare infrastructure in tribal and underserved regions. Under this initiative, ₹86.27 lakh has been allocated to upgrade facilities at Community Health Centres and Primary Health Centres in the Rajgarh district, which is recognised as an Aspirational District. The project focuses on enhancing healthcare access for tribal communities by providing essential medical equipment such as ventilators, CPAP machines, BiPAP machines, USG machines, ambulances, and other diagnostic tools to strengthen the public healthcare system in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Where was the International Rail Coach Expo (IRCE) 2026 inaugurated on March 12, 2026?
questions.explanation: ICF Chennai served as the venue for the International Rail Coach Expo (IRCE) 2026, which was inaugurated on March 12, 2026. The event was opened by U. Subba Rao, General Manager of the Integral Coach Factory, and organised in association with the Confederation of Indian Industry. The expo brought together railway manufacturers, technology providers, and industry experts from India and other countries. It showcased innovations in railway coach manufacturing, sustainability initiatives, advanced safety systems, and modern rail technologies. ICF Chennai plays a crucial role in India’s railway modernisation and is globally known for manufacturing passenger coaches and producing the Vande Bharat Express train sets.

questions.question: Honeywell partnered with which institution to establish the Centre of Excellence for Future Skills & Innovation in India?
questions.explanation: Honeywell partnered with the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay to establish the IITB-Honeywell Centre of Excellence for Future Skills & Innovation at the institute’s Powai campus. The centre is fully funded by the Honeywell Hometown Solutions India Foundation and focuses on advancing sustainability skills and innovation among students. The initiative aims to train more than 100,000 students by 2030 through specialised programs, research support, and advanced laboratory infrastructure. It will benefit undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral students from across India. The centre will also offer certificate-based programs, hands-on projects, and research grants to address real-world sustainability challenges.

questions.question: Which company unveiled an AI platform called Agent Studio to automate payment operations for businesses?
questions.explanation: Razorpay introduced an artificial intelligence platform known as Agent Studio to transform how businesses manage payment infrastructure. The platform is built on Anthropic’s Claude Agent SDK and enables companies to automate several payment-related operations using AI agents. These agents can intervene when customers abandon purchases, initiate conversations to encourage payment completion, and integrate with platforms like Shopify, Shiprocket, and WhatsApp to access customer and transaction data. The system also supports a no-code tool that allows businesses to create custom AI agents by defining tasks in plain language. Additionally, Razorpay launched an Agentic Experience Platform that allows payment operations to be handled using natural-language commands.

questions.question: Which country will host the second leg of the Asia Cup Archery in 2027 after a gap of 22 years?
questions.explanation: India has been selected to host the second leg of the Asia Cup Archery tournament in 2027, which will take place in Delhi. This marks an important moment for the country’s archery community because it will be the first major international archery event hosted by India in 22 years. The last major international archery competition organised in the country was the Asian Archery Championships held in Delhi in 2005. The decision was confirmed by World Archery Asia after discussions with the Archery Association of India. Hosting this event will provide Indian archers with a home advantage for ranking points and will also help promote and develop local archery talent.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer (MD & CEO) of HDFC ERGO General Insurance effective from April 16, 2026?
questions.explanation: Parthanil Ghosh has been appointed as the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of HDFC ERGO General Insurance, with the appointment becoming effective from April 16, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. He will succeed Anuj Tyagi, who stepped down from the role to pursue entrepreneurial opportunities. Ghosh brings more than three decades of professional experience in financial services and the insurance sector, including over 16 years in the general insurance industry. He joined HDFC ERGO in 2016 and has held several leadership roles within the company. Before this appointment, he served as the Executive Director of the company, starting on May 1, 2025.

questions.question: Which Indian port became the first major port in the country to implement a Digital Twin platform for maritime management?
questions.explanation: V.O. Chidambaranar Port became the first major port in India to implement a Digital Twin platform, a cutting-edge digital system that creates a real-time virtual replica of the port’s infrastructure and operations. The platform integrates technologies such as IoT sensors, GPS tracking, LiDAR mapping, drone imaging, and CCTV networks to monitor activities like vessel movement, berth occupancy, and crane utilisation. The system also supports predictive maintenance of cargo-handling equipment and intelligent scheduling to reduce congestion. This initiative at V.O. Chidambaranar Port aims to reduce vessel turnaround time by up to 25% and improve energy efficiency while supporting sustainability goals.

questions.question: In which state was the tribal millet-based food menu ‘Araku Kouni’ launched?
questions.explanation: The tribal millet-based food menu called ‘Araku Kouni’ was launched by Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu in the state secretariat at Guntur. The initiative aims to promote traditional millet-based diets and highlight the rich culinary heritage of tribal communities. The menu features dishes such as Korra Payasam, Ragi Chapati, Samala Pulihora, and Corn Vada, which are sourced from indigenous communities in the Alluri Sitarama Raju district. These ingredients are cultivated naturally without chemical fertilisers or pesticides. The project was developed by the district administration with support from the Indian Culinary Institute in Tirupati and tribal anganwadi workers, and it will be introduced in Haritha restaurants.

questions.question: Which GI-tagged aromatic rice from Assam was exported to the United Kingdom and Italy for the first time?
questions.explanation: Joha Rice is a traditional aromatic rice variety from Assam that received a Geographical Indication (GI) tag in 2017. Known for its distinctive fragrance, fine-grain texture, and rich taste, it is highly valued in regional cuisine. Recently, 25 metric tonnes of Joha Rice were exported for the first time to the United Kingdom and Italy, marking an important milestone for Assam’s agricultural exports. The shipment was facilitated by the Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA) in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, Government of Assam. This development helps promote Joha Rice as a premium product in international markets, particularly in Europe.

questions.question: Which state will host the world’s first autonomous maritime shipbuilding and systems centre near Juvvaladinne fishing harbour?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh will host the world’s first autonomous maritime shipbuilding and systems centre near the Juvvaladinne fishing harbour on India’s eastern coast. The project is being developed by Sagar Defence Engineering with support from the state government. Spread across about 29.58 acres in the first phase, the facility will include autonomous shipbuilding infrastructure, testing facilities, a research and development centre, and a maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) unit. The centre will focus on developing Unmanned Surface Vessels and Autonomous Underwater Vehicles for defence, surveillance, security, and research purposes, while also benefiting the local fishing community through training and technical support.

questions.question: Which AI company launched the ‘Personal Computer’ AI agent designed to automate tasks such as research, drafting emails, and managing files across devices?
questions.explanation: Perplexity launched the ‘Personal Computer’ AI agent, an advanced digital assistant designed to automate everyday productivity tasks. The system operates on a Mac mini and functions as a digital proxy that can research topics, draft emails, prepare daily briefings, and manage files across different applications and devices. It combines local system operations with cloud-based reasoning to handle complex user requests efficiently. The platform also integrates multiple large language models such as Claude, Gemini, and Grok, allowing them to collaborate during task execution. With built-in security safeguards and continuous operation, the AI agent aims to improve productivity by automating routine digital activities.

questions.question: On which date is Pi Day celebrated every year to honour the mathematical constant π?
questions.explanation: Pi Day is celebrated annually on March 14 to recognise the mathematical constant π (pi), which represents the ratio of a circle’s circumference to its diameter. The date 3/14 corresponds to the first three digits of pi, which are 3.14. The day is widely observed by students, mathematicians and science enthusiasts through activities such as math quizzes, puzzles, and pi recitation competitions. Pi Day was first celebrated in 1988 by physicist Larry Shaw at the Exploratorium science museum in San Francisco. Later, in 2009, the U.S. House of Representatives officially recognised the day to encourage interest in mathematics and science education worldwide.

questions.question: Who won the gold medal in the men’s high jump T63 category at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026 held in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shailesh Kumar, an Indian para athlete and reigning world champion, won the gold medal in the men’s high jump T63 category at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026 in New Delhi. He secured the top position with a jump of 1.84 metres, marking a strong start to his competitive season despite recovering from a left knee injury. Although the height was lower than his personal best of 1.91 metres, the performance demonstrated a steady return to competition. His training plan currently focuses on refining his take-off technique and preparing for the Asian Para Games, which is considered one of the major upcoming competitions for para athletes in the region.

questions.question: On which date is CISF Raising Day observed every year to commemorate the establishment of the Central Industrial Security Force in 1969?
questions.explanation: CISF Raising Day is observed every year on March 10 to commemorate the establishment of the Central Industrial Security Force in 1969 under the CISF Act, 1968. The day recognises the dedication, bravery and service of CISF personnel who protect India’s critical infrastructure. Initially formed with just three battalions and about 2,800 personnel, the force has grown significantly over the decades to more than 1,88,000 members. CISF plays a crucial role in safeguarding airports, metro networks, nuclear power plants, seaports and major government establishments. Various events such as ceremonial parades, awards and community programs are organized across the country to honour the contributions of the force.

questions.question: On which date is the International Day to Combat Islamophobia observed every year worldwide?
questions.explanation: The International Day to Combat Islamophobia is observed every year on March 15 to raise awareness about discrimination, prejudice, and hatred faced by Muslims around the world. The day encourages governments, organisations, and communities to promote tolerance, mutual respect, and peaceful coexistence among people of different religions and cultures. The United Nations General Assembly declared this observance in 2022 through Resolution A/RES/76/254. The date was chosen to mark the anniversary of the 2019 Christchurch mosque attacks in New Zealand, where 51 people lost their lives. The observance highlights the importance of combating religious intolerance and promoting human rights, equality, and interfaith dialogue globally.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched the Cyber Safety Protocol 2026 to protect students from AI-driven cyber threats in schools?
questions.explanation: Kerala introduced the Cyber Safety Protocol 2026 to ensure a secure digital learning environment for students in the age of artificial intelligence. Developed by Kerala Infrastructure and Technology for Education (KITE) under the state’s General Education Department, the framework addresses emerging cyber risks such as deepfakes, AI-generated misinformation, cyber fraud and digital grooming. The protocol provides guidelines for teachers, students and parents while assigning responsibilities to school authorities to maintain cyber safety standards. It also emphasizes protecting student data through “Privacy by Design” principles and aligns with major Indian legal frameworks such as the Information Technology Act 2000 and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 to encourage safe and responsible digital practices among students.

questions.question: In which city was India’s first microalgae-based PureAir Tower installed to combat vehicular pollution along the Aerocity corridor?
questions.explanation: India’s first microalgae-based PureAir Tower has been installed in New Delhi along the Aerocity highway corridor to address rising vehicular pollution. The innovative biotechnology system uses photosynthetic microalgae to capture pollutants such as carbon dioxide, particulate matter and nitrogen oxides from the surrounding air. These pollutants are then converted into oxygen and algal biomass through natural biological processes. The project was developed through collaboration between C P Arora Private Limited and Carbelim Pvt Ltd, a climate-tech startup incubated at IIT Madras. The tower is designed to provide an air purification effect comparable to more than 15 mature trees, helping improve urban air quality sustainably.

questions.question: Which ministry launched the ‘Say No To Proxy Sarpanch’ campaign to address the ‘Sarpanch Pati’ practice in village governance?
options: Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
questions.explanation: The ‘Say No To Proxy Sarpanch’ campaign was launched to address the issue of proxy leadership in village governance, commonly referred to as the “Sarpanch Pati” practice. In many villages, women are elected as sarpanches due to reservation policies, but their husbands or male relatives often take over decision-making authority. The campaign aims to promote genuine leadership by elected women representatives and encourage communities to respect their authority. It also raises awareness through public participation and social media engagement. Additional measures include advisory recommendations such as penalties for proxy governance, helplines for complaints, and capacity-building programs to strengthen women’s leadership in Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: In which state was the Common Incubation Centre at Tezpur University inaugurated on 13th March 2026?
questions.explanation: The Common Incubation Centre, inaugurated by Union Minister Shri Chirag Paswan, is located at Tezpur University, which is situated in Assam. The centre is part of the PMFME scheme and aims to promote entrepreneurship in the food processing sector by supporting startups, FPOs, SHGs, and cooperatives. Assam, being a key state in the Northeast region, plays an important role in the development of the food processing ecosystem. The initiative also highlights collaboration between government, academia, and entrepreneurs to boost local economic growth and innovation.

questions.question: Which organisation is planning to launch the Artemis II lunar mission on April 1, 2026?
questions.explanation: NASA is preparing to launch the Artemis II mission, marking a significant milestone as the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years. The mission will carry four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the far side of the Moon without landing. It will be launched aboard the Space Launch System from the Kennedy Space Centre. The mission has faced delays due to technical issues such as helium leaks and fuel system concerns, but repairs have been completed. Artemis II is a crucial step in NASA’s broader lunar exploration program, paving the way for future missions like Artemis III and IV.

questions.question: Who was posthumously awarded the Padma Shri 2026 for preserving the Kurumba painting tradition?
questions.explanation: Krishnan Raghavan, popularly known as Kitna, was posthumously honoured with the Padma Shri 2026 for his significant contribution to preserving and promoting the Kurumba painting tradition. Belonging to the Kurumba tribal community of Kerala, he dedicated his life to showcasing indigenous art forms that reflect tribal life, mythology, and nature. His efforts brought national recognition to this unique art style, which is known for its use of natural pigments and symbolic motifs. The award acknowledges his role in safeguarding cultural heritage and promoting awareness of tribal artistic traditions across the country.

questions.question: What is the revised FASTag Annual Pass fee for the financial year 2026–27?
questions.explanation: The National Highways Authority of India has revised the FASTag Annual Pass fee for the financial year 2026–27, increasing it from Rs 3,000 to Rs 3,075. This revised fee applies to non-commercial vehicles using FASTag across around 1,150 toll plazas on national highways and expressways. The annual pass offers significant convenience by allowing either one year of validity or up to 200 toll crossings, reducing the need for frequent recharges. Users can easily activate the pass through digital platforms like the Rajmarg Yatra App or the official website, making it a cost-effective and user-friendly solution for regular highway commuters.

questions.question: On which date is National Vaccination Day observed annually in India?
questions.explanation: National Vaccination Day is observed every year on March 16 in India to raise awareness about the importance of immunisation and public health. The date commemorates the launch of the Pulse Polio Programme in 1995, when the first dose of oral polio vaccine was administered nationwide. This initiative played a crucial role in eradicating polio from India, which was later declared polio-free by the World Health Organisation in 2014. The day also recognizes the efforts of healthcare workers, scientists, and policymakers in implementing large-scale vaccination drives across the country.

questions.question: In which city were advanced healthcare projects, including Proton Cancer Therapy, inaugurated by the Union Home Minister?
questions.explanation: The Union Home Minister inaugurated several major healthcare projects in Guwahati, marking a significant milestone in Assam’s medical infrastructure development. The city became notable for hosting India’s first government hospital offering Proton Cancer Therapy, an advanced treatment for cancer. Alongside this, multiple cancer centres and medical colleges were also launched or expanded, contributing to improved healthcare accessibility in the Northeast region. The initiatives are supported by increased budget allocation and aim to reduce dependency on metro cities for advanced treatments, making Guwahati a key healthcare hub in eastern India.

questions.question: At which location did ISRO conduct the sea level hot test of the CE20 Cryogenic Engine on March 10, 2026?
questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organisation carried out the sea level hot test of the CE20 Cryogenic Engine at its Propulsion Complex located in Mahendragiri, Tamil Nadu. This test was conducted at an enhanced thrust level of 22 tonnes, which is crucial for improving the payload capacity of future LVM3 missions. The successful 165-second test demonstrated the engine’s ability to withstand extreme thermal and mechanical conditions. Advanced systems such as a nozzle protection system and multi-element igniter were used, showcasing India’s growing technological capabilities in cryogenic propulsion systems and space exploration.

questions.question: Which Indian Naval Sail Training Ship reached Malta during the global expedition Lokayan-26?
questions.explanation: INS Sudarshini is an Indian Naval Sail Training Ship that arrived at Valletta, Malta, as part of its extensive Lokayan-26 expedition covering 22,000 nautical miles. The voyage highlights India’s maritime diplomacy and aims to strengthen bilateral relations through engagements with foreign nations. During its visit, the crew interacts with local authorities and armed forces to promote cooperation and goodwill. The expedition began in Kochi and includes multiple international stops, showcasing India’s naval capabilities and cultural outreach. The ship’s participation in global maritime events further enhances India’s presence on the international stage.

questions.question: Who was awarded the 2025 Jnanpith Award for contributions to Tamil literature and poetry?
questions.explanation: Vairamuthu, a renowned Tamil poet and lyricist, received the prestigious Jnanpith Award for 2025, recognizing his exceptional contribution to Tamil literature and poetry. Over several decades, he has produced a vast body of work, including poems, novels, essays, and film lyrics that have deeply influenced both literary and popular culture. His writings often explore themes such as human emotions, social justice, rural life, and nature, blending classical Tamil traditions with modern perspectives. In addition to literature, his impactful lyrics in Tamil cinema have reached a wide audience, making his work widely appreciated across generations.

questions.question: Which company received regulatory approval in China for the anaemia drug Desidustat?
questions.explanation: Zydus Lifesciences secured regulatory approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration for its innovative drug Desidustat, used to treat renal anaemia in chronic kidney disease patients. The drug functions as a HIF-PHI inhibitor, stimulating red blood cell production and improving iron metabolism. It has already shown strong clinical efficacy in Phase III trials conducted in China. The approval is significant due to China’s large CKD patient population. Additionally, the drug has been successfully marketed in India under the brand Oxemia, benefiting over 100,000 patients since its launch in 2022, highlighting its therapeutic effectiveness and commercial potential.

questions.question: How many total medals did India win at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026?
questions.explanation: India delivered an outstanding performance at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026 held in New Delhi, finishing at the top of the medal tally with a record 208 medals. This impressive haul included 75 gold, 69 silver, and 64 bronze medals across various track and field events. The competition saw participation from athletes of multiple countries, but India’s dominance was evident through several podium sweeps and remarkable individual performances. The achievement reflects the country’s growing strength in para-athletics and the success of its efforts to support and develop athletes with disabilities at an international level.
